clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: Age ≥ 60 years. Sex allowed: Both sexes. Cancer type allowed: Acute lymphoblastic leukemia. Histology allowed: B‑cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia. Cancer burden allowed: Bone marrow involvement with ≥20 % lymphoblasts. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Venetoclax. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: Philadelphia chromosome positive or Burkitt leukemia/lymphoma phenotype.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Patient is pregnant or nursing.
Uncontrolled infection.
Active hepatitis B or C infection or confirmed HIV infection.
Major surgery or radiation therapy performed within 4 weeks preceding first study dose.
Symptomatic or untreated leptomeningeal disease or spinal cord compression.
New York Heart Association class III‑IV heart disease, unstable angina, myocardial infarction, or cerebrovascular accident occurring within the last 6 months.
Cardiac ejection fraction less than 40 %.
History of another primary invasive malignancy not in complete remission for at least 2 years (excluding non‑melanoma skin cancers or carcinoma in situ).
Concomitant use of warfarin.
Consumption of grapefruit, grapefruit juice, Seville oranges, or star fruit within 3 days prior to initiating venetoclax.
Use of strong cytochrome P450 3A inducers (e.g., rifampin, rifabutin, phenytoin, carbamazepine, St. John’s wort) within 3 days prior to starting venetoclax.
Severe malabsorption syndrome or any condition prohibiting oral drug administration.
Any additional severe acute or chronic medical or psychiatric condition judged by the investigator to increase risk or confound assessment of study outcomes.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma. Histology allowed: diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma not otherwise specified or high‑grade B‑cell lymphoma with MYC and BCL2 and/or BCL6 rearrangements or high‑grade B‑cell lymphoma not otherwise specified or primary mediastinal (thymic) large B‑cell lymphoma or transformed lymphoma (e.g., transformed follicular lymphoma grade 3, marginal zone lymphoma). Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory disease after failing at least two prior lines of systemic chemotherapy that each contained an anti‑CD20 monoclonal antibody and an anthracycline, and either progressive disease after autologous stem cell transplant or ineligibility/unwillingness to undergo transplant. Prior treatment required: minimum two prior systemic regimens incorporating anti‑CD20 antibody and anthracycline. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Unable to give informed consent.
Known history of infection with HIV or active hepatitis B surface antigen-positive infection; untreated hepatitis C infection.
Pharmacologically uncontrolled seizures.
History or presence of autoimmune central nervous system disease (multiple sclerosis, optic neuritis, etc.).
Central nervous system disorders judged to impede assessment of neurotoxicity (midline shift on MRI, elevated CSF opening pressure or CSF protein >150 mg/dL).
Recent whole-brain radiotherapy within three months.
Active systemic fungal, viral, or bacterial infection.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Previous or concurrent malignant neoplasm outside approved exceptions (except adequately treated basal/squamous skin cancer, in‑situ cervical or breast carcinoma, hormone‑treated breast or prostate cancer in remission ≥2 years, or any solid tumour fully resected/remission ≥2 years).
Severe immunocompromise owing to non‑neurological autoimmune disease treatment.
Use of systemic glucocorticoids exceeding prednisone 10 mg daily chronically (≤2 mg dexamethasone permissible for CNS cohort temporarily around aphaeresis).
Cardiovascular instability: myocardial infarction, coronary intervention, unstable angina, or serious cardiac disease within six months.
Concurrent therapeutic radiotherapy (palliative normal-tissue-sparing radiotherapy allowable up to day of lymphodepletion).
Baseline dementia impeding participation as measured by ICE assessment.
History of severe immediate hypersensitivity to any investigational agent components.
Refusal to comply with long-term lentiviral gene‑therapy follow‑up.
Prior chimeric antigen receptor T‑cell therapy for any indication.
Prior allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation.
Prior administration of bi‑specific T‑cell engager antibodies.
Prior adoptive transfer of T‑cell receptors engineered T cells.

general_exclusion_criteria: active or progressively symptomatic brain metastases or leptomeningeal disease; unresolved toxicities from prior systemic anticancer therapy greater than grade 1 (excluding alopecia and vitiligo); clinically significant lung disease requiring systemic corticosteroids within six months (e.g., interstitial pneumonia, pneumonitis, pulmonary fibrosis, severe radiation pneumonitis); acute or chronic pancreatitis and/or cirrhosis except Child‑Pugh Class A; hepatic insufficiency manifested by clinical jaundice, hepatic encephalopathy, or variceal bleeding within sixty days; history of liver transplantation; prior allogeneic bone marrow transplantation; significant cardiac disease—including recent (<six months) myocardial infarction, acute coronary syndrome, New York Heart Association class III‑IV heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension, uncontrolled arrhythmias, or severe aortic stenosis; venous or arterial thromboembolic events, including stroke, transient ischemic attack, deep‑vein thrombosis, or pulmonary embolism within three months; active acute bacterial, fungal, or viral infection including hepatitis B, hepatitis C, or human immunodeficiency virus (unless controlled per investigator discretion); known or suspected hypersensitivity to the study drug or its excipients; concurrent participation in another investigational clinical trial; current pregnancy or lactation; prior invasive malignancy (other than the index cancer) within three years before registration (exception made for appropriately treated basal cell carcinoma, squamous cell skin carcinoma, non‑muscle‑invasive bladder cancer, in‑situ cervix, breast, or prostate cancer with no evidence of disease for at least two years).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >18 years. Sex allowed: Female and male. Cancer type allowed: Invasive breast cancer. Histology allowed: Hormone receptor positive (estrogen receptor positive and/or progesterone receptor positive) and human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic disease or locally recurrent unresectable disease. Prior treatment required: Documented radiographic or objective disease progression on a cyclin‑dependent kinase 4/6 inhibitor‐containing regimen (either in the metastatic setting or within twelve months after completion of a CDK4/6 inhibitor‑containing adjuvant regimen) AND documented progression on fulvestrant (alone or as part of any regimen) in the metastatic setting; no more than two prior chemotherapy regimens in the metastatic setting; no limitation on number of prior endocrine therapies. Prior treatment excluded: Previous exposure to any MEK inhibitor; prior discontinuation of abemaciclib owing to toxicity; prior discontinuation of fulvestrant owing to toxicity. Biomarkers required: Estrogen receptor positive and/or progesterone receptor positive and HER2 negative as determined by ASCO/CAP‑approved assays (assessment required during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Active brain metastases or leptomeningeal disease (stable treated brain metastases permitted).
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness including uncontrolled hypertension, uncontrolled diabetes, severe cardiopulmonary disease, New York Heart Association class III‑IV heart failure, recent myocardial infarction (<6 months), unstable angina, clinically significant arrhythmias, severe chronic liver or kidney disease, interstitial lung disease, severe dyspnea at rest or requiring supplemental oxygen.
History of a second malignant neoplasm unless disease free ≥5 years (except adequately treated cervix carcinoma in situ or non‑melanoma skin cancer).
Known hypersensitivity to components of VS‑6766, abemaciclib, or fulvestrant.
Positive serology for Human Immunodeficiency Virus with an AIDS‑defining illness within the past year or receiving potent CYP3A4‑modifying antiretrovirals.
Active hepatitis B or C infection (unless appropriately suppressed/treatment completed as specified).
Pregnancy or breastfeeding; inadequate contraception compliance.
Inability to swallow oral medication or conditions causing markedly reduced intestinal absorption (gastrectomy, extensive small‑bowel resections, active inflammatory bowel disease, baseline diarrhoea ≥ grade 2).
Ophthalmologic diseases placing the eye at risk for vascular events (glaucoma, retinal venous occlusions, uncontrolled hypertension or diabetes affecting retina, corneal epithelial instability, keratopathy, uveitis, etc.).
History of rhabdomyolysis or neuromuscular disorders associated with chronically elevated creatine kinase.
Recent systemic antibacterial, antifungal, or antivirally directed therapy for active infection (within 28 days of study start) or unresolved SARS‑CoV‑2 infection.
Concurrent use of warfarin (must switch to LMWH or DOAC before enrolment)."

general_exclusion_criteria:
Patients receiving any other investigational agents.
History of splenectomy (unless approved by principal investigator).
Allergic reactions attributable to ruxolitinib or its excipients.
Requirement for concomitant medication that is a strong cytochrome P450 3A4 inhibitor.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illnesses.
Inadequate liver function (AST/ALT >3× ULN or bilirubin >ULN) or renal function (creatinine >ULN or GFR <60 mL/min/1.73 m²) failing screen criteria.
Psychiatric illness or social circumstances likely to impair adherence to study protocols.
Pregnancy, lactation, or lack of agreement to reliable contraception methods for persons of reproductive potential.

Active uncontrolled infection necessitating IV antimicrobial therapy.
Clinically significant corneal disease.
Known HIV infection.
Active viral hepatitis (exception made for controlled low‑level HBV/HCV as detailed in protocol; otherwise excluded).
Pregnancy or nursing motherhood.
Currently active symptomatic brain metastases or those requiring ongoing steroid/antiepileptic therapy (asymptomatic, stable CNS disease permitted).
Unresolved adverse events from previous anticancer therapy greater than CTCAE grade 1 (chronic grade 2 toxicities permissible upon sponsor approval).
More than one primary malignancy diagnosed within three years, excluding appropriately treated non‑melanoma skin cancer, in‑situ carcinomas, fully cured other solid tumors, or contralateral breast cancer.
Substance abuse or any other medical condition judged to raise safety risk or impede study conduct.
Hypersensitivity to investigational product components or excipients.
Other clinician‑determined factors rendering participant unsuitable for study participation.

Pathologic T4 disease.
Planned elective pelvic lymph node radiation therapy.
Prior anorectal surgery compromising rectal anatomy (e.g., low anterior resection, abdominoperineal resection, absent rectum).
Inflammatory bowel disease requiring steroid treatment (Crohn’s disease, ulcerative colitis).
Active connective tissue disorder (lupus, scleroderma, etc.).
Urogenital anatomical abnormality precluding Barrigel injection.
White blood cell count <4000/μL or >12,000/μL.
Hemoglobin <10 g/dL.
Clinically significant coagulopathy (PTT >35 seconds, INR >1.4, platelets <100,000/mm³).
Serum ALT/AST >2.5× ULN.
Creatinine >2.0 mg/dL.
Bilirubin >2.0 mg/dL.
History of chronic renal failure.
Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus (fasting glucose >300 mg/dL despite medication).
Acquired immune deficiency syndrome (history of AIDS). Controlled HIV permitted if CD4⁺ ≥350 cells/µL without AIDS‑defining illnesses.
Contraindication to magnetic resonance imaging or PSMA/PET scanning (e.g., non‑MRI‑compatible devices).
Presence of bilateral hip prostheses incompatible with MR imaging artifacts.
Unable or unwilling to comply with study procedures.
Any condition deemed by the investigator to interfere with safe administration or completion of study therapy.

general_exclusion_criteria:
• Receipt of any investigational anticancer agent within 28 days preceding randomization.  
• Presence of centrally located (CNS) metastases that are neurologically unstable, symptomatic, or necessitating systemic steroid administration.  
• Current acute kidney injury (onset 48 hours to 7 days) or chronic kidney disease persisting ≥3 months.  
• Baseline xerostomia graded ≥2 according to CTCAE version 5.  
• Uncontrolled hypertension, myocardial infarction, angina pectoris, or coronary artery bypass graft surgery occurring within 6 months prior to consent, or any clinically active serious cardiovascular disease.  
• History of lymphoproliferative disorders or any other invasive malignancy diagnosed within the last 5 years (exceptions permitted for fully resected basal cell carcinoma, actinic keratosis, or non‑invasive colonic polyps).  
• History of pneumonitis.  
• Congestive heart failure or other significant cardiac insufficiency.  
• Clinically meaningful hepatic dysfunction.  
• Untreated or progressing intracranial metastases.  
• Active human immunodeficiency virus infection or untreated hepatitis B/C viral infections.  
• Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status greater than 2.

general_exclusion_criteria:
- Residual toxicities greater than Grade 1 from prior anticancer therapies (except alopecia).
- Recent palliative radiotherapy within 14 days or definitive radiotherapy within 42 days preceding Cycle 1 Day 1.
- Concurrent participation in another investigational drug study.
- Known hypersensitivity to peposertab (M3814), liposomal doxorubicin, or excipients thereof.
- Inability to discontinue strong CYP3A4/5, CYP2C9, or CYP2C19 inhibitors/inducers or substrates with narrow therapeutic indexes as stipulated.
- Requirement to continue potent proton‑pump inhibitors without ability to hold them appropriately.
- Left ventricular ejection fraction below institution’s lower limits of normal.
- Uncontrolled comorbid illnesses (e.g., unstable cardiovascular disease, severe infections).
- Inability to ingest oral capsules intact.
- Active or progressing brain metastases or leptomeningeal disease needing urgent intervention.
- Pregnancy or lactation; inadequate contraceptive measures for participants of reproductive potential.
- Current human immunodeficiency virus infection with detectable viral load despite antiretroviral therapy.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Metastatic breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast cancer. Prior treatment required: HER2‑0 or HER2‑low status; 0‑1 prior lines of systemic therapy in the metastatic setting; if hormone‐receptor positive (estrogen receptor ≥1% or progesterone receptor ≥1%), prior CDK4/6 inhibitor and progression on or within 12 months of completing adjuvant endocrine therapy or receipt of ≥1 line of endocrine therapy in the metastatic setting. general_exclusion_criteria: Concurrent use of any other investigational or study agents intended to treat the underlying malignancy.; Any prior treatment containing a topoisomerase I‑targeting chemotherapeutic agent, except as specifically permitted for crossover cohorts.; Receipt of live, attenuated vaccine within 30 days prior to first dosing.; Clinically significant corneal disease.; Severe hypersensitivity reaction history to trastuzumab deruxtecan, datopotamab deruxtecan, or their excipients, or to other monoclonal antibodies.; Major surgery within 2 weeks prior to study treatment initiation.; Uncontrolled intercurrent serious illness—including active infection, uncontrolled non‑malignant systemic disease, uncontrolled seizures, or psychiatric/sociological factors limiting compliance.; Current or unresolved non‑infectious pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease, or inability to exclude ILD/Pneumonitis on screening imaging.; Significant preexisting pulmonary disorders (recent PE, severe asthma/COPD, restrictive disease, pleural effusions, autoimmune/connective‑tissue disease with lung involvement, prior pneumonectomy.).; Baseline corrected QTc exceeding 470 ms in females or 450 ms in males.; Recent (<6 mo) cardiovascular interventions or events (CABG, PCI, stenting, MI, unstable angina, NYHA Class II‑III CHF, stroke).; History of second malignant neoplasm unless disease free ≥3 years or meets predefined exceptions (CIS cervix/prostate, superficial bladder, non‑melanoma skin).; Untreated or inadequately controlled HIV infection.; Active or uncontrolled Hepatitis B or C infection.; Active infectious processes including tuberculosis.; Conditions, therapies, or lab abnormalities that could confound efficacy assessment or jeopardize safe completion of the study.; Pregnancy, lactation, or intent to become pregnant.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of autoimmune disease or immune deficiency.
Active or historical idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, organising pneumonia, drug‑induced pneumonitis, idiopathic pneumonitis, or current pneumonitis on baseline CT.
Active tuberculosis.
Severe infection within four weeks preceding enrolment.
Recent therapeutic antimicrobial administration within two weeks (except prophylaxis).
Significant cardiovascular disease.
Receipt of live attenuated vaccination within four weeks or anticipated need during study.
Previous allogenic bone‑marrow or solid‑organ transplantation.
Treatment with systemic immunostimulatory agents (e.g., interferons, IL‑2) within four weeks or five drug half‑lives before starting study drugs.
Use of systemic immunosuppressive medicines within two weeks (with exceptions noted for inhaled steroids, low‑dose COPD/asthma steroids, mineralocorticoids, low‑dose adrenal replacement).
Untreated central nervous system disease, uncontrolled seizure disorder, stroke/TIA/subarachnoid haemorrhage within six months.
Prior exposure to T‑cell co‑stimulation or immune‑checkpoint inhibitor therapies (anti‑PD‑1, anti‑PD‑L1, anti‑CTLA‑4, CD137 agonists).

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease,
Active uncontrolled cardiovascular disease (recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, CABG, PCI, or stent placement within 6 months),
Congestive heart failure NYHA Class II‑IV,
Severe hypertension resistant to medication,
Myocarditis,
Significant arrhythmias or conduction abnormalities without appropriate device support,
Recent cerebrovascular accident or transient ischemic attack (< 3 months),
Prolonged QTc (> 500 ms),
Clinically significant anemia, leukopenia, or thrombocytopenia lasting > 3 months of unclear origin,
Hematologic malignancies or myelodysplastic syndrome,
Active infectious diseases requiring systemic therapy (bacterial, fungal, viral—including COVID‑19 symptomatics),
Untreated or progressive central nervous system metastases,
Ongoing seizures or neurologic disorders increasing seizure risk,
Moderate-to-severe hepatic impairment (Child‑Pugh B/C) or active cirrhosis,
Renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance < 30 ml/min),
Pregnancy or breastfeeding,
Known hypersensitivity to investigational agents or their constituents,
Concurrent participation in another therapeutic clinical trial,
Use of potent cytochrome P450 3A4 enzyme modulators (strong inhibitors or inducers) without discontinuation.

Prior treatment required: Completed prior curative‐intent therapy (surgery and/or radiation therapy ± platinum‑based chemotherapy or cetuximab) at least six months before documentation of recurrence; scheduled to receive neoadjuvant pembrolizumab together with docetaxel plus cisplatin or carboplatin followed by adjuvant pembrolizumab. Prior treatment excluded: Receipt of any anti‑Programmed Death‑1, anti‑PD‑Ligand‑1, anti‑PD‑Ligand‑2 agent, or any other T‑cell stimulating/co‑inhibitory monoclonal antibody (e.g., CTLA‑4, OX40, CD137). Biomarkers required: None. Biomarkers excluded: None.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease; clinically significant congestive heart failure or unstable coronary artery disease; left ventricular ejection fraction reduced below acceptable levels; uncontrolled hypertension; arrhythmias causing symptomatic limitation; NYHA Class III-IV heart failure; impaired renal function (creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min) despite allowance thresholds; elevated transaminases greater than three times upper limit of normal; bilirubin elevation beyond permitted level; active uncontrolled infection; HIV positivity; active hepatitis B or C infection; active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppressive therapy within the past two years; concomitant systemic glucocorticoid use >10 mg/day prednisone equivalents; prior allogeneic stem cell or solid organ transplantation; pregnancy or lactation; unwillingness or inability to use effective contraception; uncontrolled central nervous system involvement or symptomatic brain metastases; persistent grade ≥ 2 toxicity from prior anticancer therapy (except alopecia); major psychiatric disorder or substance dependence interfering with compliance; simultaneous enrolment in another therapeutic clinical trial; any condition judged by investigators to jeopardize subject’s safety or compromise interpretation of study results.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: solid tumors. Histology allowed: any. Cancer burden allowed: locally advanced unresectable or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: Received prior standard anti-cancer therapy with documented disease progression or intolerance **or** standard therapy judged inappropriate **or** an investigational agent considered appropriate standard-of-care. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: KRAS G12C mutation (assessment performed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Leptomeningeal disease or carcinomatous meningitis  
Clinically significant toxicity resulting from prior cancer therapies  
Known or suspected hypersensitivity to FMC-376 or any components of the study drug  
Conditions that would interfere with study drug absorption  
Any illness or medical history that would impact safety, protocol compliance, or interpretation of study results  
History of pneumonitis  
Congestive heart failure or symptomatic cardiac insufficiency  
Significant renal dysfunction or impaired kidney function  
Severe hepatic dysfunction or compromised liver function  
Uncontrolled brain metastases or active central nervous system involvement  
Active infections including human immunodeficiency virus or chronic hepatitis B/C  
Performance status worse than ECOG 1 (i.e., ECOG ≥2)

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Waldenström macroglobulinemia. Histology allowed: Waldenström macroglobulinemia (lymphoplasmacytic lymphoma). Cancer burden allowed: Symptomatic disease meeting International Workshop on Waldenström macroglobulinemia treatment criteria (constitutive symptoms, cytopenias, bulky adenopathy or spleen, hyperviscosity, peripheral neuropathy, amyloid, renal insufficiency, cryoglobulinemia, etc.). Prior treatment required: None (treatment‑naive). Prior treatment excluded: Any prior systemic therapy for Waldenström macroglobulinemia. Biomarkers required: Assessment of MYD88 and CXCR4 mutation status (any status accepted); biomarker determination required during screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Serious underlying medical, laboratory, psychiatric, or social condition preventing signed informed consent. Pregnant, nursing, or intending to become pregnant or breastfeed during study. Central nervous system involvement by Waldenström macroglobulinemia. Documented human immunodeficiency virus infection. Active hepatitis B infection (positive HBsAg or detectable HBV DNA) or active hepatitis C infection (detectable HCV RNA). Concurrent systemic immunosuppressant therapy (aside from glucocorticoids <20 mg/day prednisone equivalents). Active or ongoing autoimmune hemolytic anemia or immune thrombocytopenia. Concomitant use of warfarin or related anticoagulants. Receipt of live attenuated vaccination within four weeks before first study drug dose. Active uncontrolled bacterial, viral, fungal, or parasitic infection (other than limited fungal nail infection). Major surgery within four weeks prior to first dose. History of severe inherited bleeding disorders (e.g., hemophilia) or spontaneous bleeding requiring transfusion, or ischemic stroke or intracranial hemorrhage within six months. Inability to swallow oral medications. Anticipated non‑compliance with study procedures. Prior malignancy within three years except cured basal or squamous cell skin cancer, non‑muscle‑invasive bladder cancer, carcinoma in situ of cervix or breast, or localized Gleason score ≤ 6 prostate cancer. Severe or disabling pulmonary disease. Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease—including unstable angina within three months, myocardial infarction within six months, left ventricular ejection fraction ≤45 %, NYHA class III‑IV heart failure, or life‑threatening arrhythmias. Hypersensitivity to any excipient of zanubrutinib, rituximab, or bendamustine. Recent use of strong CYP3A inducers.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: epithelial ovarian cancer, fallopian tube carcinoma, primary peritoneal carcinoma. Histology allowed: any epithelial histology except mesonephric, mucinous, neuroendocrine/small cell, or undifferentiated. Cancer burden allowed: platinum‑resistant disease (progression within 6 months of last platinum) and measurable disease. Prior treatment required: at least one prior platinum‑based chemotherapy regimen; ≤4 prior lines of systemic therapy. Prior treatment excluded: weekly paclitaxel in the recurrent setting; any BCL‑XL inhibitor. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: history of major surgery within 8 weeks prior to first dose; recent small or large bowel obstruction within 8 weeks; clinically significant ascites or pleural effusion requiring repeat drainage within 4 weeks; dependency on total parenteral nutrition or continual intravenous fluid support; active infection graded ≥2 occurring within 2 weeks before treatment; baseline corrected QT interval >470 ms or congenital long QT syndrome; recent bleeding complications within 4 weeks or underlying coagulopathy; uncontrolled intercurrent illness such as symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, serious cardiac arrhythmia; current use of strong CYP3A4 or CYP2C8 enzyme inhibitors or inductors; pregnancy or breastfeeding; known allergy or hypersensitivity to paclitaxel, DT2216, or their formulations; chronic systemic steroid use >10 mg/day prednisone-equivalent or other potent immunosuppressants; active central nervous system metastases (treated, stable brain metastases allowed); inability to comply with protocol-specified contraceptive measures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: ovarian carcinosarcoma. Histology allowed: ovarian carcinosarcoma (limited to ≤20% of enrolled population). clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 to 65 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: acute myeloid leukemia. Histology allowed: acute myeloid leukemia (de novo or secondary). Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory disease with ≥5 % blasts in bone marrow or extramedullary sites documented within prior 4 weeks. Prior treatment required: at least one prior systemic anticancer regimen (examples: intensive 3+7 chemotherapy, hypomethylating agent). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA. Additional inclusion criteria applied to all spaces: available haploidentical HLA‑matched related donor aged 18‑65 years; Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status ≤2 (Karnofsky ≥60); pulmonary function tests FEV1, FVC and diffusing capacity ≥60 % predicted; cardiac left ventricular ejection fraction ≥45 % and no pulmonary hypertension; serum total bilirubin within institution’s normal limits (except permissible Gilberts variant); alanine aminotransferase/aspartate aminotransferase <2× ULN; serum creatinine within normal limits or calculated creatinine clearance >50 mL/min/1.73 m²; agreement to use effective contraception throughout study and for stipulated post‑treatment interval; able to comprehend and voluntarily sign informed consent.
general_exclusion_criteria: Unresolved non‑hematologic toxicities greater than grade 1 from prior anticancer therapy (unless isolated alopecia); prior administration of gemtuzumab ozogamicin or other agents linked to heightened risk of hepatic sinusoidal obstruction syndrome/vveno‑occlusive disease or existing/history of VOD; ongoing involvement in another interventional investigation within 21 days or five half‑lives; presence of extramedullary disease involving central nervous system, testes, or eyes; myocardial infarction occurring within two years before enrolment; deep vein thrombosis/pulmonary embolism within one year (exceptions for resolved line‑associated clot after completing anticoagulation); cerebrovascular accident or transient ischemic attack within one year; clinically significant gastrointestinal bleeding, gastric ulcer, erosive gastritis, bowel perforation, or hemoptysis within six months; personal history of thrombotic microangiopathies including hemolytic‑uremic syndrome or thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura; hypersensitivity reactions to intravenous iron preparations or murine‑derived monoclonal antibodies; detectable donor‑specific antibodies of clinical relevance necessitating desensitization procedures; inability to discontinue drugs interfering with cyclophosphamide or thio­tepa metabolism during designated peri‑conditioning window; uncontrolled bacterial, viral, or fungal infection; prior receipt of any allogeneic or autologous hematopoietic stem cell transplant or solid organ transplant; antecedent exposures preventing delivery of myeloablative total mar​row lymphoid irradiation; HIV infection while on combination antiretroviral therapy; chronic hepatitis B or C virus infection; severe psychiatric disorder or unstable social circumstance compromising adherence; current pregnancy or lactation (pregnancy testing required for women of reproductive potential, breastfeeding prohibited during study drug exposure); history of another malignant neoplasm unless disease free for at least five years or limited to in‑situ cervical carcinoma, basal cell carcinoma, or cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma diagnosed and treated within the preceding five years.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Soft tissue sarcoma of the retroperitoneal space or infraperitoneal spaces of pelvis. Histology allowed: Any soft tissue sarcoma histology except gastrointestinal stromal tumour, rhabdomyosarcoma, primitive neuroectodermal tumour, other small round blue cell sarcoma, osteosarcoma, chondrosarcoma, aggressive fibromatosis, sarcomatoid carcinoma, metastatic carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Primary, locally recurrent, or metastatic disease requiring treatment of the retroperitoneal mass. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior radiation therapy to the retroperitoneal area or prior abdominal or pelvic irradiation for another malignancy or disease. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of clinically significant pulmonary toxicities such as pneumonitis.
Unstable cardiac conditions including congestive heart failure.
Significant renal impairment beyond protocol-specified limits.
Severe hepatic dysfunction exceeding protocol-defined laboratory thresholds.
Active untreated central nervous system metastasis or symptomatic brain lesions.
Human immunodeficiency virus infection receiving antiretroviral therapy.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: Solid tumor. Histology allowed: Any solid tumor histology. Prior treatment excluded: Prior exposure to BRAF dimer inhibitor agents. Biomarkers required: Presence of a pathogenic KRAS mutation or HRAS mutation or NRAS mutation or BRAF mutation or CRAF mutation or NF1 mutation. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Pregnancy or breastfeeding; inability to comply with contraception requirements; inadequate organ function or electrolyte abnormalities; ECOG performance status >1; life expectancy ≤ 6 months; prior or concurrent prohibited medications within restricted windows (excluding washout periods); receipt of investigational anti‑tumour therapy within disallowed interval; known hypersensitivity to study drug components; invasive malignancy within 2 years apart from current indication or cured cancers; unresolved toxicities from preceding therapy; significant cardiac impairment; recent arterial or venous thromboembolic event; conditions affecting gastrointestinal absorption; major surgical procedure within 28 days before dosing; active infection including untreated HIV, Hepatitis B/C; uncontrolled central nervous system involvement or symptomatic brain mets; severe hepatic or renal insufficiency; ongoing immunosuppressive therapy; unwillingness or inability to give informed consent.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: Endometrial cancer. Histology allowed: Endometrioid endometrial carcinoma or endometrial carcinosarcoma with endometrioid epithelial component. Prior treatment excluded: Prior CDK4/6 inhibitor therapy. Biomarkers required: Estrogen receptor positive (≥1 % nuclear staining by IHC). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of brain metastases.
Allergic reaction to study drug class or chemically similar substances.
Requirement for concomitant strong/moderate CYP3A4 inducer or strong CYP3A4 inhibitor.
Impairment of gastrointestinal function affecting drug absorption.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness (ongoing active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, significant cardiac arrhythmia, severe psychiatric disorder impairing compliance).
Use of known QT‑prolonging medicines during screening or anticipated during study.
Recent serious cardiovascular event (acute coronary syndrome, PCI, or stenting within six months).
Pregnancy or lactation.
Concurrent malignancy (except adequately treated basal/squamous skin cancer, in‑situ breast or cervical carcinoma, or other cancer disease‑free ≥5 years).
Known HIV infection.
Uncontrolled hypertension (SBP > 150 mmHg or DBP > 90 mmHg).

Biomarkers required: Wildtype TP53 (by IHC or NGS), hemoglobin A1c ≤6.4 %, fasting plasma glucose ≤140 mg/DL. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of brain metastases.
Allergic reaction to study drug class or chemically similar substances.
Requirement for concomitant strong/moderate CYP3A4 inducer or strong CYP3A4 inhibitor.
Impairment of gastrointestinal function affecting drug absorption.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness (ongoing active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, significant cardiac arrhythmia, severe psychiatric disorder impairing compliance).
Use of known QT‑prolonging medicines during screening or anticipated during study.
Recent serious cardiovascular event (acute coronary syndrome, PCI, or stenting within six months).
Pregnancy or lactation.
Concurrent malignancy (except adequately treated basal/squamous skin cancer, in‑situ breast or cervical carcinoma, or other cancer disease‑free ≥5 years).
Known HIV infection.
Uncontrolled hypertension (SBP > 150 mmHg or DBP > 90 mmHg).

Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of pneumonitis or prior interstitial lung disease; clinically significant heart failure; known human immunodeficiency virus infection; active hepatitis B or C infection; ongoing infectious process requiring therapeutic intervention; solid organ transplantation or allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation; active autoimmune disease; immunodeficiency or receipt of systemic corticosteroids or other immunosuppressive medication; prior malignancy diagnosed within five years preceding enrolment.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Histology allowed: chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory disease meeting international workshop on chronic lymphocytic leukemia criteria for treatment, with measurable disease defined as at least one of the following — peripheral blood absolute B‐cell count >5,000 cells/μL, bone marrow infiltrates >30%, palpable splenomegaly or lymph node mass >1.5 cm. Prior treatment required: at least two prior systemic therapies for chronic lymphocytic leukemia, one of which must have included a covalent brutonʼs tyrosine kinase inhibitor. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of pneumonitis. Heart failure or left ventricular systolic impairment. Renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance <60 mL/min). Significant hepatic dysfunction (bilirubin >1.5× upper limit of normal, transaminases >3× ULN). Uncontrolled central nervous system disease or untreated brain metastasis. Active HIV infection or hepatitis B/C infection. Poor performance status greater than ECOG 2. Current pregnancy or breastfeeding. Recent receipt (<30 days) of live vaccinations. Requirement for chronic high-dose immunosuppression (>20 mg/day prednisolone-equivalent) or steroid dependence. Autoimmune hematologic disorders requiring high‑dose steroids (autoimmune hemolytic anemia, immune thrombocytopenia). Concurrent investigational agents for chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Allergies to study drugs or related compounds. Strong or prohibited concomitant CYP3A modulators where dose adjustment is not feasible. Cardiovascular disease judged unsafe by investigator, including baseline corrected QT interval >470 ms. Tuberculosis treatment within the prior two years. Active systemic bacterial, fungal, or viral infection necessitating IV antimicrobials. Substance/alcohol misuse. Conditions preventing safe swallowing of oral medication.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of non‑infectious pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease requiring corticosteroids, current interstitial lung disease, or suspicion of interstitial lung disease that cannot be definitively excluded by imaging; lung‑specific intercurrent medically significant severe illnesses; unresolved active infection necessitating intravenous antimicrobial therapy; pleural, pericardial, or abdominal effusions requiring invasive drainage procedures or specialized therapies; documented somatic HER2 (ERBB2) genomic alteration without corresponding HER2 protein overexpression; primary diagnosis of breast adenocarcinoma, colonic or rectal adenocarcinoma, gastric adenocarcinoma, or non‑small cell lung carcinoma (as stipulated for specific parts of the study); any concurrent medical condition judged by investigators to compromise safe participation or data integrity.

general_exclusion_criteria:
- Enrollment in another interventional clinical trial.
- Ongoing treatment with other investigational anticancer agents or devices; treatment at recurrence with carmustine wafer implants or proteasome inhibitors.
- Prior therapy with procarbazine/lomustine/vincristine (PCV) regimens, individual components thereof, carmustine wafer implantation, or bevacizumab.
- Prior use of poly(ADP-ribose) polymerase (PARP) inhibitors.
- Major surgery (other than intended resections for recurrent diffuse glioma) within four weeks before starting study medication.
- Radiotherapy within twelve weeks prior to documentation of progression unless progression occurs entirely outside the irradiated volume or is pathologically confirmed.
- Prior cranial radiotherapy exceeding 65 Gray, stereotactic radiosurgery, or brachytherapy unless recurrence is histologically proved.
- Requirement for full‑dose anticoagulation unless coagulation parameters are therapeutically controlled and dosing has been stable for at least two weeks.
- Concomitant use of medicines that are sensitive CYP2D6/CYP2C19 substrates unable to be substituted.
- Enzyme‑inducing antiseizure drugs (patients must be on non‑inducing agents or discontinued inducer ≥2 weeks).
- Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
- Known hypersensitivity to NMS‑03305293 or temozolomide formulation constituents.
- Active untreated bacterial, fungal, viral (including HIV) infections needing systemic therapy.
- Prolonged corrected QT interval (QTc ≥460 ms females, ≥450 ms males) or additional risk factors for torsades de pointe.
- Significant gastrointestinal disorders impairing oral drug absorption (ulcers, inflammatory bowel disease, short‑gut syndrome, etc.).
- Recent serious cardiovascular events (myocardial infarction, unstable angina, coronary/pedal arterial bypass, symptomatic heart failure, stroke, or transient ischemic attack) within six months.
- History of another malignant neoplasm except adequately treated non‑melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ, or localized cancer disease‑free for ≥3 years.
- Uncontrolled comorbidities or laboratory abnormalities posing excessive risk or confounding outcome assessments (severe hepatic, renal, hematologic impairment, uncontrolled brain metastasis, etc.).

general_exclusion_criteria: History of brain metastases; symptomatic skeletal event (spinal cord compression, pathologic fracture, or recent radiation/surgery to bone within 6 months); uncontrollable pain from prostate cancer or comorbidity; visceral crisis indicating rapid disease progression with life expectancy <18 months; uncontrolled intercurrent illnesses; concurrent second active malignant neoplasm; severe sleep disorders such as narcolepsy; ocular conditions preventing adequate light transmission (untreated cataract, severe glaucoma, macular degeneration, blindness, pupillary abnormalities, retinal disease); severe psychiatric impairments (bipolar disorder, mania); current night‑shift work; sensitivity to light, seizure disorder, or epilepsy; significant lingering Grade > 1 toxicities from prior anticancer therapy (except alopecia); allergies to substances chemically or biologically similar to study agents.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast carcinoma. Biomarkers required: Estrogen‑receptor ≤5% AND Progesterone‑receptor ≤5% AND HER2‑negative (per ASCO/CAP guidelines) AND PD‑L1‑negative (tumor‑ infiltrating immune cells <1% by SP142 assay OR combined positive score <10 by 22C3 assay). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Has received prior systemic anti‑cancer therapy, including investigational agents, within 4 weeks of study treatment initiation.  
Prior hypersensitivity reaction to pembrolizumab, sacituzumab govitecan, or their excipients.  
Known homozygous UGT1A1*28 polymorphism.  
Untreated, symptomatic, or actively progressing central nervous system metastases.  
Major surgery performed within 2 weeks preceding study entry.  
Uncontrolled concurrent acute or chronic medical disorders (active infection, unstable cardiovascular disease, uncontrolled diabetes, severe pulmonary disease, seizure disorder, psychiatric instability, etc.).  
Chronic systemic glucocorticoid usage exceeding 10 mg/day prednisone equivalents or any other immunosuppressive medications within the past two years.  
Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic steroids or immunosuppressants.  
History of non‑infectious pneumonitis that required steroids or current pneumonitis.  
Second malignant neoplasm unless disease free ≥3 years (exceptions: cervix or prostate carcinoma in situ, superficial bladder cancer, non‑melanoma skin cancer).  
Positive serology for HIV, hepatitis B surface antigen, or detectable hepatitis C viral RNA.  
Receipt of a live attenuated vaccine within 28 days before initiating study treatment.  
Breastfeeding individuals.

Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppressive therapy.
Any uncontrolled concurrent illness or laboratory abnormality judged by investigator to pose unacceptable risk or interfere with protocol adherence or outcome evaluation.

Presence of active central nervous system metastases or carcinomatous meningitis. Ongoing clinically significant autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression (e.g., prednisone ≥10 mg/day). Uncontrolled intercurrent illness—including active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, recent myocardial infarction/stroke (<6 months), or serious cardiac arrhythmia. Current untreated or progressing secondary malignancy (excluding appropriately treated basal cell carcinoma, cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma, or in‑situ cervical cancer). Laboratory abnormalities outside acceptable ranges (absolute neutrophil count <1,000/mm³, hemoglobin <9 g/dL, platelet count <100,000/mm³, total bilirubin >upper limit of normal, ALT/AST >2.5× ULN [≤1.5× ULN if ALP elevated] or >5× ULN with liver metastases, serum creatinine >1.5× ULN or calculated creatinine clearance <60 mL/min/1.73 m²). Positive serologic results for hepatitis B surface antigen or hepatitis C antibody (unless HCV RNA negative). Pregnant or nursing individuals, or anyone unable or unwilling to employ appropriate contraception throughout the study and for 120 days following the last administered dose.

Biomarkers required: IDH‑wildtype status verified by immunohistochemistry or DNA sequencing; when classifying as IDH‑wt diffuse glioma, demonstration of EGFR amplification or TERT promoter mutation or Chr 7 gain/Chr 10 loss. Active, uncontrolled infections at baseline.

History of grade 3 or 4 veno‑occlusive disease/sinusoidal obstructive syndrome.
CD33‑negative leukemic blast phenotype (participants may enroll but will not receive gemtuzumab ozogamicin).

Concurrent participation in another therapeutic clinical trial. inability to comply with study procedures.

general_exclusion_criteria:
* Current participation in or receipt of an investigational product/device within 4 weeks preceding first dose.
* Systemic immunosuppression exceeding 10 mg/day prednisone-equivalent or other immunosuppressants for active autoimmune disease.
* History of grade ≥3 immune‑related adverse event from prior immunotherapy (unless fully resolved to ≤grade 1).
* Active (symptomatic or progressive) central nervous system or leptomeningeal metastases.
* Concurrent actively progressing second malignancy (exceptions: adequately treated basal cell carcinoma, squamous cell carcinoma of skin, or in‑situ cervical cancer).
* Recent invasive cancer treated within 24 months (indolent tumors exempt).\n * Evidence of interstitial lung disease or active non‑infectious pneumonitis.\n * Uncontrolled acute infection requiring systemic antimicrobial therapy.\n * Significant medical conditions, laboratory abnormalities, or concomitant therapies deemed unsuitable by investigators.\n * Psychiatric illness or substance misuse interfering with adherence.\n * Pregnant, lactating, or intending conception/fatherhood during study period.\n * Receipt of live attenuated vaccination within 30 days before starting protocol therapy.\n * Known hypersensitivity to dostarilimab, cobolimab, interferon‑α, or ipilimumab constituents.\n * Documented human immunodeficiency virus infection.\n * Positive serologic markers for hepatitis B or C infection (active viremia).

Significant psychiatric or substance‑abuse disorders interfering with compliance.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Solid tumor. Histology allowed: Any solid tumor histology. Biomarkers required: Pathogenic mutation (germline or somatic) in any of the following DNA‑damage‑repair genes—as identified by a CLIA‑certified assay: BRCA1, BRCA2, PALB2, RAD51C, RAD51D, ATM, BARD1, BLM, BRIP1, CDK12, FANCA, FANCC, FANCD2, FANCE, FANCF, FANCM, MRE11A, NBN (NBS1), RAD50, RAD51B; additional homologous recombination deficiency alterations may be permitted if authorized by the PI/CTEP. Biomarkers excluded: UGT1A1 genotype showing homozygosity for *28 (TA)_7_TAA_, homozygosity for *6 (G71R), or compound heterozygosity (*28 plus *6).
general_exclusion_criteria: History of significant cardiovascular disease including NYHA Class III-IV heart failure; uncontrolled hypertension; recent myocardial infarction; clinically unstable arrhythmias; prolonged QTc (>480 ms) or use of prohibited QT‑prolonging medicines within 14 days; baseline laboratory abnormalities indicating Grade ≥2 toxicity aside from allowable low-grade cytopenias; active uncontrolled infection; ongoing moderate/severe hepatic insufficiency or cirrhosis; Child-Pugh score > A; estimated glomerular filtration rate < 60 mL/min; unresolved serious adverse events from prior anticancer therapy (grade > 1 excluding alopecia); current use of strong CYP3A4/5 inhibitors or inducers; concurrent use of drugs that are substrates of BCRP/ABC​G2, OATP1B1/B3/OATP2B1, OAT1/OAT3 within 14 days; intake of dietary/herbal supplements with recognized hepatotoxic risk; inability to comply with required biopsy schedule; lack of reliable contraception adherence (for participants of reproductive potential); pregnancy or breastfeeding; known hypersensitivity to novobiocin or related compounds.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: adenocarcinoma. Prior treatment excluded: prior anti‑HER3 antibody; prior topoisomerase I inhibitor; prior antibody‑drug conjugate containing an exatecan derivative. Biomarkers required: EGFR activating mutation (G719X **or** exon 19 deletion **or** L858R **or** L861Q) **and**, if the EGFR TKI used is erlotinib, gefitinib, or afatinib, absence of EGFR T790M mutation. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of other active malignancy within 3 years (exceptions: adequately treated basal/squamous cell skin cancer, superficial bladder tumours, curatively treated in‑situ disease). Evidence of spinal cord compression or clinically active central nervous system metastasis (symptomatic or requiring steroid/corticosteroid therapy). Recent (<6 months) myocardial infarction. Symptomatic congestive heart failure (NYHA class II‑IV), unstable angina, or serious cardiac arrhythmia requiring medication. Left ventricular ejection fraction <50%. Clinically significant ECG abnormalities (complete left bundle‑branch block, third‑degree AV block, second‑degree AV block, PR interval >250 ms). Prolonged QTc interval (>470 ms women, >450 ms men) persisting over three screenings. Current use of medications that markedly lengthen QT interval or predispose to torsades de pointes. Known congenital long QT syndrome or familial sudden cardiac death <40 years. Past or current interstitial lung disease, pulmonary fibrosis, or grade ≥2 radiation pneumonitis. Ongoing interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis at screening. Clinically significant corneal disease. Severe respiratory compromise secondary to preexisting pulmonary disease, autoimmune/connective‑tissue disease involving lungs, or prior total pneumonectomy. Chronic systemic glucocorticoids >10 mg/day prednisone-equivalent or other immunosuppressive therapy. Uncontrolled hypertension (SBP >180 mmHg or DBP >110 mmHg). Pregnant or nursing individuals, or persons planning conception without acceptable contraception. Positive HIV infection not well controlled. Active hepatitis B or C infection (serologic evidence of replication within 28 days). Poor performance status (Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group score >1). Unresolved toxicity from prior anticancer therapy greater than CTCAE grade 1 (apart from alopecia). Any major concurrent illness, lab abnormality, or psychosocial circumstance judged by investigator to place the patient at undue risk or interfere with study assessments.

Prior treatment required: Second or greater relapse **or** primary refractory disease after at least two induction attempts **or** first relapse refractory to at least one re‑induction attempt **or** any relapse occurring after hematopoietic stem cell transplant; for Philadelphia chromosome‑positive disease, must have received two different tyrosine kinase inhibitors. Prior treatment excluded: Previous administration of inotuzumab ozogamicin. Biomarkers required: Demonstration of surface CD22 expression on leukemic blasts by flow cytometry, FISH, or immunohistochemistry; for Philadelphia chromosome‑positive disease, detection of t(9;22)/BCR‑ABL fusion. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of hepatic veno‑occlusive/sinusoidal obstructive syndrome; isolated central nervous system, testicular, or other purely extramedullary site of relapse; active optic nerve or retinal involvement; current enrolment in another interventional investigational drug study; concurrent use of antileukemic agents aside from approved exceptions (hydroxyurea, intrathecal chemotherapy); chronic graft‑vs‑host disease prevention or organ‐rejectant medication usage; unrestricted systemic corticosteroid use contrary to protocol specifications; known HIV infection, hepatitis B or C infection; active uncontrolled bacterial, fungal, viral, or parasitic infection; recent serious febrile illness or bacteremia; underlying inherited bone‑marrow failure syndromes (Bloom, ataxia‑telangiectasia, Fanconi anaemia, Kostmann, Shwachman‑Diamond, etc.).

general_exclusion_criteria:

Pregnancy or lactation in females.
Inability or unwillingness to abide by contraception/reproduction guidelines through day 90 after last study drug administration.

Clinically severe active autoimmune diseases needing systemic immunosuppression within the preceding 12 months (exceptions: vitiligo, remote mild asthma/allergy). Significant morphological abnormality suggestive of myeloproliferative/neoplastic process on baseline blood smear (MPN, MDS, AML, ALL). Prior receipt of anti‑PD‑1, anti‑PD‑L1, anti‑PD‑L2, anti‑CTLA‑4 monoclonal antibodies, or experimental agents targeting T‑cell checkpoints/co‑stimulatory receptors. Live or attenuated vaccination within 30 days before first study drug administration. Documented hypersensitivity to tazemetostat, niravormet, ipilimumab, or structurally related substances. Inability to comply reliably with scheduled assessments and follow‑up procedures.

Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior cytotoxic chemotherapy for the current diagnosis (excluding steroid pretreatment and intrathecal cytarabine); receipt of hydroxyurea for >72 hours within seven days before starting protocol therapy. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Patients with Down syndrome.
Inadequate diagnostic material for mandatory molecular/genetic testing (insufficient bone marrow or peripheral blood).
Acute undifferentiated leukaemia.
Known Charcot‑Marie‑Tooth disease.
Presence of MYC translocation characteristic of Burkitt-type mature B‑cell neoplasms.
Requirement for radiotherapy at diagnosis.
Pregnant females.
Breastfeeding mothers planning to nurse during study drug exposure and for two months thereafter.
Individuals of reproductive potential unwilling or unable to use effective contraception throughout study participation and for mandated periods after dosing.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of pneumonia/pneumonitis.
Significant cardiac impairment (heart failure, recent myocardial infarction).
Renal dysfunction (creatinine ≤ 1.5×ULN acceptable; worse function excluded).
Severe hepatic dysfunction (transaminases > 3×ULN or bilirubin > 1.5×ULN unacceptable).
Active or uncontrolled central nervous system metastases.
Human immunodeficiency virus infection.
Active hepatitis B or hepatitis C infection.
Poor performance status (Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group score > 1).
Ongoing systemic corticosteroid or immunosuppressive therapy for autoimmune disease.
Symptomatic autoimmune diseases (rheumatoid arthritis, systemic scleroderma, systemic lupus erythematosus, Sjögren’s syndrome, vasculitides, Guillain‑Barre syndrome, Myasthenia Gravis, Multiple Sclerosis).
Inflammatory bowel disease or diverticulitis.
Concurrent antiplatelet or therapeutic anticoagulant therapy causing increased bleed risk.
Recent major surgery, radiation, or chemotherapeutic interventions outside allowable washout windows.
Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or unwillingness to practice adequate contraception.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: Waldenström macroglobulinemia. Histology allowed: Lymphoplasma​cytic lymphoma. Cancer burden allowed: Symptomatic disease requiring treatment according to International Workshop on Waldenström’s Macroglobulinaemia (IWWM2) criteria (evidence of hyperviscosity, peripheral neuropathy, cold agglutinin disease, cryoglobulinemia, amyloid deposition, cytopenias attributable to marrow infiltration, symptomatic bulkier nodal disease, symptomatic splenomegaly, unexplained constitutional symptoms, or organ dysfunction related to Walde­nströ̈m macroglobulinemia) together with serum Immunoglobulin M level >2 × Upper Limit of Normal. Prior treatment required: Receipt of an anti‑CD20 monoclonal antibody (e.g., rituximab) **and** receipt of a Bruton’s Tyrosine Kinase inhibitor (e.g., ibrutinib, zanubrutinib, acalabrutinib) in any order; patients whose first‐line regimen contained both drugs concurrently satisfy this requirement. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Tumor CD20 positivity by immunophenotype or immunohistochemistry (all intensity levels accepted) **and** serum IgM >2 × ULN (both evaluated at screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.

general_exclusion_criteria:
* Receipt of peripheral autologous stem cell transplantation within 12 weeks before first dose, or allogeneic stem cell transplantation within 1 year before first dose.  
* Unresolved adverse events grade ≥2 attributable to prior anticancer therapy.  
* Presence of non‑secretory multiple myeloma.  
* Active plasma cell leukemia (>20 % circulating plasma cells or >2.0×10⁹/L).  
* Waldenström’s macroglobulinemia.  
* Light‑chain amyloidosis.  
* POEMS syndrome.  
* Major surgical procedure within 4 weeks preceding first dose or scheduled during study participation.  
* Acute infection requiring antimicrobial therapy occurring within 14 days prior to first dose.  
* Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus or hypertension within 14 days prior to first dose.  
* Peripheral neuropathy grade ≥3, or grade ≥2 with pain, occurring within 2 weeks prior to first dose.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Hypersensitivity to the active substance or to any of the excipients.
Significant worsening in clinical status that increases risk of adverse events associated with lymphodepleting chemotherapy or excludes participation according to the treating physician’s judgment.
Any medical condition or laboratory abnormality judged by the investigator to place the participant at unacceptable risk for participating in the Expanded Access Protocol.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of hormone‑dependent cancer (e.g., breast cancer, prostate cancer, adenocarcinoma of unknown origin).
Brain cancer.
First‑degree family member with prostate cancer.
Uncontrolled congestive heart failure.
Severe untreated obstructive sleep apnea.

Residual neurological/cognitive deficits from prior stroke (Mini‑Mental State Exam score <24).
Serum creatinine >2.5 mg/dL.
Alanine aminotransferase >3 times the institutional upper limit of normal.
Poorly controlled diabetes mellitus (hemoglobin A1c >8.5%).
Body mass index >45 kg/m².
Untreated unipolar depressive illness.
Bipolar disorder.
Schizophrenia.

general_exclusion_criteria: Pregnancy or breastfeeding. Lack of signed informed consent. Permanent LCH‑related complications (vertebral collapse, sclerosing cholangiopathy, irreversible lung fibrosis) in the absence of active disease. Severe preexisting organ impairment (renal insufficiency with serum creatinine >3 × upper limit for age, glomerular filtration rate <50 mL/min/1.73 m², hepatic synthetic failure, pulmonary failure requiring mechanical ventilation not attributable to active LCH). Ongoing uncontrolled serious infection. Performance status prohibiting participation (generally Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group ≥ 2). Known HIV infection or chronic viral hepatitis B/C. History of drug‑induced interstitial pneumonitis or current unresolved pneumonitis. Significant cardiovascular disease (heart failure NYHA class III/IV).

Prior treatment required: None beyond the minimum line/combo exposure outlined above. general_exclusion_criteria:
History of plasma cell leukemia, primary light‑chain amyloidosis (except myeloma‑associated amyloidosis), Waldenström macroglobulinemia, or POEMS syndrome.
Known multiple myeloma brain lesions or meningeal involvement.
Treatment with any systemic anti‑myeloma therapy within five half‑lives or within 21 days prior to first study drug dosing.
History of allogenic or autologous stem cell transplantation.
Prior treatment with BCMA‑directed CAR‑T cell therapy or BiTEs (unless completed ≥12 weeks ago for cohort 2).
History of progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy, neurodegenerative CNS disorders, or seizures within 12 months.
Live or attenuated vaccinations within 28 days preceding study drug initiation.
Cardiac left ventricular ejection fraction < 40%.
Peripheral neuropathy Grade ≥ 2 (cohort 4 specific).
Malabsorption syndromes affecting oral absorption of lenalidomide (cohort 3) or pomalidomide (cohort 5) or investigational agents (cohorts 9, 10).
Autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression within past 2 years (cohorts 7, 8).
Solid organ transplant recipient (cohorts 7, 8).
Grade ≥ 3 immune‑mediated adverse event from prior checkpoint inhibition (cohorts 7, 8).
Encephalitis or meningitis within 1 year (cohort 8).
Active/intercurrent interstitial lung disease, non‑infectious pneumonitis, or pneumonitis within 5 years (cohort 8).
Radiation‑induced pneumonitis unresolved ≥6 months earlier (allowed).
Concurrent medication causing significant QT/QTc prolongation (cohort 9).

Prior complete pneumonectomy.

Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: any prior systemic anti‑lymphoma therapy (chemotherapy, targeted agents, immunotherapy); however, prior localised radiotherapy or brief (< 7 days) corticosteroid burst is permitted. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Known central nervous system involvement. Current active infection needing systemic antimicrobials. Major surgery or serious trauma within 4 weeks before starting study drug. Required chronic warfarin or vitamin K antagonist therapy. Severe inherited bleeding disorders (hemophilia A/B, von Willebrand disease) or unexplained recurrent bleeding. Stroke or intracranial haemorrhage within 6 months. Significant/life‑threatening haemorrhage within 3 months. Uncontrolled autoimmune haemolytic anaemia or immune thrombocytopaenia not attributable to lymphoma. Active hepatitis C infection (except if HCV RNA undetectable). Active hepatitis B infection (except if HBV DNA undetectable with prophylaxis). Previous malignant tumour unless cured ≥3 years ago (exceptions: non‑melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ of cervix or breast, Gleason 6 prostate cancer observed). Cardiac contraindications: NYHA class III/IV heart failure, myocardial infarction ≤6 months, unstable angina ≤3 months, uncontrolled arrhythmia, ventricular tachyarrhythmia ≤6 months, high-grade AV block without pacing, uncontrollable hypertension (SBP > 170 mmHg & DBP > 105 mmHg), resting QTcF > 480 ms. Inability to swallow capsules or marked GI absorption problems. Concomitant use of strong CYP3A inducers. Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or intention to conceive during study. Presence of severe/uncontrolled comorbidities compromising compliance. Inability to follow protocol‑mandated restrictions.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: Pancreatic cancer. Histology allowed: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: Receipt of prior standard‐of‐care therapy for pancreatic cancer. Prior treatment excluded: Prior RAS‑targeted therapy; prior PRMT5 inhibitor; prior MAT2A inhibitor; prior enrollment in a phase 3 trial of RMC‑6236 or RMC‑9805. Biomarkers required: Confirmed loss of MTAP protein or bi‑allelic MTAP gene deletion **AND** detection of an activating RAS mutation in tumor tissue (assessed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Has known allergy, hypersensitivity or intolerance to TNG462, RMC‑6236, RMC‑9805 or their excipients.  
Has uncontrolled intercurrent illness that would limit protocol compliance.  
Has an active infection requiring systemic therapy.  
Is concurrently participating in or planning simultaneous participation in another investigational study.  
Has impaired gastrointestinal function or disease that may substantially alter oral medication absorption.  
Has active or untreated central nervous system metastases associated with progressing neurological symptoms.  
Has current active liver disease of any etiology.  
Is HIV positive without meeting strict virologic/immunologic control criteria.  
Has clinically relevant cardiovascular disease.  
Has a history of or existing active interstitial lung disease.  
Is a female who is pregnant or lactating.  
Is unwilling or unable to comply with scheduled visits, study treatment administration, laboratory testing, or other study procedures.  
Has any other medically significant illness, condition, surgery, physical finding, or lab abnormality deemed by the investigator to jeopardize participant safety or compromise data integrity.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: female and male. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: infiltrating breast carcinoma confirmed histologically or cytologically. Cancer burden allowed: locally advanced or metastatic disease (measurable or non‑measurable lesions); brain metastases permitted only if asymptomatic, definitively treated (radiation or surgery), off corticosteroids for ≥4 weeks, and neurologically stable for >3 months. Prior treatment required: progression on an aromatase inhibitor combined with palbociclib *or* ribociclib as the first endocrine therapy for metastatic disease, with at least 6 months of disease control on that AI + CDK4/6 inhibitor regimen; may have received up to one prior cytotoxic chemotherapy regimen in the metastatic setting provided recovery from acute toxicity. Prior treatment excluded: prior disease progression on abemaciclib, fulvestrant, or any selective estrogen receptor degrader therapy. Biomarkers required: estrogen receptor positive *and* HER2 negative; at least one ESR1 ligand‑binding‑domain point mutation detectable in circulating tumor DNA or tumor tissue (screened at baseline). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of lymphangitic carcinomatosis involving the lung.
Grade 3 or Grade 4 interstitial lung disease occurring on prior therapy.
Visceral crisis necessitating immediate cytotoxic chemotherapy.
Known hypersensitivity to fulvestrant or related excipients.
Radiotherapy within 30 days preceding Day 1 (except localized palliative radiation/completed ≥7 days ago).
Presence of RB1 mutations or deletions deemed resistant to CDK4/6 inhibition.
Long QTc syndrome or QTc prolongation >480 ms.
Recent venous thromboembolism (pulmonary embolus or deep vein thrombosis) within 6 months unless on chronic therapeutic anticoagulation.
Conditions increasing risk for venous thromboembolic events (severe congestive heart failure, prolonged immobility) making lasofoxifene unsafe.
Concomitant use of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or strong/moderate CYP3A4 inducers.
Significant comorbidities impacting study conduct or safety, including marked malabsorption.
Active systemic bacterial or fungal infection requiring IV antimicrobial therapy.
Confirmed infection with HIV, hepatitis B, or hepatitis C viruses.
Other malignancies diagnosed within the past five years, except adequately treated basal cell or squamous cell skin cancer.
Positive serum pregnancy test (for premenopausal participants).
Inadequate contraception usage (double barrier methods) among sexually active premenopausal women and men.
Breastfeeding.
Documented non‑compliance with medical regimens.
Unwillingness or inability to abide by protocol requirements.
Participation in another investigational drug/device study within the prior 30 days.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Prior anthracycline‑containing regimen; any prior systemic immunotherapeutic agent; any prior chemotherapy or targeted therapy for large B‑cell lymphoma (excluding corticosteroids for symptom control); prior radiotherapy to the mediastinal or pericardial regions; prior solid organ transplantation; prior autologous/allogeneic stem cell transplantation; prior use of corticosteroids exceeding 30 mg/day prednisone equivalents for reasons other than lymphoma symptom control. general_exclusion_criteria:
• History of severe allergic or anaphylactic reaction to humanized or murine monoclonal antibodies or mouse-derived products  
• Grade > 1 peripheral neuropathy or demyelinating Charcot‑Marie‑Tooth disease  
• Significant or extensive cardiovascular disease, including reduced left ventricular ejection fraction <50 %  
• Clinically significant liver disease or abnormal hepatic laboratory values  
• Renal dysfunction meeting predefined lab thresholds (not detailed here)  
• Uncontrolled concurrent illness such as active infection (other than screened viruses), active or latent tuberculosis, chronic hepatitis B/C, HTLV‑1, or HIV infection  
• Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic therapy  
• History of progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy  
• Recent major surgical procedure within four weeks preceding enrollment  
• Pregnancy or breastfeeding (for women of childbearing potential)  
• Inability to comply with study procedures or follow‑up assessments.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Concurrent malignancy less than five years ago (excluding appropriately treated CIS cervix, cutaneous SCC, basal cell carcinoma, localized prostate cancer, DCIS of breast, or Ta/T1 urothelial carcinoma).
Active central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease (stable/asymptomatic treated CNS disease permissible under strict limits).
Recent cardiovascular events (stroke, TIA, myocardial infarction, unstable angina) or NYHA Class III–IV congestive heart failure within six months; QTcF > 480 ms; uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmia.
Active autoimmune diseases requiring systemic therapy within two years (exceptions: hypothyroidism, vitiligo, Graves’, Hashimoto’s thyroiditis, type Ⅰ diabetes, remote asthma/alters).
History of allogeneic hematopoietic stem‑cell or solid‑organ transplantation.
Severe IgE‑mediated or grade 3–4 hypersensitivity reaction to monoclonal antibodies or protein therapeutics.
Grade 3–4 immune‑related adverse event from prior therapy (endocrinopathy manageable with hormonal replacement exempted).
Systemic glucocorticoids ≥10 mg prednisone daily-equivalent (short bursts of low‑dose/topical/inhaled allowable).
Antibiotic therapy longer than seven days within 28 days pre‑treatment.
Live vaccination within four weeks preceding dosing.
Positive SARS‑CoV‑2 PCR or antigen screen at enrollment (eligible after isolation/quarantine once negative).
Active untreated Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection or receipt of anti‑TB medication within past year.
Use of investigational agents targeting CD73 or Adenosine A₂A receptors.
Anti‑cancer therapy administered fewer than five plasma half‑lives or less than four weeks before enrolment (radiation to a solitary field <2 weeks permitted if lesional progression evident).
Major surgical procedure (aside from biopsies/drainages/catheters) or invasive/interventional oncology therapy within four weeks before first dose.
Participation in another interventional clinical trial within four weeks prior to first dose.
Clinically significant comorbidities, psychiatric disorders, geographic constraints, or logistical issues deemed by investigators to jeopardize protocol adherence or patient safety.

Prior receipt of anti‑PD‑1, anti‑PD‑L1, anti‑PD‑L2, or other T‑cell costimulatory/co‑inhibitory agents resulting in immune‑related adverse event leading to discontinuation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast carcinoma (any subtype). Cancer burden allowed: Primary tumour pathological stage pT1‑3 (exclude pT4/inflammatory), nodal status pN0 or pN1 (up to three positive nodes, including micrometastases), no distant/metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Chemotherapy, radiation therapy, biologic therapy, endocrine therapy for the index cancer lasting > 6 weeks, hormonally based contraceptives containing progestins (including progestin‐containing intrauterine devices). Biomarkers required: Estrogen receptor positive (≥ 1 % by IHC) **and**/or progesterone receptor positive (≥ 1 % by IHC); HER2 negative by ASCO/CAP; Oncotype DX recurrence score 21‑25 **or** (score 16‑20 **and** high clinical risk [low grade & tumour > 3 cm or intermediate grade & tumour > 2 cm or high grade & tumour > 1 cm] ) for pN0 tumours; Score ≤ 25 for pN1 tumours; MammaPrint low‑risk/MP1 (required when applicable). Biomarkers excluded: MammaPrint high‑risk/MP2; HER2 positive; Tumour lacking ER and/or PR positivity.
general_exclusion_criteria:
• Evidence of clinically detectable metastatic disease.  
• Pathologic T4 lesions, including inflammatory breast cancer.  
• Previous invasive breast cancer in either breast (except prior DCIS or LCIS).  
• Life expectancy estimated to be less than ten years owing to comorbidities.  
• Laboratory abnormalities: absolute neutrophil count < 1200/mm³, platelet count < 100,000/mm³, hemoglobin < 10 g/dL, total bilirubin > ULN (or > 1.5× ULN with benign hyperbilirubinemia), ALT/AST > 3 times ULN, glomerular filtration rate < 30 ml/min/1.73 m².  
• Clinically significant cardiovascular disease or NYHA functional Class III‑IV (cardiac risk unacceptable).  
• Non‑epithelial breast malignancies (sarcoma, lymphoma, etc.).  
• Receipt of any radiation therapy, cytotoxic chemotherapy, or immunotherapy directed at the current breast cancer prior to enrolment (aside from allowable brief endocrine exposure).  
• Current pregnancy or breastfeeding; inadequate birth control compliance.  
• Active untreated chronic infections (hepatitis B with detectable viremia, hepatitis C uncurbed, uncontrolled HIV).

general_exclusion_criteria: Known central nervous system involvement by lymphoma; clinically significant cardiovascular disease; history of stroke or intracranial hemorrhage within six months before first dose of study drug.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: non‑squamous non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: non‑squamous (e.g., adenocarcinoma, bronchioalveolar carcinoma, undefined non‑square cell NSCLC). Cancer burden allowed: advanced or metastatic disease (Stage IIIb/IV) and measurable disease according to RECIST v1.1. Prior treatment required: no prior systemic anticancer therapy for advanced/metastatic disease; prior adjuvant or neoadjuvant therapy permissible if completed ≥6 months before diagnosis of advanced/metastatic disease and did not result in disease progression within 6 months afterwards. Prior treatment excluded: any prior systemic therapy for advanced/metastatic NSCLC; any prior exposure to topoisomerase I‑containing agents (including antibody‑drug conjugates); any prior TROP2‑directed therapy; any prior anti‑PD‑1, anti‑PD‑L1, anti‑PD‑L2, anti‑CTLA‑4, anti‑OX40, anti‑CD137 or other immune‑checkpoint/immunotherapy agents. Biomarkers required: PD‑L1 tumour proportion score < 50% by centralized 22C3 PharmDX assay and availability of tumour tissue for TROP2 protein expression assessment and confirmed absence of actionable genomic alterations (e.g., lacking EGFR sensitising mutations, ALK, ROS1, RET, NTRK, MET exon 14 skipping, BRAF V600E). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of symptomatic spinal cord compression; active untreated central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease (treated/stable brain metastases allowable under strict criteria); uncontrolled cardiovascular disease—including QTcF > 470 ms, myocardial infarction within 6 months, serious arrhythmia, uncontrolled angina, left ventricular ejection fraction < 50%, New York Heart Association class II‑IV heart failure unless recovered to class I with LVEF ≥ 50%; uncontrolled hypertension (SBP > 180 mmHg or DBP > 110 mmHg); severe concurrent pulmonary illness such as pulmonary embolism within 3 months, severe asthma, severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, restrictive lung disease, substantial pleural effusion, extensive autoimmune or connective‑tissue disease involving lungs, or prior complete pneumonectomy.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of non‑compliance that interferes with study participation.
Uncontrolled psychiatric illness.

Significant hepatic dysfunction (alanine aminotransferase or aspartate aminotransferase >3×upper limit of normal).

Performance status less than Karnofsky 70%.

general_exclusion_criteria:

Presence of brainstem or spinal cord tumour involvement as primary site, multicentric spread, or extensive infiltration.
Evidence of diffuse leptomeningeal disease on magnetic resonance imaging.
Uncontrolled seizure activity or other serious neurologic/cognitive impairment interfering with study procedures.
Concurrent use of corticosteroids exceeding a stable/decreasing regimen established at least 14 days before randomisation.
Active second malignant neoplasm unless cured and disease free for at least 3 years (excluding adequately treated basal/squamous non‑melanoma skin cancer or carcinoma in situ).
Any medical condition judged by the investigator to place the participant at undue risk or compromise data integrity.

Biomarkers required: Confirmed IDH1 mutation (R132H, R132C, R132G, R132S, or R132L) using IHC, PCR, or next‑generation sequencing; determination of CDKN2A/B status; and at least one of the following: absence of 1p/19q co‑deletion, presence of an ATRX loss‑of‑function mutation, or loss of normal ATRX expression.
