general_exclusion_criteria: Prior therapy with sacituzumab govitecan, irinotecan, or any topoisomerase I‑containing antibody‑drug conjugates at any time for early‑stage disease.; Receipt of adjuvant chemotherapy (all chemotherapy must have been administered in the preoperative/neoadjuvant setting).; Prior hypersensitivity reaction to atezolizumab or to the excipients of atezolizumab or sacituzumab govitecan.; Any clinical or radiographic evidence of metastatic disease.; Presence of residual ductal carcinoma in situ or lobular carcinoma in situ alone without invasive cancer, or pathological staging of pT0N0(i+) or pT0N1(mic) residual disease.; Enrollment concurrently on another investigational therapy trial.; Unresolved treatment‑related toxicity greater than Grade 1 (excluding alopecia and peripheral neuropathy).; Major surgical procedures (other than diagnostic biopsies) within four weeks preceding study treatment or anticipated need for such procedures during the study.; Systemic antibacterial therapy within two weeks prior to starting study treatment (prophylactic antibiotics are exempt).; Ongoing active infection requiring systemic therapy, active tuberculosis, serious liver disease such as cirrhosis, active bleeding diathesis, uncontrolled type I or type II diabetes mellitus, uncontrolled hypertension, hypercholesterolaemia, hypertriglyceridaemia, or hypercalcaemia graded ≥2.; Significant cardiovascular disease including New York Heart Association class III or IV congestive heart failure, myocardial infarction occurring fewer than six months ago, unstable angina, serious uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmia, or any other clinically meaningful cardiac disorder.; History of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, organizing pneumonia (e.g., bronchiolitis obliterans), drug‑induced pneumonitis, or active pneumonitis on baseline CT (radiation‑associated fibrosis is allowable).; Active (acute or chronic) autoimmune disease of any kind except adequately controlled hypothyroidism, celiac disease, psoriasis, eczema, lichen simplectic chronicus, or vitiligo.; Gastrointestinal disorders that markedly impair drug absorption (e.g., inflammatory bowel disease flare, uncontrolled nausea/vomiting/diarrhoea grade > 2, malabsorptive syndromes, extensive small‑bowel resections.).; Congenital prolonged QT syndrome or screened QTc > 480 ms calculated by Fridericia’s method.; Documented infection with Human Immunodeficiency Virus, Hepatitis B surface antigen positivity, or Hepatitis C viral RNA positivity.; Previous invasive breast cancer in either breast.; Other prior malignant diagnoses are permissible only if the individual has remained disease‑free for at least five years, excluding thyroid cancer confined to the gland without nodal involvement.; Recent exposure (within four weeks or five elimination half‑lives) to potent UGT1A1 enzyme inhibitors or inducers.; Administration of a live, attenuated vaccine within four weeks prior to initiating study treatment or planned administration thereafter.; Known allergic reactions to any study drug components, including CHO‑cell derived proteins or recombinant antibodies.; Use of systemic immunosuppressive medications within two weeks before study commencement (exceptions apply for brief low‑dose courses, inhaled steroids, or physiologic steroid replacement).; Current pregnancy or breastfeeding; participants of reproductive potential must employ approved non‑hormonal contraception throughout treatment and for six months afterward.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Active uncontrolled or symptomatic central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease.  
Concurrent malignancy or malignancy diagnosed within five years preceding enrollment (with exceptions noted).  
Known allergy or hypersensitivity to investigational product components.  
Recent palliative radiotherapy (within two weeks for limited-field or four weeks for extensive-field irradiation).  
Major surgical procedure or significant trauma within fourteen days before starting study treatment.  
Clinically significant cardiovascular disease—including recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, NYHA class II–IV congestive heart failure, left ventricular ejection fraction < 55%, uncontrolled hypertension, symptomatic arrhythmias requiring intervention, or prohibited QTc prolongation.  
Severe pulmonary comorbidity (e.g., recent pulmonary embolism, severe COPD, restrictive lung disease, interstitial lung disease, ongoing pneumonia, or prior pneumonectomy).  
History of interstitial lung disease / pneumonitis requiring steroid therapy, existing ILD/pneumonitis, or unresolved suspicious findings on imaging.  
Pregnancy, lactation, or inadequate contraception according to protocol specifications.  
Current active hepatitis B or C infection, or known HIV infection.  
Other active uncontrolled infections at enrollment.  
Receipt of live or attenuated vaccination within thirty days before first dose.  
Uncontrolled seizures, central nervous system disorders, or serious unmanaged psychiatric illness impairing adherence.  
Any additional severe or uncontrolled medical condition deemed unsuitable by investigators.  
Substance abuse or dependence posing a safety concern.  
Incapacity or refusal to comply with study assessments and follow‑up procedures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥16 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: Colon adenocarcinoma. Histology allowed: Adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Advanced or metastatic disease, including primary brain tumor; brain metastasis permissible if lesion ≤4 cm maximal dimension. Prior treatment required: Documented disease progression after most recent therapy and receipt of standard prior approved colorectal cancer therapy. Prior treatment excluded: Ongoing systematic anti‑cancer therapy at study initiation. Biomarkers required: BRAF V600 mutation **or** non‑V600 class II or class III BRAF alteration demonstrated in tumor tissue and/or circulating tumor DNA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History or current evidence of retinal vein obstruction.
Neuromuscular disorder associated with elevated creatine kinase level.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of central nervous system metastases or symptomatic spinal cord compression requiring steroids, known intracranial haemorrhage or bleeding disorders, visceral crisis, lymphangitic spread, leptomeningeal carcinomatosis, active/intercurrent interstitial lung disease or recent radiation pneumonitis, resting QTcF greater than 470 ms or congenital long QT/Torsade de Pointes syndromes, uncontrolled cardiovascular disease including hypertension, significant concurrent illnesses (e.g., hepatic impairment beyond protocol thresholds, renal insufficiency, grade ≥2 diarrhoea), other malignant neoplasms except appropriately treated basal/squamous skin cancer, cured cervical intra‑epithelial carcinoma, prostate cancer, superficial bladder cancer, or other solid tumors disease-free ≥3 years, active untreated viral/bacterial/fungal infections (HIV/AIDS, HBV, HCV, TB, SARS‑CoV‑2), concomitant prohibited medications/herbal products that cannot be stopped, anticipated major surgery within 28 days, inability to tolerate oral meds or absorb drugs owing to GI pathology, prior extensive (>25%) bone‑marrow irradiation, prior high‑dose chemotherapy with stem‑cell rescue, participation in another investigational drug study within 4 weeks, known allergy to study agents, and inadequate performance status (ECOG > 1).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: acute myeloid leukaemia. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: high risk for post‑transplant relapse defined as any of the following – de novo AML diagnosed at age ≥60 (excludes core‑binding factor AML); de novo AML in first complete remission with measurable residual disease positive by flow cytometry; secondary AML; AML transplanted in second or further complete remission; therapy‑related AML; AML with monosomy 7; AML harbouring TP53 mutation (TP53‑mutated disease eligible regardless of measurable residual disease). Prior treatment required: planned reduced‑intensity conditioning HLA‑matched related or related haploidentical allogeneic stem cell transplant with post‑cyclophosphamide graft‑vs‑host disease prophylaxis. Prior treatment excluded: planned post‑transplant maintenance with FLT3‑ITD, IDH or BCR‑ABL inhibitors; prior allogeneic stem cell transplant performed >7 days before NK cell infusion; prior solid organ transplantation; use of sirolimus for GVHD prophylaxis within the first 100 days post‑transplant; systemic glucocorticoids on the day of NK cell infusion; donor‑specific antibodies with mean fluorescence intensity > 1000 without successful desensitisation; grade ≥2 haemolytic anaemia; documented hypersensitivity to interleukin‑2 or cellular products. Biomarkers required: TP53 mutation permits enrollment independent of measurable residual disease (optional). Biomarkers excluded: FLT3‑ITD mutation, IDH mutation, BCR‑ABL fusion.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of uncontrolled concurrent illness such as active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, serious cardiac arrhythmia, or psychiatric/social disorders impairing compliance. Pregnancy or breastfeeding. Positive HIV serostatus. Active and uncontrolled hepatitis B or C infection. Prior allogeneic stem cell transplant outside the study protocol (more than 7 days before NK infusion). Prior solid organ transplantation. Known hypersensitivity to interleukin‑2, cytokines, or cellular therapeutic products. Extramedullary leukaemic involvement of central nervous system or testes. Use of systemic steroids exceeding 10 mg prednisolone equivalents on the day of NK cell infusion. Significant organ dysfunction violating predefined lab thresholds (bilirubin ≤ 1.5× ULN, AST/ALT ≤ 3× ULN, serum creatinine ≤ 2.0 mg/dL, left ventricular ejection fraction > 40 %). Performance status worse than ECOG 2/Karnofsky < 60 %.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: myelodysplastic syndrome. Histology allowed: NA. Prior treatment required: planned reduced‑intensity conditioning HLA‑matched related or related haploidentical allogeneic stem cell transplant with post‑cyclophosphamide GVHD prophylaxis. Prior treatment excluded: same exclusions as Space 1 (FLT3‑ITD, IDH or BCR‑ABL inhibitor maintenance; prior allogeneic transplant >7 days before NK infusion; prior solid organ transplant; sirolimus use for GVHD prophylaxis; systemic glucocorticoids on day of NK infusion; donor‑specific antibodies MFI > 1000 un‑desensitized; grade ≥2 haemolytic anaemia; hypersensitivity to IL‑2 or cellular products). Biomarkers required: TP53 mutation (allows enrollment regardless of disease bulk). Biomarkers excluded: FLT3‑ITD mutation, IDH mutation, BCR‑ABL fusion.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of uncontrolled concurrent illness such as active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, serious cardiac arrhythmia, or psychiatric/social disorders impairing compliance. Prior allogeneic stem cell transplant outside the study protocol (more than 7 days before NK infusion). Prior solid organ transplantation. Known hypersensitivity to interleukin‑2, cytokines, or cellular therapeutic products. Extramedullary leukaemic involvement of central nervous system or testes. Use of systemic steroids exceeding 10 mg prednisolone equivalents on the day of NK cell infusion. Significant organ dysfunction violating predefined lab thresholds (bilirubin ≤ 1.5× ULN, AST/ALT ≤ 3× ULN, serum creatinine ≤ 2.0 mg/dL, left ventricular ejection fraction > 40 %). Performance status worse than ECOG 2/Karnofsky < 60 %.

general_exclusion_criteria: Patients who are currently pregnant or nursing. History of significant pulmonary disorders such as pneumonitis. Clinically meaningful cardiac insufficiency or congestive heart failure. Significant hepatic impairment or bilirubin elevations outside predefined limits. Renal dysfunction exceeding institutional thresholds. Active untreated central nervous system malignancy or uncontrolled symptomatic brain metastasis. Known human immunodeficiency virus infection or chronic viral hepatitis B/C infection. Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status greater than 2 (for adults) or Lansky score less than 50 % (for children ≤16 years). Recent major surgery within four weeks preceding enrollment. Any ongoing severe uncontrolled systemic infection or inflammatory disorder. Conditions, concomitant medicines, or psychosocial circumstances judged by the investigators to preclude adherence to study assessments or jeopardize participant safety.

Prior treatment excluded: prior anti‑programmed death‑1 (anti‑PD‑1), anti‑programmed death ligand‑1 (anti‑PD‑L1), anti‑programmed death ligand‑2 (anti‑PD‑L2) antibodies, or agents targeting other costimulatory/inhibitory receptors such as cytotoxic T‑lymphocyte‑associated protein‑4 (CTLA‑4), OX40, CD137. Biomarkers required: CD30 >1% by immunohistochemical staining. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of non‑infectious pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease requiring steroids or current pneumonitis/ILD.; Active uncontrolled infection requiring systemic antimicrobial therapy.; Active myocardial inflammation (myocarditis) or New York Heart Association Class III‑IV congestive heart failure.; Untreated or symptomatic central nervous system involvement (active CNS metastases or leptomeningeal disease).; Significant hepatic impairment or underlying cirrhosis/alcoholic liver disease.; Moderate-to-severe renal impairment compromising safety evaluation.; Documented human immunodeficiency virus infection.; Active hepatitis B surface antigen positivity or detectable hepatitis C viral RNA.; Current or recent (within 30 days) administration of live or live‑attenuated vaccinations.; Serious psychiatric disorder or substance misuse impairing adherence.; Prior allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation within five years or active graft‑versus‑host disease.; Preexisting peripheral sensory neuropathy grade 2 or higher.; Pregnancy or nursing mothers.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: metastatic colorectal cancer. Histology allowed: adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: limited liver disease (fewer than seven lesions total and greatest diameter <3 cm) and at least one extracranial measurable target lesion per RECIST 1.1. Prior treatment required: fluoropyrimidine‐based chemotherapy **and** oxaliplatin‐based chemotherapy **and** irinotecan‐based chemotherapy **and** anti‑vascular endothelial growth factor therapy **and** anti‑epidermal growth factor receptor therapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: guanylate cyclase positive by immunohistochemistry (assessment performed at screening) **and** RAS wild‑type. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Subjects with tumor lesions located in areas prone to perforation of an organ or structure (e.g., gastrointestinal tract, urinary bladder, major vessels).
Active infection requiring systemic antimicrobial therapy or accompanied by fever (>38.1°C) within 7 days before leukapheresis, or unresolved fever at the time of investigational product infusion.
Pregnancy or current lactation.

Prior treatment required: any prior systemic therapy for advanced disease (may be treatment‐experienced or unable to tolerate existing options). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Women who are pregnant or breastfeeding.
Women of childbearing potential not using effective contraceptive methods.
Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic therapy within the past 2 years.
Systemic corticosteroid or other immunosuppressive medication use within 14 days prior to study drug administration.
History of non‑infectious pneumonitis requiring steroids, current pneumonitis, interstitial lung disease, or COVID‑19 pneumonia with residual fibrosis.
Untreated or progressive brain or central nervous system metastases.
Impaired left ventricular systolic function or clinically significant cardiovascular disease.
Recent (< 6 months) venous thromboembolism (deep‑vein thrombosis or pulmonary embolism).
Active gastric perforation, pyloric obstruction, complete biliary obstruction, bowel obstruction, or malignant ascites/pleural effusions requiring therapeutic intervention.
Prior claudin‑18.2‑directed therapy (zolbetuximab) prohibited except where expressly permitted in designated cohort.
Prior CD47‑ or SIRPA‑targeted agents.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of solid organ transplant.
Clinically significant (active) cardiovascular disease.
Known history of liver cirrhosis.
Ongoing Grade >1 peripheral neuropathy.
Demyelinating form of Charcot‑Marie‑Tooth disease.
Diagnosed with Down syndrome.
Ongoing graft‑versus‑host disease of any grade or receiving systemic GVHD treatment or prophylaxis.
History of human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) infection.
Contraindiction or hypersensitivity to any of the study intervention components.
Additional malignancy that is progressing or has required active treatment within the past 1 year.
Active infection requiring systemic therapy.
Known history of hepatitis B or known active hepatitis C virus infection.
Participants who have not adequately recovered from major surgery or have ongoing surgical complications.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Glioblastoma multiforme. Histology allowed: World Health Organization grade 4 astrocytic tumor (glioblastoma). Cancer burden allowed: Radiographic documentation of recurrent or progressive disease according to RANO criteria. Prior treatment required: Initial cranial radiation therapy completed ≥6 months before Cycle 1 Day 1. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of severe brain injury or stroke.
Ineligible for sequential MRI evaluation.
History of epileptic disorder or any seizure unrelated to tumor.
Concurrent use of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or inducers within two weeks of study drug intake.
Concomitant therapy with other seizurogenic medicines.
Past medical history of interstitial lung disease, drug‑induced ILD, or radiation pneumonitis requiring steroids.
Severe or uncontrolled chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
Previous exposure to known pneumotoxic agents leading to pulmonary toxicity.
Myopathy or elevated creatine kinase >5 times upper limit of normal on two screenings.
Cardiac dysfunction defined as myocardial infarction ≤six months prior, NYHA class II–IV heart failure, unstable angina, or uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmias.
Active severe pulmonary infection, including tuberculosis or uncontrolled hepatitis B/C (as applied in regional cohorts).
Unresolved toxicities greater than NCI CTCAE grade 1 (aside from alopecia) at study start; persistent grade 2 toxicities may preclude participation.
Prior receipt of an ATMi together with radiation therapy.
Diagnosis of diabetes mellitus (type I, type II, or steroid‑induced) (pertinent to food‑effect substudy).
Ongoing systemic glucocorticoid therapy (pertinent to food‑effect substudy).

Cancer burden allowed: Radiographic documentation of recurrent or progressive disease according to RANO criteria. Prior treatment required: Initial cranial radiation therapy completed ≥6 months before Cycle 1 Day 1. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Unmethylated O⁶‑methylguanine‑DNA methyltransferase (MGMT) promoter as demonstrated by PCR or pyrosequencing. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of severe brain injury or stroke.
Ineligible for sequential MRI evaluation.
History of epileptic disorder or any seizure unrelated to tumor.
Concurrent use of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or inducers within two weeks of study drug intake.
Concomitant therapy with other seizurogenic medicines.
Past medical history of interstitial lung disease, drug‑induced ILD, or radiation pneumonitis requiring steroids.
Severe or uncontrolled chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
Previous exposure to known pneumotoxic agents leading to pulmonary toxicity.
Myopathy or elevated creatine kinase >5 times upper limit of normal on two screenings.
Cardiac dysfunction defined as myocardial infarction ≤six months prior, NYHA class II–IV heart failure, unstable angina, or uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmias.
Active severe pulmonary infection, including tuberculosis or uncontrolled hepatitis B/C (as applied in regional cohorts).
Unresolved toxicities greater than NCI CTCAE grade 1 (aside from alopecia) at study start; persistent grade 2 toxicities may preclude participation.
Prior receipt of an ATMi together with radiation therapy.
Diagnosis of diabetes mellitus (type I, type II, or steroid‑induced) (pertinent to food‑effect substudy).
Ongoing systemic glucocorticoid therapy (pertinent to food‑effect substudy).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: multiple myeloma. Histology allowed: multiple myeloma. Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory disease with measurable disease (any of the following: M‑protein ≥0.5 g/dL (for IgA subtypes), urine M‑protein ≥200 mg/24 hrs, serum free light‑chain difference >100 mg/L, biopsy‑proved plasmacytoma, or imaging‑based measurable lesion for oligosecretory disease). Prior treatment required: receipt of at least one proteasome inhibitor **and** at least one immunomodulatory drug **and** at least one anti‑CD38 monoclonal antibody **and** 1 to 4 prior lines of systemic therapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
* Presence of plasma cell leukemia (≥5% circulating plasma cells)
* Waldenström’s macroglobulinemia
* POEMS syndrome
* Primary amyloid light‑chain amyloidosis
* Allergy or known hypersensitivity to any trial therapy or its excipients
* Complete spinal cord compression or central nervous system involvement
* Active or historical autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression within two years (exceptions noted for Type 1 diabetes, vitiligo, psoriasis, thyroid diseases not requiring immunosuppression)
* Receipt of a live, attenuated vaccine within four weeks preceding dosing
* Ongoing graft‑versus‑host disease after allogeneic stem‑cell or solid‑organ transplant requiring >20 mg/day prednisone-equivalent
* Active infections necessitating antimicrobial therapy
* Any concomitant condition, therapy, or lab abnormality judged to potentially confound trial outcomes or impede completion
* Uncontrolled psychiatric or substance‑use disorder affecting compliance
* Legal incapacity or lack of sufficient legal authority to consent
* Pregnancy or breastfeeding.

Age range allowed: >=18. Sex allowed: female. Cancer type allowed: ovarian cancer. Histology allowed: high‑grade serous. Cancer burden allowed: platinum‑resistant disease with measurable lesions per RECIST version 1.1. Prior treatment required: 1–4 prior systemic therapy lines; prior bevacizumab‐containing regimen; prior PARP inhibitor if documented deleterious BRCA1/2 mutation or homologous recombination deficiency; prior mirvetuximab if deemed appropriate per standard of care. Prior treatment excluded: prior therapy with ZN‑c3 or any other WEE1 inhibitor, ATR inhibitor, PKMYT1 inhibitor, or CHK1/2 inhibitor. Biomarkers required: cyclin E1 protein positive by immunohistochemistry performed centrally. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Major surgery within 28 days preceding first dose; hospitalisation within 14 days; any chemotherapy or targeted anticancer therapy within 21 days (or five half‑lives whichever is shorter); radiation therapy within 21 days; autologous or allogenic stem‑cell transplantation within 3 months; concurrent investigational drug use less than 28 days ago; inability to discontinue strong/moderate CYP3A/P‑gp inhibitors or inducer substances at least 14 days prior; untreated or symptomatic brain metastases or leptomeningeal disease (asymptomatic brain metastases allowable); New York Heart Association class II–IV congestive heart failure; acute kidney injury needing intervention or IV fluids within 14 days, indwelling urinary catheters or nephrostomy; significant gastrointestinal disorders impairing oral intake or causing malabsorption; active uncontrolled infection; recent bowel obstruction signs or surgeries; unresolved adverse events greater than grade 1 attributable to prior therapy (except low‑grade neuropathy, alopecia, pigment changes); pregnancy, breastfeeding, or women of child‑bearing potential with a positive pregnancy test; additional malignant neoplasm diagnosed within the past 2 years (exceptions apply for adequately treated cervical intraepithelial neoplasia, non‑melanoma skin cancer, stage Ⅰ uterine cancer, or similarly curable diseases); known immunodeficiency or HIV infection on antiretroviral therapy; active hepatitis B or C infection; receipt of wide‑field radiotherapy involving ≥20 % of bone marrow; investigators’ determination deeming participant unsuitable.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: T cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia. Histology allowed: T‑cell lineage acute lymphoblastic leukemia (blasts expressing TdT and CD3/CD7). Cancer burden allowed: First or subsequent hematologic complete remission or CRi with persistent or recurrent measurable residual disease ≥10⁻⁴ by flow cytometry (centrally verified). Prior treatment required: At least two blocks of intensive chemotherapy having achieved hematologic CR or CRi; documentation of MRD ≥10⁻⁴. Prior treatment excluded: Concurrent systemic glucocorticoids greater than 10 mg prednisone equivalents per day; ongoing graft‑versus‑host disease requiring immunosuppressive therapy. Biomarkers required: Measurable residual disease ≥10⁻⁴ by flow cytometry (central laboratory). Biomarkers excluded: NA.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of pneumonitis requiring steroids or current pneumonitis  
Active central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease or involvement of the brainstem  
Uncontrolled autoimmune disease needing systemic therapy within the past 2 years (except hormone replacement)  
Systemic steroid or other immunosuppressive therapy within 7 days prior to randomisation  
Recent receipt (within protocol-specified washouts) of investigational agents, chemotherapies, or radiation (excluded here as washout periods omitted)  
Known active infectious diseases including HIV, chronic hepatitis B or C, or active tuberculosis  
Pregnancy or lactation, or plans to become pregnant or sire a child within the study timeframe + 120 days post‑treatment  
Significant organ dysfunction (renal, hepatic, cardiac) compromising safety assessments  
Severe allergic reaction (Grade ≥ 3) to pembrolizumab or its components  
Any concurrent serious medical illness or psychosocial circumstance precluding compliance with study procedures.

Uncontrolled intercurrent serious medical conditions including acute infections.

Peripheral neuropathy graded ≥2 (CTCAE v5.0).
Unable or unwilling to undergo computed tomography or magnetic resonance imaging for spleen measurement.
Pregnancy or lactation.
Requirement for concomitant administration of a strong cytochrome P450 3A4 inhibiting medication.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: T‑cell large granular lymphocytic leukemia. Histology allowed: CD3+CD8+ greater than 650 cells per cubic millimeter **or** CD3+CD8+CD57+ greater than 500 cells per cubic millimeter together with evidence of a clonal T‑cell receptor rearrangement (assessment at screening). Cancer burden allowed: Peripheral blood findings fulfilling at least one of the following — hemoglobin less than 10 g/dL or transfusion‑dependent anemia; absolute neutrophil count less than 500 cells per cubic millimeter (**or**) absolute neutrophil count between 500–1499 cells per cubic millimeter with recurrent infections; platelet count equal to or greater than 50 × 10⁹/L. Prior treatment required: Patient may be treatment‑naïve **or** must have demonstrated lack of response or progression after at least one prior line of therapy directed against T‑large granular lymphocytic leukemia. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Flow‑cytometric detection of CD3 and CD8 (with optional CD57) along with demonstration of a clonal T‑cell receptor rearrangement. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Absolute neutrophil count less than 100 cells per cubic millimeter.
Active infection requiring ongoing antimicrobials.
Positive testing for Human Immunodeficiency Virus, Hepatitis B surface antigen, or Hepatitis C antibodies.
Concurrent systemic immunosuppressive therapy exceeding allowable doses (prednisone >20 mg daily without planned taper).
Presence of an additional active malignant neoplasm (except adequately observed early‑stage skin carcinoma or prostate cancer not receiving current therapy).
Pregnancy or currently nursing.
Unstable angina or myocardial infarction occurring within the preceding two months.
Acute worsening of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease or interstitial lung disease.
Cirrhotic liver disease.

Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of infectious or non‑infectious pneumonitis requiring steroids or current pneumonitis.
Active autoimmune disease that has required systemic immunosuppressive therapy in the preceding 2 years (excluding physiological hormone replacement).
Receipt of a live vaccine within 30 days prior to first dose of study drug.
Documented active hepatitis B infection (positive HBsAg) or active hepatitis C infection (detectable HCV RNA).
Confirmed active Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection.
Poorly controlled diabetes mellitus with glycated haemoglobin greater than 8 %.
Unable to ingest oral capsule formulation or significant gastrointestinal malfunction impairing absorption (e.g., malabsorptive syndromes, extensive resections, complete obstruction).
Concomitant use of moderate or strong CYP3A4/5 enzyme inhibitors or inducers that cannot be safely withdrawn.
Use of enzyme‑inducing antiseizure medicines not substituted with non‑enzyme‑inducing alternatives.
Cardiac compromise meeting criteria for New York Heart Association class III/IV or left ventricular ejection fraction markedly reduced.
Severe hepatic or renal dysfunction beyond laboratory thresholds stipulated in the protocol.
Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status greater than 2.
Contraindications to magnetic resonance imaging.
Isolated intra‑ocular primary CNS lymphoma without concurrent brain or spinal canal disease.
Enrollment in another interventional investigative study concurrently.
Presence of large pleural effusions, clinically evident ascites, or generalized oedema.
Diagnosed psychotic illness or substance dependence precluding adherence to study procedures.
Previous allogeneic stem cell transplantation.
Pregnancy, nursing motherhood, or intention to become pregnant or sire a child during the study period extending to 120 days after the last administered dose.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Invasive breast cancer. Histology allowed: Estrogen Receptor positive and Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 negative. Cancer burden allowed: Early‐stage (American Joint Committee on Cancer stage II‑III) disease with no evidence of locally recurrent or distant metastatic disease and demonstrable disseminated tumor cells in bone marrow by central laboratory immunohistochemistry. Prior treatment required: Completion of definitive surgery, completion of prescribed adjuvant chemotherapy and radiation therapy, and continuous adjuvant endocrine therapy at enrollment (must be on an aromatase inhibitor; tamoxifen permissible only if changed to an aromatase inhibitor ≥21 days before initiating a hydroxychloroquine‑containing regimen). Prior treatment excluded: Enrollment in another interventional clinical trial; concurrent use of tamoxifen with hydroxychloroquine; investigational anticancer agents administered contemporaneously with study medication. Biomarkers required: Presence of detectable disseminated tumor cells in bone marrow (assessed centrally by immunohistochemistry). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Patients with a history of another prior invasive breast cancer occurring ≤5 years before enrollment. Patients with prior ductal carcinoma in situ diagnosed ≤5 years before enrollment (excluded). Patients with any other prior invasive malignancy that has recurred or whose disease free interval is <5 years. Chronic high‑dose systemic steroid usage exceeding prednisolone equivalent >10 mg/day or comparable dosing of other glucocorticoids, or any other systemic immunosuppressive agent. Baseline electrocardiogram showing corrected QT interval greater than 480 milliseconds. Clinically significant unmanaged cardiovascular disease—including uncontrolled arrhythmias (excluding well‑controlled atrial fibrillation), acute coronary syndrome, myocardial infarction, unstable angina, coronary intervention or stent placement within 6 months, or New York Heart Association class III‑IV congestive heart failure. History of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease, diffusing capacity of carbon monoxide ≤50 percent of predicted, or resting arterial oxygen saturation ≤88 percent on ambient air. Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus. Severe or uncontrolled infectious processes. Significant liver disease such as cirrhosis, chronic active hepatitis, or persistently abnormal hepatitis. Positive human immunodeficiency virus infection accompanied by combination antiretroviral therapy. Gastrointestinal conditions markedly impairing oral drug absorption (for example, extensive inflammatory bowel disease, untreated severe nausea/vomiting/diarrhea, malabsorptive syndromes, or substantial small intestine resections). Active clinically significant bleeding diatheses or requirement for therapeutic anticoagulation. Retinal diseases including retinopathy or retinal venous occlusive disease. Pregnancy, lactation, or inability to employ adequate contraceptive measures among individuals of reproductive potential. Poor functional status preventing compliance with study assessments.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: squamous. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic stage IV disease. Prior treatment required: No prior systemic therapy for metastatic disease. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior systemic therapy for metastatic non‑small cell lung cancer. Biomarkers required: PD‑L1 tumour proportion score greater than 50 % (assessed by 22C3 IHC). Biomarkers excluded: Presence of actionable genomic alterations (e.g., EGFR sensitising mutation, ALK rearrangement, ROS1 rearrangement, BRAF V600E mutation, MET exon 14 skipping, NTRK fusion, RET fusion).
general_exclusion_criteria: History of small cell lung carcinoma pathology. Receipt of any prior therapy for non‑small cell lung cancer in the metastatic setting. Enrollment in another concurrent therapeutic clinical trial. Symptomatic central nervous system metastases, CNS lesions ≥1.5 cm, recent (<7 days) cranial radiotherapy, anticipated early-cycle CNS irradiation, or leptomeningeal disease. Prior malignant neoplasm except when curatively treated with no evidence of disease for ≥3 years. Active autoimmune disorder or pulmonary disease necessitating systemic therapy. Peripheral neuropathy graded ≥2. Serious infection occurring within 4 weeks preceding randomization. Recent major surgery or significant traumatic injury within 4 weeks. Past episode of non‑infectious pneumonia requiring systemic glucocorticoids or existing interstitial lung disease.

general_exclusion_criteria: history of allergic reaction to tumor‑necrosis factor α, lomustine, excipients, or intravenous biologics; absolute neutrophil count < 1.5×10⁹/L; platelet count < 100×10⁹/L; hemoglobin < 9.0 g/dL; creatinine clearance < 60 mL/min or serum creatinine > 1.5 ×ULN; ALT, AST, alkaline phosphatase ≥ 2.5 ×ULN or total bilirubin ≥ 2.0 ×ULN; international normalized ratio > 1.5 ×ULN; left ventricular ejection fraction < 55%; symptomatic congestive heart failure NYHA class II–III or higher; uncontrolled hypertension; serious cardiac arrhythmia or pacemaker dependence; ischemic peripheral artery disease grade IIb–IV; recent major traumatic injury or major surgery within 4 weeks; active or past tuberculosis; ongoing moderate/high-dose steroids (> 4 mg/day dexamethasone equivalents) or other immunosuppression; active untreated infection or sepsis; HIV, Hepatitis B or C positivity (with exception noted for resolved HBV/HCV); pregnant or breastfeeding women; inability to use adequate contraceptive measures; concurrent anticancer therapy apart from study drugs; recent use (within 7 days) of growth factors or immune modulators; recent live vaccinations (within 4 weeks) or planned live vaccines; anticoagulation with P2Y12 inhibitors or vitamin K antagonists; severe neuropsychiatric illness (major depression, bipolar I/II, OCD, schizophrenia, suicidality, homicide ideation); anxiety ≥ CTCAE grade 3; severe diabetic retinopathy; serious non‑healing wounds, ulcers, or fractures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Solid tumors (any origin). Histology allowed: NA. general_exclusion_criteria:
History of solid organ or allogeneic hematopoietic cell transplantation.
Symptomatic untreated brain metastases or unrepaired CNS involvement (unstable neurological status, steroid >10 mg/day prednisolone).
Clinically significant cardiovascular disease—including myocardial infarction or stroke within three months (six months for prostate‑cancer cohorts), unstable angina within three months (six months for prostate‑cancer cohorts), congestive heart failure/New York Heart Association class III‑IV, serious ventricular arrhythmias, or uncontrolled hypertension despite dual therapy.
Significant prolonged QT interval on screening electrocardiogram (QTcF > 480 ms for most cohorts; ≥450 ms for selected cohorts).
Major surgery within four weeks preceding study drug initiation (aside from minor procedures).
Severe gastrointestinal disorders impairing oral drug absorption (ulcerative colitis, malabsorption syndromes, refractory nausea/vomiting, extensive resections, etc.).
Active uncontrolled infection requiring IV antimicrobials within fourteen days of dosing.
Past or current pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.
Second primary malignancy deemed active or inadequately treated (exceptions noted for localized skin cancers, in‑situ cervix, superficial bladder, early-stage cured cancers, or prostate cancer in remission).
Known active or chronic HIV, hepatitis B, or hepatitis C infection.
Clinical signs of active or symptomatic viral hepatitis or chronic liver disease.
Unstable or severely uncontrolled comorbid medical or psychiatric conditions posing excess risk.
Pregnancy, lactation, or intention to become pregnant/father a child during the study and for 183 days thereafter.

Biomarkers required: estrogen receptor positive or progesterone receptor positive and HER2 negative (assessed by local pathology). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of inflammatory breast cancer (T4d); severe cardiac disease including congestive heart failure NYHA class III or higher, recent myocardial infarction, or symptomatic coronary artery disease; severe pulmonary disease or ill­ness; uncontrolled infections; interstitial lung disease or severe dyspnea at rest requiring supplemental oxygen; renal insufficiency with estimated GFR < 30 mL/min; history of extensive gastrectomy or small‑bowel resection, Crohn’s disease, ulcerative colitis, or baseline grade 2 or higher diarrhea; active systemic bacterial infection requiring IV antibiotics, active fungal infection, or active viral infection such as HIV positivity or hepatitis B or C; personal history of syncope of cardiovascular etiology, pathologic ventricular arrhythmia, or sudden cardiac arrest.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Multiple Myeloma. Histology allowed: Plasmacytic malignant neoplasm (multiple myeloma). Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory disease after at least three prior lines of therapy, not primary refractory disease, with measurable disease per International Myeloma Working Group criteria. Prior treatment required: Proteasome inhibitor (e.g., bortezomib) and Immunomodulatory drug (e.g., lenalidomide) and Anti‑CD38 antibody (e.g., daratumumab). Prior treatment excluded: anti‑GPRC5D therapy or MMAE‑containing therapy or prior allogeneic stem cell transplantation. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Clinical signs of central nervous system involvement of multiple myeloma.
Known chronic obstructive pulmonary disease or history of interstitial lung disease.
Moderate or severe persistent asthma within the past five years or uncontrolled asthma of any severity.
Severe cardiovascular disease that is not adequately controlled.
History of immunodeficiency disease.
Peripheral neuropathy grade ≥ 2.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Solid tumor (metastatic or unresectable). Histology allowed: Any solid malignancy. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic disease or locally unresectable disease. Prior treatment required: Documented progression on at least one prior standard therapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Presence of at least one of the following genomic alterations – inactivating ATM mutation **or** MYC amplification **or** FBXW8 mutation **or** CCNE1 amplification **or** mutation in any SWI/SNF complex member (ARID1A **or** PBRM1 **or** SMARCA4 **or** ARID2 **or** ARID1B **or** SMARCA2 **or** SS18) **or** ATRX/DAXX alteration. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Allergy or hypersensitivity to peposertib, M1774, or structurally related substances.; Unable to permanently discontinue strong CYP3A4/5, CYP2C19, CYP2C9 inhibitors or induters, or essential P‑glycoprotein/BCRP/OCT1/OATP1B1/OATP1B3 substrate medications.; Requirement to remain on prohibitive proton‐pump inhibitor therapy.; Persistent clinically significant toxicities (>grade 1) attributable to prior anticancer therapy, except alopecia, adequately managed endocrinopathies (≤grade 2), and mild dermatologic toxicity (≤grade 2).; Concurrent participation in another investigational anticancer protocol.; Untreated or actively progressing central nervous system metastasis or leptomeningeal disease when imminent CNS directed therapy is deemed necessary.; Uncontrolled comorbid conditions—including active infections, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, serious cardiac arrhythmias, or chronically draining catheters/device systems.; Baseline corrected QT interval (QTcF) exceeding 470 milliseconds.; Current pregnancy or nursing motherhood.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: non small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: non squamous. Cancer burden allowed: advanced or metastatic disease (unresectable Stage IIIB or IIIC or Stage IV). Prior treatment required: No prior systemic therapy for advanced/metastatic disease. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior systemic chemotherapy, targeted therapy, immunotherapy, or antibody‑drug conjugate for advanced/metastatic NSCLC—including any topoisomerase I‑containing ADC, any TROP2‑directed therapy, any anti‑programmed death‑1/programmed death‑ligand‑1/programmed death‑ligand‑2 agent, and any other immune‑checkpoint inhibitor. Biomarkers required: Programmed death‑ligand‑1 tumour proportion score ≥50% (central 22C3 IHC); negative for epidermal growth factor receptor activating mutations AND negative for anaplastic lymphoma kinase rearrangements AND negative for ros1 rearrangements; absent actionable alterations in neurotrophic tropomyosin receptor kinase, BRAF V600E, ret proto‑oncogene, or met proto‑oncogene (testing optional but must be reported as negative if done); tumor tissue submitted for assessment of trophoblast cell surface protein 2 expression. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of spinal cord compression or active/untreated central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease (stable treated brain lesions permissible).
Recent thoracic radiotherapy (< 4 weeks before study start) or cumulative lung irradiation >30 Gy within 6 months.
Past or current interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis (incl. radiation‑induced) requiring steroids or unable to rule out via imaging.
Severe cardiopulmonary comorbidities (e.g., clinically significant coronary artery disease, myocardial infarction or unstable angina within 6 months, NYHA class III–IV heart failure, uncontrolled arrhythmias, QTcF >470 ms, baseline left ventricular ejection fraction <50%).
Clinically significant ocular disease affecting the cornea.
Receipt of a live or live‑attenuated vaccine within 30 days preceding dosing.
Active, poorly controlled autoimmune disease requiring systemic therapy within the last 2 years.
Immunodeficiency conditions, uncontrolled HIV infection, active hepatitis B or C infections, or ongoing serious infectious processes necessitating intravenous antimicrobial therapy.
Allogenic solid organ or hematopoietic stem‑cell transplantation.
Unresolved grade ≥2 adverse events related to prior anticancer therapy.
Performance status worse than ECOG 1 (unless otherwise stipulated).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: solid tumor. Histology allowed: Any. Cancer burden allowed: locally advanced, unresectable, and/or metastatic disease (excluding active central nervous system metastases). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: KRAS G12C mutation or KRAS G12D mutation or KRAS G12V mutation or KRAS G12A mutation or KRAS G12S mutation or KRAS G13D mutation detectable in tumor tissue or circulating tumor DNA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Pregnancy or planned breastfeeding within 180 days of last dose.; inability to swallow oral tablets.; active central nervous system metastases or carcinomatous meningitis.; unresolved toxicities from prior therapy greater than Grade 1 (exceptions: alopecia, hearing loss, peripheral neuropathy, controlled endocrine abnormalities); significant cardiovascular disease including unstable angina, acute coronary syndromes, recent myocardial infarction, reduced left ventricular ejection fraction or heart failure, uncontrolled or symptomatic arrhythmias.; active hepatitis B, hepatitis C, or untreated HIV infection.; concurrent active malignancy unless in remission with life expectancy >2 years.; active uncontrolled systemic bacterial, viral, fungal, or parasitic infection.; history of non‑infectious pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease with current clinically significant findings.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: all sexes. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: HER2‑positive breast cancer. Cancer burden allowed: unresectable or metastatic disease with measurable lesion(s) per RECIST v1.1; includes patients with treated or clinically inactive central nervous system metastases. Prior treatment required: prior trastuzumab deruxtecan (patient must have progressive disease on or intolerance to this agent). Prior treatment excluded: history of allogeneic bone marrow, stem cell, or solid organ transplantation. Biomarkers required: HER2 overexpression/amplification positive by ASCO‑CAP guidelines (assessed by central laboratory). Biomarkers excluded: none.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Has known or suspected leptomeningeal disease.
Has uncontrolled or significant cardiovascular disease.
Has residual toxicity from prior cancer therapy greater than Grade 1.
Has uncontrolled infection requiring intravenous antibiotic, antiviral, or antifungal therapy.
Has known HIV infection.
Has active hepatitis B or C infection.
Has active SARS‑CoV‑2 infection.
History of life‑ threatening hypersensitivity to monoclonal antibodies or to reagents in zanidatamab formulation.
Is unable to receive trastuzumab due to medical contraindication.
Has a serious underlying medical or psychiatric condition impeding safe receipt of study treatment.
Any condition that prevents receiving the physician’s choice chemotherapy.
Investigator‑determined condition that would contraindicate participation or confound study results.
Known hypersensitivity to any components of the study drugs, including chemotherapy.
Female participants who are currently pregnant or nursing.
Male or female participants planning a future pregnancy.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: Prostatic adenocarcinoma. Histology allowed: Pure adenocarcinoma (mixed adenocarcinoma/neuroendocrine components not permitted). Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic castration‐resistant prostate cancer with ≥ 1 metastatic lesion confirmed by imaging and documented disease progression per PCWG3 criteria (serologic PSA rise, soft‑tissue growth, or emergence of ≥2 new bone lesions). Prior treatment required: (1) Prior bilateral orchiectomy and/or ongoing androgen‑deprivation therapy resulting in castrate testosterone (<50 ng/dL or <1.7 nmol/L); (2) Documented progression following at least one androgen‑receptor‑directed therapy (enzalutamide **or** abiraterone **or** apalutamide **or** darolutamide); (3) Receipt of exactly one taxane chemotherapeutic agent (e.g., docetaxel **or** cabazitaxel) administered in the metastatic castration‑resistant setting. Prior treatment excluded: Steap1‑targeted therapy (any exposure). Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
• Use of any anticancer, immuno‑oncology, or investigational agents within four weeks preceding randomization (excluding continued hormonal manipulation).  
• Prior prostate‑specific membrane antigen radioligand therapy within three months of first dose (unless ≤ two total cycles delivered).  
• Prior palliative external beam radiotherapy within two weeks of first dose without full recovery from acute toxic effects.  
• Ongoing concurrent cytotoxic chemotherapy, additional androgen‑receptor‑directed therapy, immune checkpoint inhibition, radioligand therapy, poly (ADP‑ribose) polymerase inhibitor, biologics, or experimental agents.  
• Recent radionuclide therapy (radium‑223) within two months of first dose.  
• Administration of live or live‑attenuated vaccinations within four weeks of first dose.  
• Active central nervous system metastases (participants with treated, asymptomatic, stable dural involvement are acceptable).  
• Unresolved adverse events greater than Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events grade 1 (with exceptions noted for alopecia or chronic stable toxicities).  
• Performance status worse than ECOG 1.  
• Clinically significant cardiac insufficiency, hepatic impairment, renal dysfunction, uncontrolled infections (including HIV/HBV/HCV), or other serious comorbidities precluding safe participation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: male. Cancer type allowed: prostate cancer. Histology allowed: adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: oligometastatic disease with 1‑5 PSMA‑positive metastatic lesions on PSMA PET/CT, at least one lesion qualifying as distant metastasis (M1) per AJCC 8th ed.; permissible sites include regional/pelvic lymph nodes (N1), distant lymph nodes (M1a) < 10 mm, bone (M1b), lung and other visceral locations (M1c) except liver and brain; all lesions must be suitable for stereotactic body radiotherapy. Prior treatment required: definitive local therapy to the prostate comprising radical prostatectomy with or without postoperative radiation to the prostate bed/pelvic nodes ***or*** external beam radiation therapy to the prostate (with or without seminal vesicle and/or pelvic node coverage) with or without brachytherapy. Prior treatment excluded: any prior systemic therapy aimed at metastatic prostate cancer, including androgen‑deprivation therapy (chemical or surgical castration), androgen‑receptor pathway inhibitors, chemotherapy, radiopharmaceutical agents (e.g., strontium‑89, PSMA‑targeted radionuclides), immunotherapy (e.g., sipuleucel‑T), poly(ADP‑ribose) polymerase inhibitors, or other investigational/systemic anticancer agents. Biomarkers required: PSMA‑positive finding on ^68Ga‑gozetotide or ^18F‑piflufolastat PET/CT confirming 1‑5 metastatic lesions; biochemical recurrence demonstrated by PSA nadir + 2 ng/mL after radiation or PSA > 0.2 ng/mL rising after prostatectomy. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Participants with de novo oligometastatic prostate cancer at screening; unmanaged bladder outflow obstruction or uncontrollable urinary incontinence; other concurrently occurring malignancies anticipated to affect life expectancy or impede disease evaluation (except appropriately treated non‑melanoma skin cancer or superficial bladder cancer); clinically significant cardiac rhythm disorders (e.g., sustained ventricular tachycardia, second‑ or third‑degree AV block without pacing), documented congenital Long QT syndrome or family history of torsades de pointes; immediate necessity for commencement of androgen‑deprivation therapy as judged by treating physician; inadequate organ function precluding safe administration of study drug (severe hepatic, renal, or hematologic impairment).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: non‑small cell lung carcinoma. History of non‑infectious interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis requiring steroids, or unresolved/current ILD/pneumonitis.
Spinal cord compression or a history of leptomeningeal carcinomatosis.
Active infection including tuberculosis, hepatitis B virus, hepatitis C virus, or human immunodeficiency virus.
Uncontrolled brain metastases (unless treated, asymptomatic, stable, and not requiring >10 mg prednisone/day).
Significant cardiac comorbidities as defined in the protocol.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: multiple myeloma. Histology allowed: plasma cell myeloma. Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory disease per International Myeloma Working Group criteria with measurable disease (Serum M‑protein ≥0.5 g/dL **or** Urine M‑protein ≥200 mg/24 h **or** Involved free light chain ≥10 mg/dL with abnormal κ/λ ratio). Prior treatment required: at least two prior lines of systemic antimyeloma therapy **and** prior exposure to an immunomodulatory imide agent **and** prior exposure to a proteasome inhibitor **and** prior exposure to an anti‑CD38 monoclonal antibody. Prior treatment excluded: prior exposure to iberdomide **or** prior exposure to belantamab mafodotin **or** prior receipt of any BCMA‑directed therapy **or** prior allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Uncontrolled HIV infection, active hepatitis B or C infection not meeting rescue criteria, ECOG performance status >2, unresolved grade ≥3 toxicities from prior therapy, active secondary malignancies interfering with assessments, symptomatic amyloid neuropathy, active POEMS syndrome, overt plasma cell leukemia, uncontrolled cardiac disease (significant arrhythmias, recent myocardial infarction or coronary interventions, NYHA class III–IV heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension, QTc prolongation >480 ms, left ventricular ejection fraction <30 %), severe respiratory disorders (history of pneumonitis), baseline renal impairment with calculated CrCl <30 ml/min, hepatic dysfunction exceeding Child‑Pugh class A parameters (total bilirubin >2 mg/dL, AST/ALT >2.5× ULN), active intracranial lesions or progressing brain metastases, active infectious processes (bacterial, viral, fungal), ongoing strong CYP3A4 inhibitor or inducer use without appropriate interruption, existing corneal epithelial disease beyond mild change, inability to safely ingest oral medication, uncontrolled bleeding diatheses.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: cutaneous melanoma. Histology allowed: malignant melanoma arising from skin. Cancer burden allowed: AJCC stage IIIb, IIIc, IIId (regional advanced) or stage IV (metastatic), regardless of resectability. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: prior immunotherapy for metastatic melanoma (e.g., PD‑1/PD‑L1 inhibitors) except anti‑CTLA‑4 antibodies; receipt of any anti‑cancer agent, investigational oncology therapy, or immunosuppressive medication (e.g., methotrexate, azathioprine) within six months; systemic corticosteroids exceeding 10 mg/day prednisone-equivalent within fourteen days; non‑oncology vaccinations (other than SARS‑CoV‑2 vaccine) administered within four weeks preceding first dose of nivolumab/pembrolizumab and continued up to eight weeks after last study dose. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: documented activating mutation or amplification of FLT3.
general_exclusion_criteria: Active brain metastases or leptomeningeal disease. Severe untreated autoimmune disorders (exceptions: vitiligo, Type I diabetes, stable hypothyroidism on replacement, mild psoriasis without systemic therapy, or similar controlled conditions). Positive serology for hepatitis B surface antigen, hepatitis C RNA, or HIV infection. Significant hepatic impairment (AST/ALT > 3×ULN) or biliary obstruction, clinically meaningful renal insufficiency (creatinine > 1.5×ULN or calculated clearance <40 ml/min). Poor performance status (ECOG ≥ 2). Ongoing uncontrolled infection or intercurrent illness. Pregnancy or lactation. Requirement for prohibited concomitant chemotherapy, targeted therapy, radiation, or experimental agents outside the study regimen. Need for imminent major surgery unrelated to melanoma resection. Presence of FLT3 genetic alteration. Current incarceration or inability to comply with study procedures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Multiple myeloma. Histology allowed: Plasma cell neoplasm. Cancer burden allowed: Newly diagnosed disease with measurable disease defined as serum monoclonal protein ≥1.0 g/dL **or** urine monoclonal protein ≥200 mg/24 h **or** serum free‑light chain ≥10 mg/dL **with** abnormal serum immunoglobulin κ/λ ratio (assessment performed at screening). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior chimeric antigen receptor T‑cell therapy; any prior B‑cell maturation antigen–directed therapy; any prior therapeutic intervention for multiple myeloma or smoldering myeloma other than a short course of corticosteroids. Biomarkers required: Presence of measurable disease as defined above (central laboratory assessment). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Received a strong cytochrome P450 3A4 inducer within five drug half‑lives prior to randomisation; Receipt of or planning to receive any live, attenuated vaccine (excluding COVID‑19 vaccines) within four weeks prior to randomisation; Known active or prior central nervous system involvement or clinical signs of meningial infiltration by multiple myeloma; Stroke or seizure occurring within six months of consent; Uncontrolled infections including HIV, hepatitis B or C; Significant cardiovascular disease (e.g., NYHA class III/IV heart failure); Severe hepatic impairment; Severe renal impairment; Poor performance status greater than ECOG 1.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: small cell lung carcinoma. Histology allowed: small cell lung carcinoma (WHO 2021 classification, including pure and combined forms). Cancer burden allowed: unresectable extensive‑stage disease (metastatic). Prior treatment required: at least one prior line of standard therapy for small cell lung carcinoma resulting in progressive disease, inadequate response, intolerance, or decline of further standard therapy. Prior treatment excluded: any prior cellular immunotherapy (e.g., CAR‑T) AND any prior DLL3‑directed therapy. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of checkpoint inhibitor‑associated pneumonitis; clinically significant ascites, pleural or peritoneal effusions; known congenital or acquired severe immunodeficiency uncorrectable medically; leptomeningeal metastases; active or symptomatic brain metastases (except adequately treated and stable lesions off high‑dose steroids); active autoimmune diseases requiring systemic immunosuppression (excluding physiological corticosteroid replacement or inhaled steroids); unresolved cardiovascular instability—including unstable angina or myocardial infarction within six months, or left ventricular ejection fraction < 45%; prior or concomitant malignant neoplasm aside from permitted exceptions; serious or uncontrolled medical illnesses posing undue safety risks; active uncontrolled infectious processes, including HIV, hepatitis B, or hepatitis C (unless meeting specified virologic control criteria); dependence on supplemental oxygen to maintain saturations; cognitive impairment such as dementia or delirium; incomplete recovery from major surgical procedure within four weeks before leukapheresis; current pregnancy or breastfeeding, or plans to conceive or parent biologic children within one year post‑infusion; inability or unwillingness to employ highly effective contraceptive methods; known hypersensitivity to LB2102 formulation constituents, fludarabine, cyclophosphamide, or tocilizumab; lingering grade > 1 adverse effects from preceding anticancer therapy (aside from alopecia); receipt of allogenic stem cell transplantation or solid organ graft; contraindicating allergic reactions to study drug excipients.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: any age. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: Epstein‑Barr virus associated post‑transplant lymphoproliferative disease. clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: male. Cancer type allowed: prostate carcinoma. Histology allowed: prostatic adenocarcinoma (variant components less than 50%). Cancer burden allowed: metastatic castration‑resistant prostate cancer with at least one measurable metastatic lesion and documented progressive disease (by PSA rise or radiographic/BONE progression). Prior treatment required: at least one taxane chemotherapy (docetaxel or cabazitaxel) **and** at least one androgen‑receptor signalling inhibitor (abiraterone, enzalutamide, apalutamide, or darolutamide). Prior treatment excluded: previous administration of 177Lu‑PSMA‑617. Biomarkers required: PSMA‑avidity demonstrated by at least one lesion with uptake greater than liver on ^68Ga‑PSMA‑11 PET **and** serum testosterone ≤50 ng/dL. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
- Previous receipt of 177Lu‑PSMA‑617.
- Known hypersensitivity to 177Lu‑PSMA‑617, carboplatin, or structurally similar substances.
- Untreated brain metastases (treated brain metastases allowable if stable off steroids for ≥4 weeks).

general_exclusion_criteria:
• Unable to undergo magnetic resonance imaging of the brain  
• Greater than Grade 2 intracranial hemorrhage  
• Active systemic disease or uncontrolled intercurrent illness  
• Significant cardiovascular disease (symptomatic arrhythmias, congestive heart failure, recent myocardial infarction, NYHA class III/IV, prolonged QTc)  
• Difficulty swallowing oral medications or severe gastrointestinal disorder affecting absorption  
• Current HIV infection, active hepatitis B or C infection, or past hepatitis B core antibody positivity without documented negative PCR  
• Ongoing serious infection (bacterial, viral, fungal)  
• Hypersensitivity to acalabrutinise or its constituents  
• Active bleeding diatheses or coagulation abnormalities (prolonged PT/aPTT > 2× ULN)  
• Autoimmune hemolytic anaemia or immune thrombocytopenia not controlled  
• Requirement for strong CYP3A4 modulators, warfarin/vitamin K antagonist anticoagulation, or other prohibited concomitant medicines  
• Recent (< 6 months) cerebrovascular accident or intracranial hemorrhagic event  
• Major surgical procedure within 28 days preceding first dose  
• Pregnancy, lactation, or inadequate contraceptive protection where applicable  
• Participation in another therapeutic clinical trial concurrently  
• Advanced liver disease classified as Child‑Pugh score C  
• Uncontrolled hypertension despite optimized therapy.

Current active malignancies (with limited exceptions such as adequately treated skin carcinomas, long‑term remitted stage I solid tumors, low‑risk prostate cancer). Significant untreated autoimmune disorders. Positive HIV serology. Planned autologous stem cell transplantation within six months of starting study drug. Recent (<3 months) acute coronary syndromes or unstable ventricular arrhythmias. Left ventricular ejection fraction <30%. Severe valvular stenosis (valve area <1.0 cm²) or critical congenital heart disease. Sustained ventricular tachycardia, aborted ventricular fibrillation, or bradyarrhythmias requiring pacing without existing pacemaker/defibrillator. Corrected QT interval >550 ms absent a paced rhythm. Acute myocardial ischemia or concerning conduction disturbances on baseline electrocardiogram (other than isolated first‑degree AV block, Mobitz type Ⅰ second‑degree AV block, right/left bundle branch blocks, stable atrial fibrillation, or benign bifascicular block). Major surgical procedure involving general anaesthesia within four weeks before registration or planned during the study. New York Heart Association Class IV heart failure or acutely decompensated congestive heart failure. Persistent medically unmanaged orthostatic hypotension leading to syncope/presyncope.

Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior therapeutic surgery (aside from diagnostic biopsy), any prior radiotherapy directed at the index tumor, any prior systemic anticancer therapy for the index tumor, cumulative lifetime anthracycline exposure greater than 450 mg/m² doxorubicin-equivalent, documented hypersensitivity to doxorubicin, ifosfamide, dacarbazine or their formulations. Biomarkers required: Overexpression of MDM2 protein **AND** overexpression of CDK4 protein demonstrated by immunohistochemistry (assessment mandated at screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
• History of congestive heart failure  
• Clinically significant coronary artery disease (angina pectoris) or myocardial infarction within 12 months preceding enrolment  
• Uncontrolled arterial hypertension (systolic ≥150 mm Hg or diastolic ≥100 mm Hg despite optimized therapy)  
• Uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmia  
• Active, untreated, or uncontrolled infectious disease  
• Administration of live attenuated vaccinations within 30 days prior to registration  
• Interstitial cystitis or obstructive uropathy affecting the urinary bladder  
• Presence of another invasive malignancy diagnosed within the previous five years, excluding adequately treated basal/squamous cell carcinoma of the skin, appropriately staged cervical carcinoma, or clinically stage T1–T2a, Gleason ≤ 6 prostate adenocarcinoma  
• Severe comorbidities rendering the participant unsuitable for anesthesia or major surgery (e.g., uncontrolled diabetes mellitus, severe pulmonary disease)  
• Pregnancy or lactation, and inability or unwillingness to practice effective contraception throughout the study period and designated post‑treatment interval  
• Contraindications to iodinated contrast media, magnetic resonance imaging, or investigational imaging tracers required by the protocol.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: male. Cancer type allowed: prostate carcinoma. Histology allowed: adenocarcinoma. general_exclusion_criteria: Painful metastatic prostate cancer requiring opioid or other analgesic interventions. Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status ≥3. Unstable or serious concomitant medical, psychiatric, or laboratory conditions compromising safety. Active uncontrolled infection, including known HIV/AIDS or chronic hepatitis B or C. Current therapeutic anticoagulation with warfarin, rivaroxaban, or apixaban (unless transition to low molecular weight heparin possible). History of venous thromboembolism within the past year not managed with systemic anticoagulation. Hematocrit greater than 51%. Untreated severe obstructive sleep apnea. Uncontrolled or severely compromised congestive heart failure. Known allergy to sesame seed oil or cottonseed oil. Recent major surgery requiring general anaesthesia within three weeks preceding screening or incomplete postoperative healing.

general_exclusion_criteria:
* Prior therapy with an antibody‑drug conjugate containing a topoisomerase I inhibitor.
* Presence of primary platinum‑refractory disease (no response or progression ≤91 days after completion of first‑line platinum).
* History of another malignant neoplasm within 3 years preceding study entry, excluding low‑risk lesions (carcinoma in situ of cervix, non‑melanoma skin cancer, ductal carcinoma in situ, stage I uterus cancer, etc.).
* Active central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease (treated/stable brain metastases permitted under strict stability criteria).
* Recent (>30 days) acute gastrointestinal obstruction or current obstructive findings; dependence on parenteral nutrition requires special consideration.
* Symptomatic ascites necessitating paracentesis more frequently than once every ~4 weeks; presence of an indwelling peritoneal drainage catheter subject to additional evaluation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: Any. Prior treatment excluded: Receipt of more than one JAK2 inhibitor; prior treatment with BH3‑mimetic compounds, bromodomain & extraterminal (BET) inhibitors, phosphatidylinositol‑3‑kinase or telomerase inhibitors; prior hematopoietic stem cell transplantation; current eligibility for stem cell transplantation. Concomitant anticancer therapy for an active malignant neoplasm or myelofibrosis outside of protocol‑specified best available therapy.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: Endometrial intraepithelial neoplasia (including complex atypical hyperplasia and atypical hyperplasia). Histology allowed: Adenomatous/endometrioid precancerous epithelium consistent with EIN. Cancer burden allowed: Disease confined to the endometrium without invasive carcinoma (pre‑invasive lesion). Prior treatment required: Must be a candidate for definitive surgical management (hysterectomy) or acceptable for insertion of a progestin‐releasing intrauterine device. Prior treatment excluded: Concurrent hormonal contraceptives or post‑menopausal hormone therapy (unless low‑potency progestins ≤40 mg megestrol, ≤10 mg medroxyprogesterone, ≤10 mg norethindrone acetate, ≤0.35 mg norethindrone, ≤300 mg oral micronised progesterone used for <8 weeks and initiated after index biopsy); current use of selective estrogen receptor modulators; current use of aromatase inhibitors; current use of metformin therapy; current use of dofetilide, ulipristal, carbonic anhydrase inhibitors; current use of medicines that markedly reduce metformin clearance (ranolazine, vandetanib, dolutegravir, cimetidine). Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of pulmonary embolism, thrombotic stroke, arterial thrombosis, or deep vein thrombosis.
Pregnancy or intention to become pregnant during the study; lack of adequate contraception.
Breastfeeding.
Uncontrolled intercurrent acute or serious illnesses or psychiatric disorders impairing protocol adherence.
Performance status greater than ECOG 2 (Karnofsky score <60%).
Total bilirubin >1.5×upper limit of normal, AST or ALT >3×upper limit of normal, serum creatinine >1.5×upper limit of normal.
Active infectious diseases requiring treatment (uncontrolled HIV, untreated hepatitis B/C).
Alcohol use disorder posing heightened risk of metformin–associated lactic acidosis.
Diabetes managed with sulfonylureas or meglitinides.
Inability to perform regular capillary glucose monitoring (for insulin‑treated diabetics).
Any other concurrently enrolling investigational product.

History of active interstitial pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.
Inherited or acquired severe immunodeficiency requiring active systemic therapy (e.g., primary immunodeficiency syndromes, organ transplant recipient).
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness threatening safety (active infections, significant autoimmune diseases, serious cardiac arrhythmias, unstable angina, gastrointestinal disorders impairing pill ingestion or absorption).
Systemic corticosteroid use exceeding 10 mg prednisone equivalent daily for adults or >0.5 mg/kg (max 10 mg/day) for minors within 14 days before enrollment.
Peripheral neuropathy greater than Grade 1 at baseline or known Charcot‑Marie‑Tooth disease.
Concurrent malignant disease that could confound safety/efficacy assessments.
Live vaccination within 30 days preceding Day 1 of protocol therapy (except permissible mRNA COVID‑19 vaccines).
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Failure to agree to adequate contraception throughout treatment and designated follow‑up periods.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Major surgery within 14 days before study drug initiation or incomplete recovery from operative complications.
Use of prohibited concomitant medications that cannot be stopped seven days prior to dosing.
Receipt of other investigational therapeutic agents.
Administration of additional antitumour agents aside from prescribed study regimens.
Co‑administration of immune‑suppressive agents (other than permissible corticosteroids).
Herbal supplements potentially interacting with study drug pharmacokinetics.
Known hypersensitivity to any components of abemaciclib formulation.
Active systemic bacterial infection requiring IV antibiotics, active fungal infection, or detected viral infection (including HIV positivity, active hepatitis B or C).
Significant gastrointestinal disorder impairing drug absorption (e.g., ulcerative disease, uncontrolled nausea/vomiting/diarrhea, malabsorptive syndromes, extensive small‑bowel resections).
History of serious cardiovascular events: syncopal episodes of cardiac origin, pathogenic ventricular arrhythmias, or sudden cardiac death.
Uncontrolled/severe comorbid illnesses posing excessive safety risk (e.g., chronic pancreatitis, chronic active hepatitis).
Incapacity to ingest oral tablets.
Severe respiratory pathology (interstitial lung disease, resting hypoxia requiring supplemental O₂, severe dyspnoea).
Renal insufficiency with calculated creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min.
Baseline hepatic dysfunction exceeding allowable thresholds (unless met by exemption rules already outlined).
Pre‑existent moderate-to-severe inflammatory bowel disease or baseline diarrhoea graded ≥ 2.
Documented pattern of non‑adherence to medical regimens.
Any second malignant neoplasm diagnosed within two years preceding enrolment, except adequately treated basal/squamous cutaneous carcinoma, non‑melanoma skin cancer, or surgically cured cervix uteri cancer.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of clinically significant adverse reaction to PD‑1 or PD‑L1 therapy resulting in discontinuation.
Systemic administration of immunosuppressive agents within seven days preceding study treatment (except topical/inhaled steroids and physiological hormone replacements).
Pregnancy or breastfeeding in women of childbearing potential.
Prior solid organ transplant.
Active hepatitis B, hepatitis C, or HIV infection (unless well‑controlled under stipulated conditions).
Active autoimmune disease requiring chronic glucocorticoids (>10 mg prednisolone daily) or other immunosuppression (exceptions permitted after investigator discretion).
Unstable cardiovascular disease—including congestive heart failure greater than NYHA Class II, untreated coronary artery disease, recent (<3 months) acute coronary syndrome, unstable angina, or serious ventricular arrhythmia.
QTc interval exceeding 480 ms (Fridericia corrected).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: Small‑cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Advanced disease that is recurrent, refractory, or progressing after standard therapy with at least one measurable lesion per RECIST 1.1. Prior treatment required: Documented progression after at least one line of standard therapy for small‑cell lung cancer. Prior treatment excluded: History of severe toxicity leading to discontinuation of an Aurora kinase inhibitor or a VEGF/VEGFR inhibitor. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
• Current or recent (<21 days) systemic anticancer therapy (excluding permitted washout).
• High‑dose (>10 mg/day prednisone-equivalent) systemic glucocorticoid usage.
• Concurrent malignancies expected to confound evaluation.
• NYHA class II or greater congestive heart failure, uncontrolled angina, MI within 6 months, left ventricle EF < 50%, clinically significant arrhythmias, or untreated coronary artery disease.
• Prolonged corrected QT interval (QTcF > 470 ms women, > 450 ms men).
• Untreated or symptomatic hypertension (SBP ≥ 140 mmHg or DBP ≥ 90 mmHg) despite stable monotherapy.
• Active hemoptysis, recent GI bleed, or high risk for hemorrhage.
• Uncontrolled pleural effusion, pericardial effusion, or ascites.
• Symptomatic central nervous system metastases requiring intervention.
• Venous thromboembolism events within 6 months.
• Interstitial lung disease needing treatment or evident on baseline CT/MRI.
• Persistent Grade ≥ 2 adverse effects from prior therapy (aside from alopecia).
• Gastrointestinal disorders affecting oral drug administration (e.g., swallowing difficulty, chronic diarrhoea, bowel obstruction) or total gastrectomy.
• Major surgery within 6 weeks or minor surgery within 2 weeks preceding screening.
• Proteinuria ≥2+ dipstick or ≥1 g/24h urinary protein.
• Active viral infections (hepatitis B, hepatitis C, HIV) or serious bacterial/fungal infection.
• Cognitive or psychiatric disorder impairing consent/compliance.
• Pregnant, nursing, or attempting conception; lack of adequate contraception.
• Substance dependence interfering with protocol adherence.
• Concomitant use of potent CYP3A4 inhibitors or inducers throughout dose‑escalation phase.
