general_exclusion_criteria:
History of significant hemoptysis or pulmonary haemorrhage within four weeks of starting study medication.
Major surgery performed within four weeks of initiating study treatment.
Intestinal disease, inflammatory bowel disease, extensive gastric resection, or other gastrointestinal conditions likely to impair absorption of oral medication or prevent swallowing.
Cardiovascular abnormalities deemed clinically significant.
Uncontrolled concurrent illness including severe infection, unstable cardiovascular disease, or psychiatric disorder limiting compliance.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Concurrent participation in another interventional oncology trial.
Any condition judged by investigator to place subject at undue risk or interfere with interpretation of results.

general_exclusion_criteria:
• Receiving current anticancer therapy or investigational agents.
• Lack of adequate tumor tissue for glypican‑3 testing.
• History of uncontrolled seizures.
• Need for systemic corticosteroids exceeding 0.15 mg/kg/day prednisolone equivalent (unless inhaled/topical/adrenal‐replacement dosing).
• Presence of a central nervous system primary tumor, CNS metastasis, or clinically significant cerebrovascular lesion.
• Baseline corrected QTc greater than 480 ms.
• Inability to attend scheduled follow‑ups or complete required assessments.
• Active uncontrolled infection.
• Previous solid organ transplantation.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of cardiac disease such as myocardial infarction, unstable angina, severe vascular disease, or symptomatic arrhythmia. Major concurrent medical illnesses that significantly increase risk (severe cardiovascular disease, uncontrolled hypertension, etc.). Recent use of strong cytochrome P450 3A4 enzyme inducers within two weeks of first dosing. Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or lack of adequate contraception for men and women of reproductive potential. Clinical situations requiring immediate tumour reduction due to proximity to vital structures.

Prior treatment excluded: Any prior chemotherapy or radiotherapy, except up to 1–2 doses of single‑agent carboplatin for relapsed seminoma post‑adjuvant therapy, or low‑dose induction chemotherapy (cisplatin ± etoposide, carboplatin ± etoposide, or baby‑BOP) permissible only in very select cases. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
• History of another primary malignancy (except appropriately treated non‑melanoma skin cancer, germ cell tumor, or malignancies >5 years ago with no recurrence).
• Significant cardiac disease precluding adequate intravenous hydration for cisplatin.
• Clinically significant cardiopulmonary disease that contraindicates bleomycin administration.
• Peripheral neuropathy grade ≥ 2 or clinically significant sensorineural hearing loss or tinnitus.
• Active severe infections or concomitant illnesses compromising safe participation.
• Inadequate contraceptive measures (men must use dual methods including condoms throughout treatment and for 1 year thereafter).
• Known allergy or hypersensitivity to any investigational agents.
• Psychological, social, geographic, substance‑use disorders, or other conditions judged by investigators to impede adherence to protocol.

general_exclusion_criteria: Subjects with neuroblastoma having only bone‑marrow involvement assessable solely by aspirate.; Recent major surgical procedure within 14 days preceding first dose.; Active systemic bacterial, fungal, or viral infection—including human immunodeficiency virus positivity.; Clinically significant gastrointestinal disorders impairing oral drug absorption (e.g., Crohn’s disease, ulcerative colitis, short bowel syndrome).; Cardiac contraindications: baseline QTc > 480 ms, serious rhythm/conduction disturbances, or conditions increasing risk of torsades de pointes (heart failure, congenital Long QT, pertinent medications).; Peripheral sensory neuropathy graded ≥ 2.; Concomitant use of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or inducers now or anticipated.; History of hypersensitivity reaction to repotrectinib or its formulation components.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 12 months to 21 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: non‑Hodgkin lymphoma. Histology allowed: non‑Hodgkin lymphoma (any subclass). Cancer burden allowed: recurrent or refractory disease; measurable or evaluable disease permitted. Prior treatment required: documentation of prior anticancer therapy resulting in recurrence or refractory status. Prior treatment excluded: prior exposure to tegavivint. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of pneumonitis or pulmonary fibrosis.
Active congestive heart failure, symptomatic coronary artery disease, or uncontrolled arrhythmia.
Clinically significant hepatic dysfunction (bilirubin >1.5× ULN, ALT >135 U/L).
Severe renal impairment (creatinine clearance <70 ml/min/1.73 m²).
Uncontrolled central nervous system metastases (primary brain tumors excluded; craniopharyngioma exempted).
Active untreated viral infections including HIV, chronic hepatitis B or C.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding; lack of reliable contraception for participants of reproductive potential.
Concurrent participation in another investigational drug study or ongoing use of other anticancer agents.
Receipt of strong CYP3A4 inducer or inhibitor within prohibited window.
Recent bisphosphonate (<4 weeks) or denosumab (>180 days) exposure.
Primary brain tumors (aside from designated exceptions).
Metabolic bone disorders (hyperparathyroidism, Paget’s disease, osteomalacia) or grade ≥ 2 hypocalcemia not correctable with oral calcium.
Vitamin D level <20 ng/ml not supplemented appropriately.
Grade ≥ 3 osteoporosis.
Ongoing uncontrolled bacterial, fungal, or opportunistic infection.
Previous solid organ transplantation.
Inability to comply with study procedures or follow‑up assessments.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years or older. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: Solid tumors (primary central nervous system tumors excluded). Histology allowed: Any. Cancer burden allowed: Advanced or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: Progressive disease following at least one prior line of therapy administered in the advanced or metastatic setting. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Pathogenic alteration in BRCA1 or BRCA2 **or** alteration in any homologous recombination deficiency‐associated gene (e.g., ATM, BARD1, BRIP1, CDK12, CHEK1, CHEK2, FANCL, PALB2, PPP2R2A, RAD51B, RAD51C, RAD51D, RAD54L, NBN, FANCA). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Known primary central nervous system malignancy.
Impairment of gastrointestinal function or gastrointestinal disease that may substantially affect drug absorption.
Active, uncontrolled infection.
Clinically significant cardiovascular abnormalities.
Major surgical procedure within four weeks before enrolment.
Radiation therapy within two weeks before enrolment.
Systemic cytotoxic chemotherapy within four weeks before enrolment.
Radioimmunotherapy within six weeks before enrolment.
Therapeutic antibody administration within four weeks before enrolment.
Anti‑cancer small‑molecule therapy within five half‑lives or two weeks (whichever is shorter) before enrolment.
Current active hepatic or biliary disease.
History of allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell or solid organ transplantation (for applicable cohort).
Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppressive therapy within the preceding two years (for applicable cohort).
History of non‑infectious pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease necessitating corticosteroids or currently ongoing pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease (for applicable cohort).

Prior treatment required: Pathogenic germline or somatic BRCA1 mutation **or** pathogenic germline or somatic BRCA2 mutation **or** pathogenic germline PALB2 mutation. Prior treatment excluded: More than 2 prior lines of cytotoxic chemotherapy for metastatic disease (ADC and checkpoint inhibitors count toward this limit); prior exposure to a CSF1R antibody; clinical progression on a PARP inhibitor or receipt of a PARP inhibitor within 12 months preceding enrollment. Biomarkers required: Confirmation of deleterious or presumptively deleterious germline or somatic BRCA1 mutation, germline or somatic BRCA2 mutation, **or** germline PALB2 mutation by a CLIA‑certified laboratory. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: Low grade serous ovarian cancer. Histology allowed: Low grade serous epithelial ovarian carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Measurable disease per RECIST 1.1. Prior treatment required: None specifically required; any number of prior antitumour therapies permitted. Prior treatment excluded: Prior MEK inhibitor therapy; prior CDK4/6 inhibitor therapy. Biomarkers required: Presence of KRAS mutation **or** NRAS mutation **or** HRAS mutation **or** rare RAF fusion **and** absence of BRAF V600E mutation. Biomarkers excluded: BRAF V600E mutation.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of interstitial lung disease, idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, organising pneumonia, drug‑induced pneumonitis, or active pneumonitis on imaging. Recent (<6 months) bowel perforation or intestinal fistula. Unable to swallow oral medication or clinically significant malabsorption (gastrectomy, active IBD). Active dermatological disorder requiring systemic therapy within the preceding year. Personal history of rhabdomyolysis. Significant ophthalmologic diseases—including glaucoma, retinal venous occlusion, elevated intra‑ocular pressure >21 mmHg, corneal instability, keratitis, or other serious ocular surface inflammation. Known hypersensitivity to palbociclib, binimetinib, or excipients. Previous allogeneic hematopoietic stem‑cell or solid‑organ transplantation. Untreated central nervous system metastasis; brain lesions not locally controlled for ≤1 month or ongoing steroid dependence. Residual CTCAE grade ≥2 toxicity from prior anticancer therapy (grade 2 alopecia exempt). Left ventricular ejection fraction <50%. Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease, severe hepatic insufficiency (bilirubin >1.5×ULN unless Gilberts’ variant <3 mg/dL), or renal impairment (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min). Concurrent strong CYP3A4 inducer/inhibitor usage without adequate interruption. Current HIV infection with detectable viral load, active hepatitis B or C infection not adequately suppressed/cured. Pregnancy or breastfeeding.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≤ 30 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: neuroblastoma or ganglioneuroblastoma (nodular). Histology allowed: neuroblastic tumour (neuroblastoma, ganglioneuroblastoma nodular). Cancer burden allowed: newly diagnosed high‑risk disease defined as any of the following – (a) any age with INRG stage L2, MS, or M **and** MYCN amplification; (b) age ≥ 547 days with INRG stage M regardless of biological features; (c) any age originally staged L1 with MYCN amplification that progresses to stage M without preceding systemic chemotherapy; or (d) age ≥ 547 days originally staged L1, L2, or MS that progresses to stage M without preceding systemic chemotherapy. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: any prior anticancer therapy other than (i) emergent initiation of topotecan ± cyclophosphamide as stipulated, (ii) a single cycle of low‑ or intermediate‑risk chemotherapy (per protocols analogous to ANBL0531, ANBL1232, etc.), or (iii) localized emergency radiation delivered before or shortly after definitive diagnosis. Biomarkers required: determination of MYCN status during screening; MYCN amplification required for enrolment pathways that stipulate MYCN‑amplified disease. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Patients with known bone marrow failure syndromes.
Patients receiving chronic systemic immunosuppressive medication (excluding topical/inhaled corticosteroids) for indications other than allergy prophylaxis or adrenal support.
Patients with a primary immunodeficiency disorder necessitating regular intravenous immune globulin replacement.
Female patients who are pregnant or planning to become pregnant; a negative pregnancy test is required for females of child‑bearing potential.
Breastfeeding mothers.
Individuals of reproductive potential who decline to use adequate contraception throughout the study period.
Uncontrolled cardiovascular abnormalities (shortening fraction < 27 % or ejection fraction < 50 %).

general_exclusion_criteria: History of pneumonitis. Symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, significant cardiac arrhythmias. Renal insufficiency or hepatic impairment meeting predefined laboratory thresholds. Uncontrolled brain metastases requiring immediate neurosurgical intervention. Current infection with human immunodeficiency virus, hepatitis B, or hepatitis C. Poor performance status exceeding Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group grade 2. Pregnancy or breastfeeding. Requirement for urgent surgical stabilization of skeletal metastases (e.g., impending pathological fracture of femur). Concurrent receipt of alternate locally ablative therapies that would prevent delivery of stereotactic body radiation therapy to intended targets. Progressive disease greater than 1 cm that is not amenable to stereotactic body radiation therapy. Presence of an additional actively progressing malignancy requiring current therapy, except for adequately treated superficial skin carcinomas, in‑situ cervical neoplasms, or low‑risk prostate adenocarcinoma under active surveillance.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: systemic light‐chain amyloidosis. Prior treatment required: at least one prior line of anti‑plasma‑cell therapy. Prior treatment excluded: prior exposure to ixazomib (MLN9708) and prior exposure to venetoclax. Biomarkers required: t(11;14) translocation positive by fluorescent in‑situ hybridisation on bone‑marrow specimen. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Major surgery or radiotherapy within 14 days prior to enrolment; anti‑plasma‑cell therapy within 4 weeks prior to enrolment; unresolved toxicity greater than grade 1 from prior anticancer therapy (except alopecia); participation in another investigational study within 30 days; central nervous system involvement; hypersensitivity to venetoclax, ixazomib, dexamethasone or structurally related substances; use of strong or moderate CYP3A inhibitors or inducers, or P‑gp interacting drugs; uncontrolled intercurrent illnesses—including active serious infection, untreated hepatitis B or C, uncontrolled hypertension, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, significant arrhythmias, myocardial infarction within 6 months}; psychiatric or social circumstances limiting protocol adherence; current lactation or positive pregnancy test; ineffective contraception in females of child‑bearing potential or males not employing approved barriers/abstinence; left ventricular ejection fraction < 35%; NYHA functional class III/IV; advanced cardiac amyloidosis Mayo stage IIIB (NT‑ProBNP > 8500 pg/mL); peripheral neuropathy grade ≥ 3 (grade 2 with pain); clinically significant gastrointestinal disorders affecting oral drug absorption; grade ≥ 3 diarrhea; inability to demonstrate measurable disease by serum free light chain, serum M‑spike, or urinary monoclonal protein.

general_exclusion_criteria:
• Unable to give adequate informed consent.
• Active suicidal ideation with intent/plans or recent attempt (within past 6 months).
• Current or historical primary psychotic disorders, major depressive disorder with psychotic features, bipolar I disorder, dissociative identity disorder, or unresolved severe psychiatric illnesses.
• Ongoing substance use disorder active within the past year.
• Requirement for concurrent serotininergic antidepressants, SNRIs, TCAs, efavirenz, serotonergic dietary supplements, MAO inhibitors, antipsychotics, mood stabilizers, aldehyde dehydrogenase inhibitors, strong UGT1A0/A1A10 inhibitors (cannot be safely tapered/discontinued).
• Conditions impeding oral intake or digestion/absorption.
• Clinically significant uncontrolled hematologic, endocrinologic, cerebrovascular, cardiovascular, coronary, pulmonary, renal, gastrointestinal, immune-compromised, or neurologic diseases (except well-controlled hypothyroidism on stable replacement).
• Laboratory abnormalities grade ≥ 3 affecting cognition or drug metabolism (per CTCAE).
• Cirrhosis or liver failure.
• Uncontrolled hypertension (BP ≥ 140/90 mm Hg confirmed on three readings).
• Resting heart rate > 100 bpm on three measurements.
• History of clinically significant ventricular arrhythmias, Wolff‑Parkinson‑White syndrome unablated, recent (>12 mo) symptomatic arrhythmias (atrial fibrillation/flutter, SVT, VT) unless fully treated.
• Risk factors for torsades de pointe (heart failure, electrolyte disturbances, congenital long QT).
• Myocardial infarction, established coronary artery disease, or congestive heart failure.
• Prolonged baseline QTc/F > 450 ms in men or > 460 ms in women (evaluated according to sex assigned at birth unless hormone therapy ≥5 yr).
• Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or ability to conceive without reliable contraceptive measures (effective contraception required).
• Known hypersensitivity to any component of the investigational medicinal product.

Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: epidermal growth factor receptor exon 20 insertion mutation. Biomarkers excluded: epidermal growth factor receptor T790M mutation.
general_exclusion_criteria:
* Presence of leptomeningeal disease or spinal cord compression (except radiographic-only without neurologic signs).
* History of New York Heart Association class III or IV congestive heart failure, severe non‑ischemic cardiomyopathy, recent (<6 months) unstable angina, myocardial infarction, or ventricular arrhythmias.
* Current or past interstitial lung disease, drug‑induced interstitial lung disease, or radiation pneumonitis requiring steroids, or any clinically active interstitial lung disease.
* Symptomatic Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection.
* Active infection requiring systemic therapy or current hepatitis B or C infection (patients who are HBsAg negative or have normal HBV DNA levels are permissible).
* Uncontrolled gastrointestinal disorders such as inflammatory bowel disease, short bowel syndrome, or other significant malabsorptive conditions.
* Other concomitant serious unmanaged medical, psychiatric, or substance abuse conditions.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥12 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Acute myeloid leukaemia. Histology allowed: Acute myeloid leukaemia. Cancer burden allowed: First remission with morphologic blasts < 5% after high‑intensity chemotherapy **or** first remission after ≥2 cycles low‑intensity therapy (hypomethylating agent or low‑dose cytarabine) **or** second remission after any therapy; measurable residual disease ≥ 0.1% by multicolour flow cytometry; no clinically active extramedullary disease. Prior treatment required: Received high‑intensity chemotherapy or ≥2 cycles low‑intensity AML therapy achieving first remission, or any therapy achieving second remission. Prior treatment excluded: Prior menin inhibitor exposure. Biomarkers required: NPM1 mutation detected (screened during enrolment). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Active hepatitis B virus infection, active hepatitis C virus infection, or human immunodeficiency virus infection; pregnancy or breastfeeding; uncontrolled active infection; significant cardiovascular disease within six months including myocardial infarction, unstable angina, New York Heart Association class II or higher heart failure, serious ventricular arrhythmia, stroke or transient ischemic attack; corrected QT interval prolongation greater than 450 ms in males or 470 ms in females; clinically active central nervous system leukaemia; concurrent active malignant neoplasm requiring treatment; severe gastrointestinal or metabolic disorders impairing oral medication absorption; dependence on systemic immunosuppressive therapy post–hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (except allowable low‑dose corticosteroids); grades greater than 2 acute graft‑versus‑host disease, moderate/severe limited chronic graft‑vs‑host disease, or extensive chronic graft‑vs‑host disease.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18. Sex allowed: male. Cancer type allowed: prostate cancer. Histology allowed: adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: pelvic nodal disease only (pathological or imaging confirmation) with no distant metastasis (M0). Prior treatment required: radical prostatectomy (any surgical approach). Prior treatment excluded: systemic chemotherapy for prostate cancer; prior radiotherapy to the prostate/surgical bed that would overlap planned postoperative radiation fields; androgen deprivation therapy administered before radical prostatectomy; prior androgen‐receptor signaling inhibitor use lasting more than 180 days before registration; current use of a 5‑alpha reductase inhibitor. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: history of seizure or neurologic condition predisposing to seizure; recent stroke (≤ 1 year); severe or unstable coronary artery disease, myocardial infarction, arterial or venous thrombosis, or clinically significant ventricular arrhythmia within 12 months; New York Heart Association class III/IV congestive heart failure; uncontrolled hypertension (systolic ≥ 160 mmHg or diastolic ≥ 100 mmHg); active untreated infection; gastrointestinal disorder impairing oral drug absorption; baseline moderate-to-severe hepatic impairment (Child‑Pugh B or C); creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min; inability to swallow oral tablets; chronic glucocorticoid use exceeding 10 mg prednisone equivalents daily; simultaneous participation in another therapeutic clinical trial; inflammatory bowel disease.

Prior treatment required: prior adjuvant endocrine therapy showing resistance (relapse on‑treatment ≥12 months or off‑treatment ≤12 months). Biomarkers required: ESR1 mutation status assessed (mutated or non‑mutated). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of pneumonitis  
Cardiac failure or active cardiac disease / history of cardiac dysfunction  
Clinically significant hepatic disease or liver dysfunction  
Significant renal insufficiency  
Uncontrolled or symptomatic brain metastases  
Active infection with HIV or chronic viral hepatitis B/C  
Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status greater than 1 (poor functional status)

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Glioblastoma. Histology allowed: Glioblastoma (World Health Organization Grade IV astrocytic tumor). Cancer burden allowed: Newly diagnosed glioblastoma with gadolinium‑enhancing tumor visible on presurgical magnetic resonance imaging; disease limited to the brain. Prior treatment required: Diagnostic surgical resection or needle/core biopsy yielding adequate tumor tissue; stable background corticosteroid dosing ≤4 mg dexamethasone (or equivalent) daily for at least seven consecutive days preceding study treatment. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior radiotherapy, chemotherapy (including temozolomide), Carmustine wafer implantation, intracerebral or intraventricular anticancer agents, immunotherapy, or other antitumor therapy aimed at glioma of any grade. Biomarkers required: None. Biomarkers excluded: None.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History or current diagnosis of clinically significant cardiac disease (myocardial infarction, unstable angina, congestive cardiomyopathy, symptomatic pericarditis, recent coronary artery bypass graft, chronic heart failure) or resting QTc ≥450 ms (men) / ≥460 ms (women), long QT syndrome, familial sudden death syndromes, unexplained torsades de pointes risk factors, inability to calculate corrected QT, serious cardiac arrhythmias, left bundle branch block, high‑grade atrioventricular block, left ventricular ejection fraction <50%.
Uncontrolled arterial hypertension (systolic BP ≥160 mm Hg and/or diastolic BP ≥100 mm Hg) despite antihypertensive therapy.
Active infection requiring systemic antibiotics, human immunodeficiency virus infection, active hepatitis B or C infection.
Significant hepatic impairment (alanine aminotransferase or aspartate aminotransferase >3× upper limit of normal, total bilirubin >1.5× upper limit of normal unless due to Gilberts’ syndrome) or pancreatic enzyme elevation suggestive of ongoing pancreatitis.
Renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance <60 ml/min).
Performance status worse than ECOG 2 (or Karnofsky score <70%).  
Concurrent or planned anticancer therapy for glioma outside the study protocol.  
Any other active malignancy within three years prior to enrollment, except adequately treated non‑melanoma skin cancer (basal or squamous cell carcinoma) or superficial urothelial carcinoma.

clinical_space_summary: Age range approved: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Glioblastoma. Histology allowed: Glioblastoma (World Health Organization Grade IV astrocytic tumor). Cancer burden allowed: First or second recurrence of glioblastoma after prior cranial radiotherapy, with at least one bidimensional contrast‑enhancing lesion measuring ≥10 mm per modified RANO criteria, and demonstrable uptake of [68Ga]Ga‑NeoB on PET/CT or PET/MRI localized to the tumor region. Prior treatment required: Documentation of prior external beam radiotherapy delivered for the index glioblastoma episode (completion ≥12 weeks before enrollment). Prior treatment excluded: Prior therapeutic-use bevacizumab (at any time); more than two prior systemic anticancer therapy lines (adjuvant counting as a line); more than one repeat surgical resection for recurrent disease; any intracerebral or intraventricular anticancer agent administered before study treatment. Biomarkers required: Positive [68Ga]Ga‑NeoB uptake on PET imaging. Biomarkers excluded: None.

Active infection requiring systemic antibiotics, human immunodeficiency virus infection, active hepatitis B or C infection.

Any other active malignancy within three years prior to enrollment, except adequately treated non‑melanoma skin cancer (basal or squamous cell carcinoma) or superficial urothelial carcinoma.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: greater than or equal to 12 months and less than or equal to 21 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: diffuse intrinsic pontine glioma. Histology allowed: high‑grade glioma of the pons (diagnosis may be made clinically without tissue). Cancer burden allowed: localized disease confined to the pons meeting radiographic criteria (≤2⁄3 pontine involvement required), no metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: none (surgery and/or corticosteroids permitted). general_exclusion_criteria:
Patients with uncontrolled infections.
Patients who have undergone prior solid organ transplantation.
Patients with grade > 1 extrapyramidal movement disorders.
Patients with known macular degeneration, uncontrolled glaucoma, or cataracts.
Patients with documented metastatic disease.
Patients with gliomatosis cerebri type 1 or 2 (unless H3 K27M‑mutant bithalamic).
Patients unable to receive prescribed radiation therapy.
Pregnant women.
Breastfeeding mothers (unless agreeing not to nurse).
Women of child‑bearing potential without a recent negative pregnancy test.
Individuals of reproductive potential not willing to use two reliable forms of contraception throughout study participation and for 90 days post‑last dose.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: greater than or equal to 12 months and less than 18 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: high‑grade glioma (non‑pontine locations). Histology allowed: anaplastic astrocytoma, glioblastoma, high‑grade glioma NOT OTHERWISE SPECIFIED, and H3 K27M‑wild‑type. Cancer burden allowed: localized disease without distant/metastatic dissemination. Prior treatment required: none (surgery and/or corticosteroids permitted). Prior treatment excluded: any prior systemic anticancer therapy, prior cranial/spinal irradiation, prior investigational agent, other concomitant anticancer agents. Biomarkers required: H3 K27M wild‑type and BRAF V600 wild‑type and IDH1 wild‑type. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Patients with uncontrolled infections.
Patients who have undergone prior solid organ transplantation.
Patients with grade > 1 extrapyramidal movement disorders.
Patients with known macular degeneration, uncontrolled glaucoma, or cataracts.
Patients with documented metastatic disease.
Patients with gliomatosis cerebri type 1 or 2 (unless H3 K27M‑mutant bithalamic).
Patients unable to receive prescribed radiation therapy.
Pregnant women.
Breastfeeding mothers (unless agreeing not to nurse).
Women of child‑bearing potential without a recent negative pregnancy test.
Individuals of reproductive potential not willing to use two reliable forms of contraception throughout study participation and for 90 days post‑last dose.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: non‑squamous. Cancer burden allowed: Stage IV (metastatic) disease with at least one meas­urable lesion per RECIST 1.1; stable treated brain metastases permissible. Prior treatment required: No prior systemic therapy for metastatic disease (previous platinum‑based adjuvant, neoadjuvant, or definitive chemoradiation allowable if >6 months elapsed). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: STK11 mutation or KEAP1 mutation or KRAS mutation. Biomarkers excluded: Activating epidermal growth factor receptor mutation and ALK fusion.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of acute or uncontrolled illness or allogeneic organ transplantation.
Mixed small cell lung cancer and non‑small cell lung cancer histology.
Major surgical procedure within 28 days preceding study drug administration or planned during study.
Active or prior autoimmune or inflammatory disorders requiring systemic immunosuppression (excluding vitiligo, alopecia, stable hypothyroidism, dietary‑only celiac disease).
Medical contraindication to platinum‑doublet chemotherapy.
Other primary malignant neoplasm diagnosed within 2 years (except adequately treated non‑melanoma skin cancer or in‑situ disease).
Persistent grade ≥ 2 adverse events attributable to prior anticancer therapy (excluding alopecia and vitiligo).
Grade ≤ 2 peripheral neuropathy allowed at investigator’s discretion; higher grades excluded.
Spinal cord compression unless asymptomatic and stable.
Significant cardiovascular disease (symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, clinically significant arrhythmias, uncontrolled atrial fibrillation, sustained ventricular tachyarrhythmia).
Concurrent receipt of chemotherapy, investigational agents, biological or hormonal antitumour therapy.
Radiation therapy exceeding protocol allowances (unless definitive >6 months prior, palliative brain irradiation meeting stability criteria, or limited palliative bone irradiation involving <30 % marrow).
Untreated or progressive brain metastases at screening.
Leptomeningeal carcinomatosis.
Positive testing for active Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection.
Active hepatitis B surface antigen positivity, active hepatitis C viremia, or unresolved hepatitis D coinfection.
Human immunodeficiency virus infection that is not virologically suppressed.
Use of systemic immunosuppressive medications within 14 days (exceptions noted for localized/topical steroids, physiological glucocorticoid dosing ≤10 mg prednisolone daily, peri‑procedure prophylaxis, or single-dose palliation).
Receipt of live attenuated vaccination within 30 days.
Enrollment in another interventional clinical trial (observational studies exempt).
Known hypersensitivity to study drugs or excipients.
Pregnancy, lactation, or plans to conceive; inadequate contraceptive measures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: glioblastoma, World Health Organization grade 4, IDH‐wildtype (includes gliosarcoma variant). Histology allowed: IDH‑wildtype glioblastoma or gliosarcoma. Cancer burden allowed: recurrent or progressively growing disease with meas­urable lesion according to RANO 2.0, no more than two prior relapses, no diffuse leptomeningeal dissemination, no extracranial disease, Karnofsky Performance Status ≥60. Prior treatment required: prior cranial radiotherapy (with or without chemotherapy). Prior treatment excluded: prior exposure to troriluzole or riluzole. Biomarkers required: tumor demonstrated IDH1/2 wild‑type status by certified DNA sequencing assay. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of serious pulmonary conditions such as interstitial lung disease. Significant cardiovascular disease (New York Heart Association Class > IIb) or prior receipt of cardiotoxic chemotherapies without satisfactory functional assessment. Baseline laboratory abnormalities: absolute neutrophil count <1500/mm³, platelets <100 000/mm³, hemoglobin <9 g/dL, AST or ALT >1.5×upper limit of normal, total bilirubin >2×ULN (except permissible Gilberts’ scenario), serum creatinine >1.5 mg/dL or CrCl <60 ml/min. Pregnancy or nursing. Positive beta‑hCG in individuals assigned female at birth. Current untreated or symptomatic infectious disease needing systemic therapy. Ongoing use of strong CYP1A2 modulators or hepatotoxic drugs incompatible with study drug metabolism. Contraindications to magnetic resonance imaging. Recent major surgery (<28 days). Uncontrolled or symptomatic brain metastasis (though irrelevant for primary brain tumour context). Severe, acute, or chronic medical or psychiatric illness compromising safe participation or data integrity. Conditions impairing ability to swallow or absorb oral medication.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: locally‑advanced, unresectable, or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: no more than three prior systemic cytotoxic chemotherapy regimens (including antibody‑drug conjugates) for locally‑advanced/metastatic breast cancer; if known BRCA mutation, must have received a poly (ADP‑ribose) polymerase inhibitor where available and not medically contraindicated; must have documented disease progression on or after, or intolerance to, fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan, sacituzumab govitecan, or other topoisomerase I inhibitor therapies if available as standard of care; for hormone‑receptor‑positive tumours: must have shown intolerance to endocrine therapy or endocrine‐therapy‑resistant disease defined as either (a) progression on ≥ two lines of endocrine therapy for locally‑advanced/metastatic breast cancer together with prior cyclin‑dependent kinase 4/6 inhibition in the adjuvant or metastatic setting, **or** (b) progression on one line of endocrine therapy for locally‑advanced/metastatic disease combined with relapse while on adjuvant endocrine therapy after definitive surgery and having received a CDK4/6 inhibitor in the adjuvant or advanced setting; for hormone‑receptor‑negative, HER2‑low, programmed death ligand‑1‑positive (combined positive score ≥ 10) tumours: must have received pembrolizumab (or another PD‑(L)1 inhibitor) in combination with chemotherapy if available and not medically contraindicated. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: HER2‑low status (immunohistochemistry 1⁺ or immunohistochemistry 2⁺ with ISH‑negative) and appropriate hormonal‑receptor or PD‑L1 status as stipulated for the corresponding tumour subset. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Known hypersensitivity to any excipients in disitamab vedotin or tucatinib.; Prior therapy with antibody‑drug conjugates bearing monomethyl auristatin E payload.; Prior therapy with tucatinib.; Presence of active central nervous system or leptomeningeal metastasis.; History of another invasive malignancy within three years preceding enrollment or any residual disease from a previously diagnosed cancer.; Inability to swallow oral tablets or capsular formulations or existence of clinically significant gastrointestinal disorder compromising adequate oral medication absorption.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Non‑ocular, non‑uveal melanoma (includes cutaneous, mucosal, and other primary sites). Histology allowed: Malignant melanoma (any histologic subtype except ocular/uveal). Cancer burden allowed: Unresectable stage IIIb, IIIc, IIId or stage IV melanoma. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Any systemic anticancer therapy for metastatic disease (including immune checkpoint inhibitors, BRAF/MEK inhibitors, cytotoxic chemotherapy, radiopharmaceuticals). Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Patients with a history of pneumonitis.
Patients with symptomatic congestive heart failure.
Patients with clinically significant renal dysfunction.
Patients with moderate-to-severe hepatic dysfunction.
Patients with uncontrolled brain metastases.
Patients infected with human immunodeficiency virus or chronic hepatitis B or C.
Patients with Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status greater than or equal to 2.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >6 months to <18 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: Mature B‑cell non‑Hodgkin lymphoma. Histology allowed: Burkitt lymphoma or mature B‑cell leukemia (FAB L3) or diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma or primary mediastinal B‑cell lymphoma. Cancer burden allowed: First relapsed or refractory disease with measurable lesions. Prior treatment required: Received standard first‑line chemoimmunotherapy (rituximab‑based regimen). Prior treatment excluded: Glofitamab. Biomarkers required: CD20‑positive tumour (IHC or flow cytometry). Biomarkers excluded: NA.

Age range allowed: >6 months to ≤30 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: Mature B‑cell non‑Hodgkin lymphoma. Histology allowed: Burkitt lymphoma or mature B‑cell leukemia (FAB L3) or diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma or primary mediatinal B‑cell lymphoma. Cancer burden allowed: Second or higher relapsed or refractory disease after at least two prior systemic chemoimmunotherapy regimens (all established therapies exhausted) with measurable lesions. Prior treatment required: At minimum two prior systemic chemoimmunotherapy regimens (rituximab‑based). Prior treatment excluded: Glofitamab. Biomarkers required: CD20‑positive tumour (IHC or flow cytometry). Biomarkers excluded: NA.

Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Histology allowed: mature B‑cell neoplasm consistent with chronic lymphocytic leukemia/Small lymphocytic lymphoma. Cancer burden allowed: measurably elevated circulating disease (absolute lymphocyte count >10 ×10⁹/L **or** total white blood cell count ≥20 ×10⁹/L); does not meet International Workshop on Chronic Lymphocytic Leukaemia 2018 criteria for immediate treatment (asymptomatic, early‑stage disease); no palpable lymphadenopathy greater than 5 cm longest diameter; no documented Richter transformation; no central nervous system involvement; lymphocyte doubling time ≥6 months if white blood cell count >30 ×10⁹/L; serum immunoglobulin concentration ≥400 mg/dL and not reliant on chronic intravenous immunoglobulin supplementation; no uncontrolled autoimmune cytopenia. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: any prior chronic lymphocytic leukemia‑directed therapy—including chemotherapeutic regimens, Bruton tyrosine kinase or phosphoinositide‑3‑kinase delta inhibitors, anti‑CD20 monoclonal antibodies, radiotherapy, or hormonally based CLL therapies; prior exposure to programmed death‑1/programmed death ligand‑1/language‑2 checkpoint inhibitors; preceding autologous or allogenic hematopoietic stem cell or bone‑marrow transplantation; enrollment in other investigational therapeutic protocols. Biomarkers required: IGHV gene segment unmutated (≤2 % divergence from germline sequence) confirmed by molecular testing. Biomarkers excluded: deletion of chromosome 17p involving ≥10 % of malignant cells identified by fluorescent in‑situ hybridisation.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of clinically significant pneumonitis or acute respiratory distress syndromes; uncontrolled cardiovascular disease such as symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, or life‑threatening arrhythmias; hepatic insufficiency exceeding twice the institutional upper limit for transaminases or bilirubin elevation; renal impairment resulting in creatinine clearance less than institution‑defined threshold; unresolved or progressing intracranial/metastatic brain lesions; active infection with Human Immunodeficiency Virus, Hepatitis B virus, or Hepatitis C virus; inability to comply with study procedures owing to psychiatric, cognitive, or social factors; pregnancy or breastfeeding; contraindication to receive live or experimental vaccines; hypersensitivity reaction to any component of the investigational products (neoantigen peptide mixture, poly‑ICLC, cyclophosphamide, pembrolizumab).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Solid tumor. Histology allowed: Any. Cancer burden allowed: Advanced, relapsed or refractory disease (patients may have non‑central nervous system metastases; active central nervous system metastases are excluded, whereas previously treated, radiographically stable CNS lesions are permitted). Prior treatment required: Disease refractory to all locally available standard therapies (implies prior systemic anticancer therapy). Prior treatment excluded: Previous administration of any CD24‑directed agent **or** prior autologous or allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant **or** prior solid organ transplant. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.
Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease (e.g., New York Heart Association class III–IV heart failure, unstable angina, recent myocardial infarction).
Significant hepatic impairment (bilirubin > 1.5 times ULN despite allowances) or pancreatic insufficiency.
Severe renal impairment (creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min).
Active, uncontrolled central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease.
Known human immunodeficiency virus infection or chronic hepatitis B/C infection with detectable viral load.
Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or unwillingness to use adequate contraception.
Performance status worse than Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group grade 2.
Concurrent enrollment in another interventional oncology trial or receiving concomitant antineoplastic therapy not part of protocol.
Clinically significant laboratory abnormalities outside protocol-specified ranges not already covered in inclusion criteria.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 60 years or < 60 years deemed unfit for intensive cytarabine‐based induction; Sex allowed: Male and Female; Cancer type allowed: acute myeloid leukaemia; Histology allowed: morphology consistent with WHO 2016 definition of acute myeloid leukaemia, excluding acute promyelocytic leukaemia (PML‑RARA), AML with RUNX1‑RUNX1T1 fusion, and AML with CBFB‑MYH11 fusion; Cancer burden allowed: newly diagnosed disease (no prior curative intent AML therapy); Prior treatment required: No prior AML‑directed therapy except hydroxyurea, all‑trans retinoic acid, leukapheresis, or emergency low‑dose cytarabine; Prior treatment excluded: Any prior hypomethylating agent exposure and any prior FLT3 inhibitor exposure; Biomarkers required: FLT3‑internal tandem duplication (FL​T3‑ITD) **or** FLT3‑TKD D735/D836 mutation documented by MyeloMATCH screening; Biomarkers excluded: NA.

Age range allowed: ≥ 3 years and < 30 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: central nervous system germinoma. Histology allowed: pure germinoma. Cancer burden allowed: localized disease confined to suprasellar and/or pineal region with contiguous ventricular or unifocal parenchymal extension. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: any prior tumor‐directed therapy (chemotherapy, radiation) other than surgical intervention or corticosteroids. Biomarkers required: serum or cerebrospinal fluid β‑human chorionic gonadotropin level 5‑50 mIU/mL **and** α‑fetoprotein within institutional normal limits (or ≤ 10 ng/mL if institutional normal unavailable). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of significant pulmonary fibrosis or interstitial lung disease, unresolved pneumonia, recent acute respiratory infection, congestive heart failure NYHA Class III-IV, left ventricular ejection fraction < 50%, uncontrolled hypertension, clinically significant arrhythmia, myocardial infarction within past six months, severe hepatic impairment (bilirubin > 1.5× ULN, ALT > 5× ULN), severe renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance < 60 ml/min), untreated symptomatic brain metastasis causing neurologic compromise, ongoing infections including HIV/AIDS, chronic hepatitis B or C with detectable viremia, autoimmune disorders requiring immunosuppression, concurrent malignancy requiring active therapy, inability to comply with study procedures, pregnancy or lactation, lack of adequate contraception for participants of reproductive potential.

Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Prior antibody drug conjugate containing auristatin payload OR Prior B7‑H4 targeted treatment. Biomarkers required: Estrogen receptor negative AND Progesterone receptor negative AND Human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative (triple‑negative). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
• Recent major surgery within 28 days preceding study treatment.  
• Systemic anticancer therapy administered within 28 days (or five half‐lives) of study start, or within 14 days (five half‑lives) for small‑molecule targeted agents.  
• Palliative thoracic radiation within three months or radiation to other anatomical sites within 14 days before enrollment.  
• Active second malignancy requiring treatment within two years, except adequately treated basal cell or squamous cell skin cancer, or carcinoma in situ of the breast or cervix.  
• Untreated central nervous system metastases, history of leptomeningeal spread, or carcinomatous meningitis.  
• Significant preexisting liver disease (cirrhosis, hepatic fibrosis, esophageal or gastric varices, or other clinically meaningful hepatic impairment).  
• Severe, uncontrolled systemic illnesses—including clinically significant cardiovascular, pulmonary, or metabolic disorders—and any intercurrent illness judged to raise safety risks or impede protocol assessments.  
• Clinically significant cardiovascular disease.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: solid malignancy (any primary site). Histology allowed: any histology. Cancer burden allowed: locally advanced (unresectable) or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: documented disease progression after all available standard therapy for the specific tumor type, or absence of any established standard therapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Pregnancy or breastfeeding; history of active pneumonitis; congestive heart failure class III or IV; recent (<6 months) uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmias, myocardium infarction, stroke/TIA, pericarditis, or other serious cardiovascular events; prolonged QTc (>470 ms) or congenital Long QT syndrome; significant hepatic impairment; significant renal insufficiency; uncontrolled hypertension; active uncontrolled systemic bacterial, viral, or fungal infection; HIV-positive status (unless suppressed on HAART with undetectable viral load); chronic hepatitis B infection (with exceptions based on management); active hepatitis C infection (exceptions if cured and undetectable RNA); current or recent (≤6 months) pancreatitis; resting arterial O₂ saturation <92% on ambient air or dependence on continuous supplementary oxygen; uncontrolled or symptomatic central nervous system metastases, leptomeningeal disease, or carcinomatous meningitis (stable asymptomatic brain metastases allowed after ≥1 month stability post‑radiation); requirement for high‑dose glucocorticoids (>10 mg prednisolone equivalents per day) (physiologic replacement doses allowable); concomitant participation in another interventional oncology trial; inability to safely ingest oral tablets due to gastrointestinal disorder affecting absorption; known hypersensitivity to FX‑909 or its excipients; any condition judged by investigator to compromise adherence to protocol or confound safety/efficacy evaluations.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: urothelial carcinoma. Histology allowed: urothelial carcinoma. Prior treatment required: progression after all available standard therapy (anti‑PD(L)1 antibodies, antibody‑drug conjugates, and platinum‑based doublet chemotherapy) and receipt of ≤4 prior systemic therapies for advanced or metastatic disease. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: PPARG protein expression Tumor Positivity Score ≥60% by investigational IHC assay (assessment performed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Pregnancy or breastfeeding; history of active pneumonitis; congestive heart failure class III or IV; recent (<6 months) uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmias, myocardium infarction, stroke/TIA, pericarditis, or other serious cardiovascular events; prolonged QTc (>470 ms) or congenital Long QT syndrome; significant hepatic impairment; significant renal insufficiency; uncontrolled hypertension; active uncontrolled systemic bacterial, viral, or fungal infection; HIV-positive status (unless suppressed on HAART with undetectable viral load); chronic hepatitis B infection (with exceptions based on management); active hepatitis C infection (exceptions if cured and undetectable RNA); current or recent (≤6 months) pancreatitis; resting arterial O₂ saturation <92% on ambient air or dependence on continuous supplementary oxygen; uncontrolled or symptomatic central nervous system metastases, leptomeningeal disease, or carcinomatous meningitis (stable asymptomatic brain metastases allowed after ≥1 month stability post‑radiation); requirement for high‑dose glucocorticoids (>10 mg prednisolone equivalents per day) (physiologic replacement doses allowable); concomitant participation in another interventional oncology trial; inability to safely ingest oral tablets due to gastrointestinal disorder affecting absorption; known hypersensitivity to FX‑909 or its excipients; any condition judged by investigator to compromise adherence to protocol or confound safety/efficacy evaluations.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of serious pulmonary toxicity such as pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease. Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease including symptomatic heart failure, myocardial infarction within six months, arrhythmias, or cardiac functional class poorer than New York Heart Association Class IIb. Significant hepatic impairment (bilirubin >1.5× ULN, transaminases >5× ULN despite allowances for liver metastases) or renal impairment (creatinine clearance <60 mL/min). Active, untreated central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease. Ongoing infections including uncontrolled HIV, chronic hepatitis B or C, or other opportunistic pathogens. Autoimmune disorders requiring systemic corticosteroids greater than physiologic doses. Current pregnancy or breastfeeding. Known hypersensitivity to azenoshetib or structurally related compounds. Concurrent administration of strong cytochrome P450 3A4 enzyme modulators. Dependence on enteral/parenteral nutritional support preventing safe oral intake. Recent major surgical procedure (within 28 days) or minor procedure (within 7 days) interfering with study assessments. Performance status worse than Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group grade 2.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Injectable tumor located insufficient distance from critical structures (major airways, neurovascular bundles).  
Known central nervous system metastases or leptomeningebral carcinomatosis unless adequately treated and clinically stable off steroids for ≥14 days.  
Severe infection requiring systemic intravenous antibiotics or inpatient care near the time of first injection.  
Requirement for systemic immunosuppressive medication exceeding 10 mg prednisone-equivalent daily (one-time pulses exempt).  
Active autoimmune disease necessitating systemic immunosuppression.  
Presence of active lung disease or pneumonitis.  
History of Grade 4 toxicities resulting from prior PD‑1 pathway inhibitors.  
Any additional malignancy anticipated to need therapeutic intervention within the subsequent 24 months (excluding basal/squamous cell skin cancers, non‑invasive cervical cancer, and localized prostate cancer).  
Active infection with Mycobacterium tuberculosis, human immunodeficiency virus, hepatitis B virus, or hepatitis C virus.  
Pregnancy or current breastfeeding.  
Significant cardiovascular disorders: myocardial infarction ≤12 months before enrollment, unstable angina, or New York Heart Association Class III‑IV congestive heart failure occurring ≤6 months before enrollment.  
Medical or psychiatric condition rendering safe study conduct unreasonable.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: any. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: any malignant neoplasm. Histology allowed: any. Cancer burden allowed: NA. Prior treatment required: participation in a qualifying parent pembrolizumab‑based study with receipt of pembrolizumab monotherapy, pembrolizumab‑based combination therapy, or lenvatinib (per protocol arm assignment). general_exclusion_criteria:
Severe hypersensitivity (grade ≥ 3) to pembrolizumab or its excipients.  
Receipt of a live vaccine within 30 days prior to first dose of second‑course treatment.  
Diagnosis of immunodeficiency or ongoing systemic glucocorticoids (>10 mg/day prednisone equivalent) or other immunosuppressive therapy within 7 days of Cycle 1 Day 1.  
Presence of an additionally diagnosed malignancy that is progressing or requires active treatment (early-stage exceptions apply).  
Known active central nervous system metastases or carcinomatous meningitis.  
Active autoimmune disease that has required systemic treatment in the past 2 years (replacement hormone therapy exempt).  
History of non‑infectious pneumonitis requiring steroids or current pneumonitis.  
Interstitial lung disease in non‑small cell lung cancer participants.  
Active infection requiring systemic therapy.  
Positive history or testing for Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection.  
Positive history or testing for hepatitis B or hepatitis C (parent‑study rules applied).  
Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or plans to conceive or cause conception during the study period and until 120 days after last dose.  
Severe cardiovascular disease (significant arrhythmias, NYHA class III/IV congestive heart failure, symptomatic ischemic heart disease).  
Hepatic decompensation (Child‑Pugh score > 6).  
Uncontrolled thyroid dysfunction.  
Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus.  
Previous allogeneic tissue or solid organ transplant.  
Active tuberculosis infection.  
Major surgery performed ≤3 weeks before first dose of lenvatinib.  
Pre‑existing grade ≥ 3 gastrointestinal or nongastrointestinal fistula (lenvatinib).  
Urine protein excretion ≥1 g/24 hours (lenvatinib).  
Left ventricular ejection fraction below institutional normal limits (lenvatinib).  
Radiographic evidence of major blood‑vessel encasement/invasion or intratumoral cavitation (lenvatinib).  
Corrected QT interval (Fridericia) >480 milliseconds (lenvatinib).  
Clinically significant cardiovascular event within 12 months preceding first dose (lenvatinib).  
Conditions impairing oral absorption of lenvatinib (e.g., gastrointestinal malabsorption).  
Recent active hemoptysis occurring within 3 weeks before first dose (lenvatinib).  
Hypersensitivity to any component of lenvatinib.

general_exclusion_criteria:
• Concurrent active antitumor therapy for glioblastoma outside the protocol.
• Extensive leptomeningeal disease.
• History of another active malignant neoplasm within the preceding 3 years.
• Significant cardiovascular disease (heart failure, recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina).
• Uncontrolled hypertension.
• Clinically meaningful pulmonary toxicity or interstitial lung disease.
• Severe hepatic insufficiency (bilirubin > 2× ULN or AST/ALT > 5× ULN) or severe renal impairment (creatinine clearance <30 ml/min).
• Known human immunodeficiency virus infection or chronic hepatitis B or C requiring ongoing antiviral therapy.
• Pregnant or breastfeeding individuals.
• Poor functional status (performance score lower than stipulated in the respective arm).

Prior treatment required: Minimum of three core-agent exposures as noted. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Smoldering multiple myeloma; non‑secretory multiple myeloma lacking measurable M‑protein/FLC; active systemic AL amyloidosis; active plasma cell leukemia; recent (<14 day) red blood cell or platelet transfusions or growth factor support (arm‑specific windows omitted here); recent (>7 day) platelet transfusion, G‑CSF, or PEG‑FILGRASTIM; any tumor‑directed therapy within 2 weeks (radiation, chemo, immuno) or radio‑immunotherapy within 6 weeks; prior investigational anticancer therapy within 3 weeks; recent autologous stem cell transplant (<1 month) or allo‑transplant (<3 months); ongoing graft‑versus‑host disease; expected survival < 3 months; major surgery within 4 weeks; uncontrolled cardiovascular disease (symptomatic ischemia, NYHA III/IV CHF, MI < 3 months, EF < 50 %; arrhythmias causing instability); uncontrolled hypertension; active infection requiring IV antimicrobial therapy; known active hepatitis A, B, or C; HIV positivity; significant gastrointestinal disorder impairing tablet ingestion/absorption; current pregnancy or lactation; untreated/uncontrolled central nervous system disease; grade > 2 baseline neuropathy or painful grade ≥ 2 neuropathy (arms 2, 4, 8); intolerance/allergy to any study drug components; ocular surface disease preventing safe administration of belantamab mafodotin; history of allogeneic stem cell or solid organ transplant (for arm 12); hypersensitivity to thalidomide analogues, carfilzomib, dexamethasone, or related excipients; refusal/inability to abstain from blood donation per schedule; inability/refusal to take mandated thrombosis prophylaxis; use of strong/moderate CYP3A4 modulators or PPIs near initiation; concurrent malignant disease requiring active therapy (excluding appropriately treated superficial skin cancers, cervical/carci­noma in situ, or localized prostate cancer); detectable hepatitis B DNA viremia; inability to follow required anti‑emetogenic prophylaxis.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Locally advanced or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: Receipt of standard therapies for non‑small cell lung cancer. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Epidermal growth factor receptor mutation (any class including classic, atypical, exon 20 insertion) **or** Human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 kinase domain mutation (in exons 18, 19, 20, 21); biomarker testing performed during screening. Biomarkers excluded: Presence of other oncogenic driver alterations (e.g., moderate or high MET amplification) **or** histologic transformation to small cell carcinoma; biomarker testing performed during screening.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of any concurrent malignancy within the previous 2 years.
Unresolved toxicities from prior therapies.
History of interstitial lung disease from any cause.
Clinically significant cardiovascular event within 6 months or significant cardiac history.
Active participation in another investigational therapeutic study.
Any significant and uncontrolled medical condition, such as acute infection.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: Urothelial carcinoma of the bladder, ureter, renal pelvis, or urethra. Histology allowed: Predominantly urothelial carcinoma (variant histology permissible if urothelial component dominant; any amount of squamous differentiation allowed except pure squamous cell carcinoma). Cancer burden allowed: Post‑radical surgery disease‑free state with no gross residual tumour or distant metastasis; microscopic positive margins accepted; no measurable disease by RECIST 1.1. Prior treatment required: Radical cystectomy, nephroureterctomy, or ureterectomy performed 3 weeks to 12 weeks prior to pre‑registration; no neoadjuvant systemic therapy; cisplatin‑ineligible (creatinine clearance < 60 mL/min, grade ≥ 2 auditory/hearing loss, grade ≥ 2 peripheral neuropathy, NYHA class III heart failure, or ECOG = 2) **or** cisplatin‑eligible but declining/planning to forego adjuvant cisplatin‐based chemotherapy. Prior treatment excluded: Prior receipt of any LAG‑3 blocking antibody; prior adjuvant systemic therapy after surgery; prior adjuvant radiation therapy after surgery; concomitant investigational oncology drug administered within a washout interval (ignored per instructions). Biomarkers required: Availability of tumour tissue for central Signatera circulating tumour DNA testing with a reported result (positive or negative). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of clinically significant pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease; uncontrolled congestive heart failure (NYHA class II or greater) or recent acute coronary syndromes; left ventricular ejection fraction markedly reduced; ongoing dialysis dependence; active uncontrolled hypertension; serious arrhythmias; active or unresolved autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression exceeding low‑dose glucocorticoids; prior grade ≥ 3 immune‑related adverse events from checkpoint inhibitors; active infectious hepatitis B or C not adequately suppressed; HIV infection with detectable viral load; inability to comply with protocol assessments; Pregnant or breastfeeding individuals; inadequate bone marrow reserve (absolute neutrophil count < 1200/mm³, platelets < 100 × 109/L, hemoglobin < 8 g/dL); hepatic insufficiency (bilirubin > 1.5 × ULN, AST/ALT > 3 × ULN); renal impairment beyond stipulated limits (creatinine clearance ≤ 30 ml/min); performance status worse than ECOG 2; simultaneous participation in another therapeutic clinical trial; receiving live vaccinations within 30 days preceding registration.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: Urothelinal carcinoma of the bladder, ureter, renal pelvis, or urethra. Histology allowed: Predominantly urothelial carcinoma (variants permitted as above). Cancer burden allowed: As in space 1 (post‑operative disease‑free, no macro­scopic residual disease or metastasis). Prior treatment required: Radical cystectomy, nephroureterectomy, or ureterectomy performed 3 weeks to 12 weeks prior to pre‑registration; no neoadjuvant systemic therapy; pathological stage pT2 N0 on the surgical specimen; central Signatera circulating tumour DNA test must be positive. Prior treatment excluded: Prior receipt of any LAG‑3 blocking antibody; prior adjuvant systemic therapy after surgery; prior adjuvant radiation therapy after surgery; concomitant investigational oncology drug administered within a washout interval (ignore). Biomarkers required: Central Signatera ctDNA positive result (required for eligibility). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of clinically significant pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease; uncontrolled congestive heart failure (NYHA class II or greater) or recent acute coronary syndromes; left ventricular ejection fraction markedly reduced; ongoing dialysis dependence; active uncontrolled hypertension; serious arrhythmias; active or unresolved autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression exceeding low‑dose glucocorticoids; prior grade ≥ 3 immune‑related adverse events from checkpoint inhibitors; active infectious hepatitis B or C not adequately suppressed; HIV infection with detectable viral load; inability to comply with protocol assessments; Pregnant or breastfeeding individuals; inadequate bone marrow reserve (absolute neutrophil count < 1200/mm³, platelets < 100 × 109/L, hemoglobin < 8 g/dL); hepatic insufficiency (bilirubin > 1.5 × ULN, AST/ALT > 3 × ULN); renal impairment beyond stipulated limits (creatinine clearance ≤ 30 ml/min); performance status worse than ECOG 2; simultaneous participation in another therapeutic clinical trial; receiving live vaccinations within 30 days preceding registration.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: Urothelional carcinoma of the bladder, ureter, renal pelvis, or urethra. Histology allowed: Predominantly urothelial carcinoma (variant allowance as above). Cancer burden allowed: Post‑radical surgery disease‑free state with no visible metastasis; microscopically involved margins allowable; no measurable disease by RECIST 1.1. Prior treatment required: Radical cystectomy, nephroureterectomy, or ureterectomy performed 3 weeks to 12 weeks prior to pre‑registration; receipt of neoadjuvant systemic therapy (which may consist of cisplatin‑based chemotherapy alone, cisplatin‑plus PD‑1/PD‑L1 inhibition, or enfortumab vedotin ＋ PD‑1/PD‑L1 inhibition); pathological findings of ypT2 through ypT4a and/or ypN+ on the surgical specimen. Prior treatment excluded: Prior receipt of any LAG‑3 blocking antibody; prior adjuvant systemic therapy after surgery; prior adjuvant radiation therapy after surgery; concomitant investigational oncology drug administered within a washout interval (ignore). Biomarkers required: Central Signatera circulating tumour DNA test result (positive or negative). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of clinically significant pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease; uncontrolled congestive heart failure (NYHA class II or greater) or recent acute coronary syndromes; left ventricular ejection fraction markedly reduced; ongoing dialysis dependence; active uncontrolled hypertension; serious arrhythmias; active or unresolved autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression exceeding low‑dose glucocorticoids; prior grade ≥ 3 immune‑related adverse events from checkpoint inhibitors; active infectious hepatitis B or C not adequately suppressed; HIV infection with detectable viral load; inability to comply with protocol assessments; Pregnant or breastfeeding individuals; inadequate bone marrow reserve (absolute neutrophil count < 1200/mm³, platelets < 100 × 109/L, hemoglobin < 8 g/dL); hepatic insufficiency (bilirubin > 1.5 × ULN, AST/ALT > 3 × ULN); renal impairment beyond stipulated limits (creatinine clearance ≤ 30 ml/min); performance status worse than ECOG 2; simultaneous participation in another therapeutic clinical trial; receiving live vaccinations within 30 days preceding registration.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: > 18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Triple negative invasive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Residual invasive disease in the breast or regional lymph nodes after neoadjuvant therapy and surgery; no distant metastasis. Prior treatment required: Completion of neoadjuvant systemic therapy, definitive surgery, and postoperative radiotherapy with sufficient recovery. Prior treatment excluded: Prior exposure to any CTLA‑4 antagonist, OX‑40 agonist, or CD137 agonist; any HER2‑directed therapy; endocrine therapy exceeding 4 weeks duration or concurrent endocrine therapy during study treatment; any topoisomerase Ⅰ inhibitor; any antibody‑drug conjugate containing a topoisomerase Ⅰ inhibitor. Biomarkers required: Estrogen receptor ≤10% **and** Progesterone receptor ≤10% **and** Human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative (per ASCO/CAP IHC/ISH). Biomarkers excluded: Documented deleterious germline BRCA1 or BRCA2 mutation.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of myocardial infarction or unstable angina within six months, serious ventricular arrhythmia (ventricular tachycardia or ventricular fibrillation), high‑grade atrioventricular block, other significant cardiac arrhythmias, left ventricular ejection fraction less than 50%. Active serious infection requiring antimicrobial therapy.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: solid tumor (all sites). Histology allowed: any solid‐tumour histology. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic disease or locally advanced unresectable disease. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: previous PI3K/AKT/mTOR inhibitor exposure. Biomarkers required: documented phosphoinositide‑3‑kinase catalytic subunit alpha (PI3Kα) mutation identified in tumor tissue obtained at screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of a solid tumor or haematological malignancy distinct from the current cancer diagnosed within the preceding 2 years.; Presence of symptomatic brain or spinal cord metastases.; Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus (HbA1c ≥ 8% or fasting glucose ≥ 140 mg/dL or requiring insulin.).; Ongoing adverse effects from prior anticancer therapy greater than CTCAE grade 1 (with exceptions for alopecia and peripheral neuropathy).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Female (breast tissue primarily occurs in women; however trial permits males with breast cancer). Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: hormone‑receptor–positive invasive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic disease or locally advanced unresectable disease. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: previous PI3K/AKT/mTOR inhibitor exposure. Biomarkers required: documented PI3Kα mutation identified in tumor tissue obtained at screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: bronchial neuroendocrine tumor. Histology allowed: well‑differentiated or moderately‑differentiated neuroendocrine tumor AND low‑grade or intermediate‑grade AND typical carcinoid or atypical carcinoid. Cancer burden allowed: advanced disease (recurring, locally advanced/unresectable, or metastatic) WITH measurable disease per RECIST v1.1 AND documented radiological disease progression within the last 12 months. Prior treatment required: None mandatory; IF prior systemic anticancer therapy was given THEN disease progression on that therapy must have been demonstrated. Prior treatment excluded: peptide receptor radionuclide therapy (any lutetium Lu‑177 dotatate) AND mammalian target of rapamycin inhibitors (e.g., everolimus, temsirolimus, sirolimus, deforolimus). Biomarkers required: somatostatin receptor positivity on ^68Ga‑DOTATATE PET (tumor uptake greater than background liver) CONFIRMED by central imaging core laboratory during screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
- Known central nervous system metastases unless treated and clinically stable for ≥14 days.
- Presence of another actively progressing malignant disease requiring therapy (excludes non‑melanoma skin cancers or in‑situ carcinomas).
- Active hepatitis B infection (HBsAg positive) or active hepatitis C infection (detectable RNA), unless hepatitis B is suppressed with undetectable viral load on antiviral therapy.
- Uncontrolled HIV infection (must be on antiretroviral therapy with undetectable viral load).
- Current serious bacterial, fungal, or viral infection requiring antimicrobial therapy within 3 days before enrollment.
- Receipt of live attenuated vaccination within 7 days prior to registration.
- Decompensated liver cirrhosis.
- Prior drug‑induced pneumonitis that was symptomatic or required treatment.
- Medical conditions impairing swallowing or gastrointestinal absorption affecting oral agents.
- Known hypersensitivity to everolimus or other rapamycin analogues.
- Requirement for chronic coadministration of strong P‑glycoprotein modulators or potent CYP3A4 inhibitors/inducers without appropriate discontinuation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: World Health Organization grade IV glioblastoma and variants (gliosarcoma, glioblastoma with oligodendroglial features, giant cell glioblastoma, astrocytoma with molecular features of glioblastoma, IDH‑mutated glioblastoma). Histology allowed: as above. Cancer burden allowed: newly diagnosed, postoperative residual enhancing tumor ≤1 cm max diameter, predominately supratentorial, no diffuse leptomeningeal dissemination, no predominant infratentorial/spinal cord disease. Prior treatment required: surgical resection (gross‐total or subtotal) and candidacy for conventional external‑beam radiotherapy (to be administered per protocol). Prior treatment excluded: temozolomide, any anti‑PD‑1/PD‑L1/PD‑L2 therapy, other systemic anticancer agents, stereotactic radiosurgery, carmustine wafer implantation, Optune® tumour treating fields, other investigational therapeutics, systemic corticosteroids within 5 days before radiation start. Biomarkers required: adequate tumor specimen for whole exome/DNA sequencing; MGMT promoter methylation status recorded (not prerequisite for enrolment). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of severe allergy to any vaccine product; active, known, or suspected autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppressive therapy within the past two years (excluding controlled vitiligo, Type 1 diabetes, thyroid hormone replacement, or mild psoriasis); chronic viral infections with untreated HIV, hepatitis B, or hepatitis C; uncontrolled intercurrent illness such as active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, or significant cardiac arrhythmia; pregnant or breastfeeding persons; inability/unwillingness to comply with effective contraception requirements; prior exposure to anti‑PD‑1, anti‑PD‑L1, or anti‑PD‑L2 agents; recent receipt (≤30 days) of live vaccinations; existing non‑infectious pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease; active central nervous system metastases or carcinomatous meningitis; simultaneous participation in another interventional oncology trial within 4 weeks of study start; unresolved toxicities (>Grade 1) from prior anticancer therapy; inadequate organ function contrary to protocol specifications.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: World Health Organization grade IV glioblastoma and variants (gliosarcoma, glioblastoma with oligodendroglial features, giant cell glioblastoma, astrocytoma with molecular features of glioblastoma, IDH‑mutated glioblastoma). Histology allowed: as above. Cancer burden allowed: newly diagnosed, postoperative residual enhancing tumor ≤1 cm max diameter, predominately supratentorial, no diffuse leptomeningeal dissemination, no predominant infratentorial/spinal cord disease. Prior treatment required: surgical resection and commencement of conventional external‑beam radiotherapy together with NeoVax vaccination; pembrolizumab to begin within 2 weeks of radiotherapy start and continue every 3 weeks for up to 2 years. clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Solid tumor. Histology allowed: Any solid tumor histology. Cancer burden allowed: Advanced or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: At least one prior line of systemic anticancer therapy. Prior treatment excluded: Prior exposure to a MAT2A inhibitor or PRMT inhibitor or sacituzumab govitecan. Biomarkers required: Presence of homozygous loss (deletion) of MTAP confirmed by testing performed during screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Known symptomatic brain metastases  
Known primary central nervous system malignant neoplasm  
Active liver or biliary disease  
Impairment of gastrointestinal function affecting oral intake or absorption  
Active untreated/uncontrolled infection  
Clinically significant cardiovascular abnormalities (e.g., recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, congestive heart failure NYHA class III–IV)  
Radiation therapy involving more than 25 percent of total bone-marrow volume  
Concurrent use of strong CYP3A4/5 enzyme inhibitors or inducer medications expected to affect study drug metabolism  
Receipt of another investigational medicinal product concurrently

general_exclusion_criteria: History or presence of actively symptomatic central nervous system metastasis or primary central nervous system cancer.; Clinically relevant cardiovascular abnormalities.; Major surgical procedure performed within 28 days before starting study medication.; Systemic arterial thrombotic or embolic events occurring within 6 months preceding enrollment.; Venous thrombotic events (deep vein thrombosis or pulmonary embolism) occurring within 1 month preceding enrollment.; Known allergy or hypersensitivity to components of the investigational product.; Significant malabsorptive disorder affecting oral drug uptake.; Ongoing concomitant malignancy that requires therapeutic intervention.; Poor overall health conditions deemed medically unsuitable by investigator.
