clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Triple negative breast cancer. Histology allowed: Carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Advanced/metastatic or unresectable disease. Prior treatment required: Disease refractory to or relapsed after standard therapy. Prior treatment excluded: None applicable. Biomarkers required: Estrogen receptor < 1% **and** Progesterone receptor < 1% **and** HER2 score 0‑1⁺ or non‑amplified (**assessed via local testing**). Biomarkers excluded: Presence of PI3K pathway activating alterations (inactivating PTEN or PIK3R1 mutations/deletions, PIK3CA amplification, activating PIK3CA/Akt/mTOR mutations).
general_exclusion_criteria:
• Residual toxicities greater than Grade 1 from prior anticancer therapy (with exceptions for alopecia and peripheral neuropathy).
• Concurrent receipt of any other investigational agents.
• Allergic reaction to compounds chemically or biochemically similar to ZEN003694 or binimetinib.
• Requirement for therapeutic‐dose anticoagulation.
• Current use of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or inducers (unless stopped ≥7 days before study start).
• Use of medicines that significantly prolong the QT interval.
• Uncontrolled intercurrent acute illness.
• Significant psychiatric disorder or social situation limiting adherence to study protocols.
• Pregnancy or lactation; inadequate contraceptive measures.
• Cardiovascular events (stroke, myocardial infarction, unstable angina) within six months preceding enrolment.
• Conditions compromising oral drug absorption (gastrectomy, intestinal bypass, chronic diarrhoea, gastroparesis).
• History of retinal vein occlusion.
• Past episode of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Any malignant neoplasm for which an approved epidermal growth factor receptor inhibitor is indicated (for example, colorectal carcinoma, non‑small cell lung cancer, pancreatic adenocarcinoma, head and neck squamous cell carcinoma, bile duct carcinoma, among others). Histology allowed: Any histologic subtype suitable for epidermal growth factor receptor inhibitor therapy. Cancer burden allowed: Currently receiving an epidermal growth factor receptor inhibitor; development of papular and/or pustular eruptive rash or cutaneous burning attributable to the inhibitor, graded ≤ 3 by CTCAE and ARIGA, involving ≤ 30 % total body‐surface area; Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status 0‑2; predicted life expectancy ≥3 months. Prior treatment required: Ongoing treatment with an approved epidermal growth factor receptor inhibitor for cancer. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of severe cutaneous toxicity (CTCAE grade 4 or >30 % BSA involvement).
Active untreated or unstable infection.
Concurrent or recent (<4 weeks) radiation therapy to head, neck, or upper torso overlapping with epidermal growth factor receptor inhibitor therapy.
Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or plans to conceive during the study period.
Significant hematologic abnormalities (ANC <1000/mm³, platelets <50,000/mm³, WBC <3000/mm³).
Liver function tests exceeding 2.5× ULN for AST/ALT or bilirubin >1.5× ULN.
Renal impairment with creatinine >1.5× ULN.
Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease, congestive heart failure, or clinically significant pulmonary disease.
Known hypersensitivity to components of the investigational product.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: classical Hodgkin lymphoma. Histology allowed: classical Hodgkin lymphoma (according to WHO classification, excluding nodular lymphocyte‐predominant Hodgkin lymphoma). Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory disease after no more than one prior line of systemic therapy (radiotherapy not counted); measurable disease defined as at least one fluorodeoxyglucose‑PET avid lesion ≥ 1.5 cm in longest diameter; not a candidate for autologous stem‑cell transplantation. Prior treatment required: Documentation of receipt of ≤1 prior systemic anticancer regimen (any chemotherapeutic regimen used in first‑line setting); adequate recovery of blood counts (absolute neutrophils ≥1000/mm³, platelets ≥50 000/mm³, hemoglobin ≥8 g/dL). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Live vaccines administered within 30 days before enrollment.
Grade ≥ 2 peripheral neuropathy.
History of grade ≥ 3 hypersensitivity to brentuximab vedotin or nivolumab.
Active central nervous system involvement by lymphoma.
Other primary malignant neoplasm not in remission for ≥3 years (exceptions: cured non‑melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ of cervix, or tumors unlikely to require treatment for ≥2 years).
Systemic corticosteroid or other immunosuppressive therapy exceeding 10 mg/day prednisone-equivalent within 14 days of dosing (unless inhaled/topical or adrenal replacement dosage without active autoimmune disease).
Past or current progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy.
Inherited or acquired immunodeficiency syndromes.
Active pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.
Active, clinically significant autoimmune disease (exceptionally limited cases such as vitiligo, psoriasis not needing systemic therapy, lymphoma‑associated hemolysis, well‑controlled type I diabetes, or stable thyroid disorder).
Unresolved chronic hepatitis B or C infection (active replication) or untreated HIV infection (patients with controlled HIV permissible per protocol specifications).
Recent major cardiovascular events: stroke/TIA, unstable angina, myocardial infarction, or congestive heart failure/New York Heart Association Class III‑IV within six months.
Pregnancy or lactation.
Inability to comply with study procedures or follow‑up assessments.

Known hypersensitivity to investigational agents or components thereof.
Concurrent participation in another therapeutic interventional trial (except certain supportive care studies).
Inability to understand or sign informed consent documents.
Performance status lower than required threshold (Karnofsky < 70% for adults, Lansky/Karpski < 70% for pediatrics).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Locally advanced or metastatic disease, unresectable, measurable disease according to RECIST version 1.1 or bone‐only disease. Prior treatment required: No prior systemic anticancer therapy for advanced/metastatic disease. Prior treatment excluded: Prior (neo)adjuvant endocrine therapy with recurrence occurring during or within 12 months after the last dose of endocrine therapy; Prior (neo)adjuvant cyclin‑dependent kinase 4/6 inhibitor with recurrence occurring during or within 12 months after the last dose of CDK4/6 inhibitor. Biomarkers required: Estrogen receptor–positive or progesterone receptor–positive tumor and Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2–negative tumor. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of pneumonitis.
Heart failure or significant cardiac disease.
Renal insufficiency or severe renal impairment.
Hepatic dysfunction or clinically significant liver disease.
Uncontrolled central nervous system metastases.
Active HIV infection or chronic viral hepatitis B/C.
Performance status worse than ECOG 2.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: cutaneous melanoma. Histology allowed: cutaneous melanoma (all histological variants except blue nevus subtype, mucosal, acral lentiginous, and ocular/choroidal melanoma). Cancer burden allowed: unresectable and/or metastatic disease with measurable lesion(s). Prior treatment required: documented disease progression after treatment with an anti‑programmed death 1 monoclonal antibody (administered as monotherapy or in combination). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: detection of two functional copies of the G protein‐coupled estrogen receptor (GPER) protein‑coding sequence. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Allogeneic solid organ or tissue transplant.
Uncontrolled or active autoimmune disease or significant immunodeficiency disorder.
Concurrent medical conditions that would interfere with safe participation or compliance with study procedures.
History of severe adverse reaction to anti‑PD‑1 therapy rendering further administration of pembrolizumab unsafe.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: greater than 18 years. Sex allowed: male and female. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: any breast cancer histology. Cancer burden allowed: advanced or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: no more than 1 prior hormonal regimen for advanced/metastatic disease and no more than 1 prior chemotherapy (including antibody–drug conjugates) for locally advanced or metastatic disease. Prior treatment excluded: prior palazestrant (OP‑1250). Biomarkers required: estrogen receptor positive **and** HER2 negative (determined on most recent archival metastatic tumour specimen). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of a prior or concomitant malignant neoplasm likely to interfere with safety or efficacy assessments. Clinically significant, untreated cardiovascular disease or cardiac repolarisation abnormalities. Cerebrovascular accident or transient ischemic attack occurring within six months before enrollment. Pulmonary embolism or deep vein thrombosis within six months preceding enrolment, or deemed at heightened thrombotic risk. Previous episode of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease. Presence of leptomeningeal disease or symptomatic spinal cord compression. Gastrointestinal conditions impairing oral medication absorption. Documented human immunodeficiency virus infection. Significant hepatic impairment classified as Child‑Pugh class B or C, including active hepatitis B or C, chronic alcoholic liver disease, or established cirrhosis. Severe hypersensitivity skin reactions such as Stevens‑Johnson syndrome, toxic epidermal necrolysis, erythrema multiforme, or DRESS syndrome. Ongoing acute infection or circumstances placing the participant at high risk for serious infection (e.g., immune deficiency, poorly controlled diabetes, unstable cardiac disease, frailty, malnutrition). Major medical, psychiatric, or social comorbidity compromising protocol compliance or data interpretation.
